routes.name: Dogma
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Second boulder up the arete between the vertical face and downclimb with the big hollow flake/jug. Super fun.
routes.Location: Second boulder

routes.name: Forbidden Fruit
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Second boulder, farthest face. Flat and vertical. Lots of options, all in the V0 or V1 range.
routes.Location: Second boulder

routes.name: Garden of Eden
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Few sit-start options, also a few stand start options. All lead up to the top lip and right. Some harder variations.
routes.Location: On the boulder closest to the road, up the steep side

routes.name: Genesis
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: First boulder. Up the right side of the steep face using a shallow pocket to start. Slap up the arete.

routes.name: Original Sin
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: On the second boulder up the steepest part on looker's right from the road. Stand start using a jug up to an arete and high hold
routes.Location: Second boulder

routes.name: Paradise
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Up the arete feature with cool holds
routes.Location: Just left of the steep face on the first boulder

routes.name: Git Yer Comeuppance
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start far right at the bottom of the 45 degree wall matched on the good sloper. Move up the corner to a dyno, then up and left through some cool moves. Top out.
routes.Location: At the far left of the main face.

routes.name: Jason's Traverse
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Follow the obvious line from right-to-left, or left to right.
routes.Location: On the first face you encounter when coming from the main area.

routes.name: The Bandit
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start far right on a side pull and a high foot, work your way up to the prow with a big move to a good sloper. Pull over the top.
routes.Location: On the first problem you come across, known as the Guardian Boulder

routes.name: Bandito
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start far left and follow jugs and edges up to the same good sloper as the bandit. Top out as bandit.
routes.Location: On the Guardian boulder's left side.

routes.name: Captain's Arete
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start with right hand in the crack and left on the arete. Slap up the near vertical arete with your hand and heel until you feel solid and make the move back right to the jug. Jugs to top out.    Much harder the shorter you are.
routes.Location: On the obvious highball arete of the Captain Boulder

routes.name: Captains Crack
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start with all hands and feet in the crack and work your way up the crack using a few good side pulls to help. Top out as captains arete.
routes.Location: On the right face of the Captain Boulder

routes.name: Captains Slab
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on a bad slopper down low. Work your way up the near vertical face on small holds. Load's of potential for cheese grating.
routes.Location: On the left side of the Captain Boulder

routes.name: Don't Blow It
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start down in the pit in between a small boulder and the wall. Start with both feet on a ledge, right hand on the arete and left hand on the small side pull sloped edge. Work you hands onto the arete and continue up to a big committing move to a great hold left. Top out staying to the right side of the boulder.
routes.Location: As you come around to the captain boulder it is downhill on your left in a pit.

routes.name: Innie
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the horn same as Outie and work your hand into the crack and too out using the crack only.
routes.Location: Uphill from the left end of the faces.

routes.name: Outie
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: SDS on the rail on the left of the crack. Follow the feature up and right to a big throw to a sharp edge to top out.
routes.Location: On Belly Button Boulder.

routes.name: Outie Left
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: SDS as Outie. Traverse up the feature past the crack but head straight up to the lip instead of continuing the traverse. Mantle the top out.
routes.Location: Belly Button Boulder

routes.name: Project
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the same undercling as Slouch. Move left up to a large ledge. Mantle up to a nice lay-back to the top.
routes.Location: Left of Slouch

routes.name: Slouch
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sloper+Ouch=Slouch. Start on the obvious undercling and throw for the dagger covered sloper up and right. It hurts. Continue slapping up painful slopers to the top. A rare great landing.
routes.Location: Straight left from the Guardian Boulder on the downhill side of the Steeple Boulder.

routes.name: Steeple Slab
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the slab climbers left of Steeple Squeeze follow some flakes to a committing move up high.
routes.Location: Left slab face of Steeple Squeeze

routes.name: Steeple Squeeze
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: An amazing problem on amazing rock over an amazingly bad landing. Start at the very bottom point of the rock smearing on the slab left with feet. Climb onto the right slap with odd guppy grips until you are fully on the slab right. Work your hands up the arete until you find the good right hand edge out right. Slow your pulse and make some committing moves up high to top out.
routes.Location: On the obvious "Steeple" on the Steeple Boulder, opposite the Captain's Arete
routes.Protection: Lots of Pads/Spotters bring this from an "x" to "PG13".  Need to toss a rope for the down climb.

routes.name: Sumo Wrestler
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Wes thinks it should be a 4 star. Start as far left on the sloppy lip as possible. Work your way right to the good centre sloper and top out.

routes.name: Classic Flake
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Super-classic granite layback flake. The flake fizzles out up high and leads to a scary slab climb the is easier than it looks.
routes.Location: Left of Swig Swag

routes.name: Lightning Crack
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Up right from Yet to be Sober. Obvious Crack.

routes.name: Swig Swag
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Very fun V1! Work your way up the huge blocks to the main top-out ledge. Hardest moves are up top. Spotter necessary.
routes.Location: Left of 'Yet to be High'

routes.name: Yet to be High
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Same as yet to be sober but instead of the lower flake, traverse across the small seam of tiny crimps. meets yet to be sober at the good jug.

routes.name: Yet to be Sober
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start far left on the upwards facing flake. Traverse right and work your way onto the large slopey corner holds. One big move up to the sloped dish and a committing move on a small side-pull crimp will get to you to better holds up top. Top out on the ledge then rappel or climb the next section of easy slab and walk down.

routes.name: The Agony of De-feet
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb easy ground up to the first bolt, following the bolts through progressively harder slab climbing. Finish up at a large tree on the right, or continue up on gear. May want some small pieces to protect the slab, or prepare for runout sport.

routes.name: Aria's Orange Slushy
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb up the sloping corner onto the slabby face at 5.6ish climbing, and follow the orange "slushy" stain on the rock.
routes.Location: First route left of the Chimney.
routes.Protection: bolted; anchor and rap rings at top

routes.name: Dude, I Think She is the King
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the slab past 2 small cam placements to a line of bolts.  Go right at the last bolt to a pin in the corner and then back left to a 1" crack which leads to a bolt anchor above the leaning cedar.
routes.Location: Start 10m right of Rob's Route.

routes.name: Father and Son
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the bolted S-shaped line up the sustained wall to the Metolius rap hangers for the first pitch of Perspective. This was the first sport climb at Eagle's Nest and was hand drilled while the first ascensionist's father belayed patiently over 2 days (bolts took 30 min. each!)
routes.Location: 10 feet right of Perspective at an aspen tree.

routes.name: Father's Day Slab
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: P1: At the large dirty ledge move left past a bolt into a treed gully to gain a slab. Climb the right line of bolts up to anchors. (25m) P2: Continue up past bolts to the left side of the roof to a large ledge sharing the anchor for Lichen It.
routes.Location: Same start as for Lichen It.

routes.name: Finger Stinger
routes.FA:: FA David Smart, FFA Peter Reilly
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the first pitch of 5.8 Stairway to Heaven. At the anchors, instead of going up and right (2nd pitch of Stairway to Heaven) go left towards the slight overhang that follows into the thin crack to the top. It is a thin diagonal traverse with good feet leading to the roof. Lay back onto the small ledge on the left side of the small roof, then smear and lay back the thin seam to progress higher into the crack. The uneven crackling has some good handjams in some spots, and some very thin laybacking in others. Fantastic Route to a 2 bolt anchor. Can 5.4 scramble for another 15 meters (50 feet) to the top (look for tat with carabiners on 2x trees for anchoring/repelling).
routes.Location: Must climb the first pitch of 5.8 Stairway to Heaven to approach 5.11b Finger Stinger.
routes.Protection: Small cams in multiples up to .75. Can use a #2 under roof, but needs extension, or plug smaller gear along the continuous vertical crack instead (just back it up). 2 bolt anchor at the top.

routes.name: Johnny Be Good
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start up a short gently overhung corner, up the face past a cedar towards the right side of a large undercling flake. Traverse leftwards along the flake on good underclings and jams towards the obvious handcrack in the roof. Step into the roof and fire through. Easy terrain leads to a rap anchor behind the large tree.
routes.Location: head left along the cliff, downhill from the offwidth of "Perspective". Find the corner crack in a lowpoint.
routes.Protection: Gear to 3", save a camalot #2 to pull the roof.

routes.name: Migizi Wazoson
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Route description by FAist:Pitch one (The Rocks) 5.9, 50ft: Dance up the technical cracks then up the left traverse to the arête beside the steep head wall. Stick-clipping the first bolt is recommended as the crux is low. 8 bolts. You can alternatively climb Tilting Planet and continue to the next pitch.Pitch two (The Waters) 5.9, 88 ft: Crack your way up a deceivingly easy looking slab to a mid slab crux that takes a moment to figure out but feels good when you do. Follow the bolts to some fun moves into a traverse with lots of great holds all the way to the anchor for a relaxing finish. 9 bolts.Pitch three (The Forests) 5.4, 40ft: Casual pitch that heads up a fun and easy little crack to Snake Ledge. Easy but mossy walk onto the ledge. 4 bolts.Pitch four (The Skies) 5.6, 50ft: One of the best pitches on the route that follows more slab on great holds and great feet with an even more spectacular view. Wonderful way to finish it off. 6 bolts.Note: You can combine pitches 3 and 4 easily.Finish the route off by rapping back down from the top of pitch-four to the top of pitch-two. From here rap down through some trees that part to allow passage down to the top of pitch one. Rap to the ground from here.Or hike left and down the steep gully with a fixed rope.
routes.Location: Located left of central of all the bolted routes on the wall, roughly at the right end of the main parking lot.

routes.name: Perspective
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: P1: Take the low angle offwidth crack up past some ledges to a large belay ledge with a geocache.   P2: Go around the right side of the large flake, and climb the crack to the top.
routes.Location: First route in from the approach trail next to the auto parts store. Obvious large crack on the corner.
routes.Protection: Standard rack up to 4", a newer leader might want a 5" piece for some of the crux offwidth moves.

routes.name: The Swiftly Tilting Planet
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Follow the bolted line up a steep thin slab to rappel anchors. The hardest parts should be the start and at the 4th bolt.
routes.Location: 15 feet left of Perspective.

routes.name: Whose Line Is It Anyway?
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Bolted crack
routes.Location: Second route from the left of Dirty Harry.

routes.name: The B Bent Over
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on Screaming Blue, go left on bolts straight up.

routes.name: Bright Green Guru
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Fingercrack, Climb the flake to the fingercrack. Move right at its end to another crack.

routes.name: I Did it in Heels
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Hand crack to offwidth before anchors.

routes.name: John Be Careful
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Slopey pockets
routes.Location: 3rd route from right
routes.Protection: Bolts  Shares anchors with Screaming Blue.

routes.name: Kim is a Hag
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Low crux, hidden pocket.
routes.Location: 2nd route, next to Tiny
routes.Protection: Bolts  Finish on Tiny Kingdom anchors

routes.name: Manual Labour
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Pumpy flaky layback, with a stout crux.

routes.name: Martyr's Shrine
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Very thin crack beside Manual Labour.

routes.name: Motley Crue
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Last sport route on the wall. Very slopey.

routes.name: Red Amanda
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Difficult start to more of the same.

routes.name: Screaming Blue Rancheros
routes.FA:: Judy Barnes, Chris Oates, Tom Valis
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the left bolt line to anchors.

routes.name: Short Sharp Shock
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Barely exists, just right of Motley Crue.
routes.Protection: Had 4 bolts (sleeves still visible)  No anchors.

routes.name: Swindle! It's A Swindle!
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: 3m left of Under the Volcano.

routes.name: The Tiny Kingdom of Kanga Tarsis
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Short Crack on the right side of Berlin, first route when entering the crevice.
routes.Location: first route, right most climb.

routes.name: To Kill a Mockingbird
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Crank hard to anchors.

routes.name: Under the Volcano
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Corner start, go left to good holds, work the crux.  Run out to 3rd bolt.

routes.name: Welcome to Berlin
routes.FA:: John Wier, Mike Nadeau, Gerry Banning (1988)
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Originally a one-bolt trad climb, the addition of new bolts and anchors have made this a significantly better route.

routes.name: Unknown
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Starts off with a couple of pockets to the left of the crack, and provides a satisfying crimp before moving into some bigger holds. The start can be graded up if you choose to stay to the right of the anchors and tackle the excellent crack.
routes.Location: About 600 yards in you will come to a small canyon, where a massive boulder has separated from the main cliff.   This route is set to the very left side of the small canyon.
routes.Protection: Only 3 draws needed, the first is set a little high and can be set by rapelling, or by climbing on the left side to reach it. It is best to top out or you can use the two bolts set at the top for a rapell.

routes.name: Unknown 2
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Ideally you start on the crack in the middle, with some big gaps and nasty little crimps.  Then a little higher you have more crack to work with, but with little around it, and next to nothing for the feet.  For an easier start, you can start on the barn door to the right, and then finish in the middle.
routes.Location: This is in the middle of the bend crack.  The top is easily identified by the two bolts, and there is a trail to the bottom that is easy to find.
routes.Protection: 2 Bolts at the top or trees can be used to anchor for the top rope.  Some day I hope to bolt this route.

routes.name: Unknown 3
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The Climb starts with a beatiful flake to lay back on, and then gets spotty about half way up. Then you can go left for a significant challenge, or right onto the dihedral for an easy finish.
routes.Location: About 600 yards in you will come to a small canyon, where a massive boulder has separated from the main cliff.   This route is set to the middle - right side of the small canyon.
routes.Protection: 3 Bolts, ok top anchors, but easier to top out. The first clip is ridiculously high, and the second clip is only necessary if you go left.   Note that the top of the barn door sounds hollow, and flexes a little. As such, belaying from away and to the right is recommended.

routes.name: Walk in the Park
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Big holds and several ledges make this walk in the park a satsfying feel good warm up.  The start has a bit of a mantle, and the end has a small outcropping to get over, but otherwise, it is a comfortable dihedral, with lots of options.
routes.Location: In the canyon area of the bend, about 600 yards from the road, tops out at the top of the trail to the bottom.
routes.Protection: Top Rope for now, though you can lead it if you use the unusual bolt pattern of the climb next to it.

routes.name: Big Apple
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the jug on the left and move right using shallow pockets until you can go up to two jubs - mossy

routes.name: Big Rock
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start in the cave on the undercling and move out right on pockets to a jug into the slab

routes.name: Break Up the Drive
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Up the left side of the steep face, start on an undercling - big reaches

routes.name: Erratica
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on Break Up the Drive and move right up the vertical face using jugs. A direct start called Chair Force One V0 climbs into it from the right of the start of Drive.

routes.name: Finkle
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start in the cave and to straight up the slab

routes.name: Sharp Cheddar
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Up the left side of the steep face - mossy and high top-out

routes.name: Soft Cheddar
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on Sharp Cheddar but move left on the slab - mossy

routes.FA:: Ted Schotten, Brian Cook, Willi Apflauer
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Nice climb with a burly 5.8 finish. Or step down and left for the 5.7 ramp (not recommended).  Pitch 1 20m - Follow the ramp near the bottom of the easy way down around a bulge to a belay ledge.  Pitch 2 15m - Climb up then step right onto a slab with a crack. Follow this to the overhang above.  Pitch 3 10m - Pull the overhang (recommended) or step down and left and follow the ramp to the top (not recommended). Also possible to exit right (also not recommended).
routes.Location: Takes the obvious ramp near the bottom of the easy way down gully.

routes.name: Back of the Pinnacle
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Description from the Bon Echo Guide BookFrequently wet and slippery – and consequently more difficult. Start: on the south side of the Birthday Ridge gully, by a spring 15m from the lake. The pinnacle is a huge landmark. 1. 35m Go up the inside corner and across right to a ledge beside a huge cedar. Cross the tree and continue up the corner to a large chimney with a chockstone. Climb up the chimney to a good stance with a bolt. 2. 25m Climb two small corners. Walk 15m to a tree. 3. 25m Climb up the inside corner to the pinnacle. Alternatively, climb a short, steep crack in the side of the pinnacle (5.9). Step across to the main wall and then go up and slightly right to the edge of a slab. Climb the slab to the top. There’s a two bolt belay station here.-----------While the Guide Book describes the route as slippery and wet, I did not find that to be any more true of this route than any other corner / chimney. The first 15 feet may be more prone to moisture, being near a spring under tree cover.Some bolts are on the route near trees. Please use the bolt and do not sling the trees, which are old and fragile.
routes.Location: On the south side of the Birthday Ridge gully, by a spring 15m from the lake. The pinnacle is a huge landmark, and this route is the chimney side opposite of The Front of the Pinnacle. You can access the gully from the climber's cliff-top trail by rappelling the Saucer Lucy anchor.
routes.Protection: Full range of cams and nuts, and many slings to help with drag. Options to protect are plenty after the first 15 feet or so. Some larger cams (I used all three of the metolius supercams) will help with the chockstone chimney. A long sling (480cm) will help to sling the pinnacle for an anchor, but it is definitely not necessary.

routes.name: Birthday Ridge
routes.FA:: 1956 David Fisher, Marnie Gilmour, Alan Bruce-Robertson,  Kay McCormick
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This easy climb follows a very easy ridge. Its moderate grade make it a great climb to show a non climber what the fuss is all about.
routes.Location: Follows a prominent ridge in a huge corner using the picture in the online guidebook it is impossible to miss. Carry double ropes of the rappel.
routes.Protection: Most belays are bolted. A single rack of cams and nuts plus double ropes for the rappel. Anchors are bolted.

routes.name: Boris' Route
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Perhaps the best 5.5 at Bon Echo.  Pitch 1 20m - ascent the trace of a ramp to a wall featuring black cokey rock and continue up the wall and ramp to an obvious belay ledge.  Pitch 2 25m - continue up the ramp to a small sloping ledge with two bolts.  Pitch 3 25m - move up and left to a large ledge below an overhang. Pull the overhang and belay at two bolts.
routes.Location: 25m north of Birthday Ridge. Belay 2m above the water level.
routes.Protection: Generally good placements with a few pitons and bolts.

routes.name: Compulsion
routes.FA:: History: FA: Helmut Microys, Eric Marshall, Werner Heiss, Sept 1969. FFA (pitches 1, 2 and 3): Dave Lanman, Reg Smart, Adam Gibbs, July 1982. (pitch 4
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Continuously challenging, direct and exposed!Pitch 1 (10a, 25m)Start at the same anchor for Fanny Hill. Climb the east left slanting ramp until a small cedar. Climb the face, then the dihedral through the overhang to a good ledge with a large cedar tree. Belay on 2 bolts. Slightly runout.Pitch 2 (10b, 23m)Climb directly up the face to a bolt. Traverse up and right to a large flake, then continue traversing to a vague corner with a piton and bolt. Continue upwards until under a right arching roof. Exit the roof on the left side to two belay bolts. Many prefer to link with the next pitch.Pitch 3 (5.9, 23m)Continue up from the bolts to a small overlap, then up on good holds with little gear to a notch in the right side of the roof. Easier but dirty climbing above traverses up and right to a 2 bolt anchor. Pitch 4 (10d, 20m)Up the wonderful right slanting crack to the top of the cliff.An easy escape up a ramp exists to the left.
routes.Location: Left of Fanny Hill

routes.name: Conclusion
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Pitch 1: Start from a low ledge, traverses low underneath a slight overhang then up the ramp moving up and left until you move left and climb up a short headwall over a mantleshelf to the first belay. About 90 ft.  Pitch 2: Follow diagonally up and left and then move left underneath the overhang then up the  corner over some biggish blocks to the trees.
routes.Location: The climb starts about 25m south of  Yellow Groove and follows the next diagonal line of weakness.

routes.name: Cool Corner
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Pitch 1 - 25m - Climb up and diagonally right past a small cedar tree then follow the crack to a belay on the right side of the Birthday Ridge slab.  Pitch 2 - 40m - Climb the corner to where you can escape onto the slab above. Climb up until it merges with Birthday Ridge at the top.
routes.Location: Start as for Birthday Ridge.

routes.name: Double Crux Bypass
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Pitch 1 (5.8): Climb up flakes to a small ledge under a steep corner split by thin horizontal cracks. Climb through the corner to slabby easier ground, which leads to a large ledge with two belay bolts. Quite serious due to poor protection. An alternate and much easier start climbs to the same ledge via the far right.Pitch 2 (5.7): Climb up and left for a long ways until it is possible to climb steeply up over blocky ground to a large belay ledge. Gear belay.Pitch 3 (5.9): Traverse to the right end of the ledge, then climb a blocky overhang to a notch/small ledge out right. Above this a small ledge traverses under a overhang to the left, which is split by an obvious but short handcrack. Climb the handcrack (crux) to top.
routes.Location: 20m to the right of Vertiginous

routes.name: The Entertainer
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Step off the boat and onto a small ledge.  You can build a belay right here, though it's probably easier to scramble up for 5 meters to a larger ledge with more opportunities for gear.P1 (5.7?) From the large ledge, traverse up and right on mediocre rock (i.e., beware of loose blocks) with well spaced gear.  After 10 meters, you will find yourself at a crack below the left side of the pillar.  We climbed straight up from here for approximately 15 meters, passing a blank spot before reaching the base of the pillar, where the climbing eases up.  Build a gear anchor and belay atop the pillar.P2 (5.9) Up and left from the belay is a finger crack that continues for 2-3 body lengths.  Have your nuts and small cams ready and fire away!  Once the crack is below you, start traversing left and slightly up, passing a piton or two and an awkwardly placed bolt.  You are aiming for a small tree in a left angling overhang.  Above the tree is a small ledge with a nest of old rusty pitons...and two brand new 1/2" bolts.  Belay here.P3 (5.?) Climb up then left, or left then up, or diagonally past a bulge 2 meters above the belay.  Continue up varied climbing for 10 meters to gain a large ledge.  From here I'm not sure where the route is supposed to go; I'll describe how we finished.  Scramble left over a large tree, then up until just below a small overhang.  Pulling the roof and you're done.  There are a few large bolts on the ground 7 meters from the cliff edge for a belay.
routes.Location: Starts approximately 100 meters right of a small talus slope that marks a break in the cliff.  The route stays to the left of a large overhang.  There is a large pillar 1/3 of the way up the cliff that looks like it will topple off any day now.  The first pitch ends atop this pillar.

routes.name: Fanny Hill
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Pitch 1 35m - Move left and up onto large blocks then up to a large ledge. Traverse right and climb up the crack to a large stance.  Pitch 2 18m - Climb the steep wall on the left, moving up and right over a series of platforms to the obvious ramp.  Pitch 3 30m - Climb the easy ramp and exit via the overhang or through the shrubs.
routes.Location: 33m right of Compulsion and 45m left of Sweet Dreams.
routes.Protection: Generally good gear placements. Watch for loose rock.

routes.name: Formication
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the left side of the slab to the a two bolt belay at the top. 30m   Alternatively, climb the centre of the slab past a single bolt. 5.8
routes.Protection: Rather sparse gear, especially when climbing the direct variation.

routes.name: French Form
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the slabby ramp via the corner to a set of bolts midway. Continue up the ramp/corner to a ledge with a large cedar and anchor bolts on the right. (Easily combined with pitch 1). Move the belay a few meters back to a steep wall (bolts). Follow a lightning bolt crack to a small roof, and either step right and around at 5.4 or continue straight up (better) at 5.6.
routes.Location: 50m left of boris's

routes.name: Front of the Pinnacle
routes.FA:: 1956 David Fisher, Marnie Gilmour, Alan Bruce-Robertson,  Kay McCormick
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The crux of this route is a spooky step from the pinnacle. This is what also makes this route so good. Exhilarating exposure on the 2nd pitch will have you laughing the whole way.
routes.Location: Starts on a large ramp and follows it to a pinnacle where you belay for the infamous Pinnacle pitch. Great route!! A must do.
routes.Protection: Long slings. Small rack of cams and nuts

routes.name: Garden Wall
routes.FA:: Bob Adams, David Atherton, Quentin Shepley
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rarely climbed.  Pitch 1 35m - Climb the ramp to a small belay stance.  Pitch 2 27m - Follow the ramp to the top.  Pitch 3 35m - Continue upwards through easy terrain and shrubbery.
routes.Location: Second ramp from the north end of the cliff.

routes.name: Harmful Armful
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Straightforward until the top of pitch 3. This was an all nut ascent: one nut leading, one nutfollowing and no pitons in between!Start: as for Faithful Turkey.1. 25m Move up right for a short way, then go straight up. Traverse back left below a band of black rock. Climb up a steep wall into a left-sloping groove. Climb it to the top of a pinnacle. (Alternatively, reach this point by traversing diagonally from Boris’s Route.) Continue up left for about 10m to a sloping ledge.2. 15m Go straight up, past a small tree, to a cedar tree belay.3. 30m Traverse left past a smooth vertical wall. Turn the corner and climb right into a left facing overhanging open book. Ascend this almost to the top. Hand traverse the slightlyoverhanging wall left on good holds for 2m to a small ledge. Climb right, passing an easyoverhang onto ledges with cedar trees.4. 10m Over easy rock to the top.3(a). 5.10 Instead of traversing left at the top of the open book, go onto the right wall and climb a deep overhanging crack to a ledge. Belay. Then up and right to join Birthday Ridge at the top.History: FA: Helmut Microys, Mark Hillhouse, Sept 1977.FA pitch 3(a): Tom Gibson, Steve Labelle, Sept 1980.

routes.name: Inferno
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This climb is somewhat atypical for Bon Echo because there's no routefinding skills required.  Simply locate the clean cut dihedral and climb it!P1: (5.9+) Scramble up easy terrain and clip a bolt before pulling over a roof.  Another 10 meters of moderate, but slightly runout climbing puts you on a ledge below the obvious corner.  There is no pro for a ways leading up to this ledge, so make sure your gear here is solid.  Fire the obvious crux using a less than obvious sequence to gain good holds in the crack above you.  After finishing the corner, continue scrambling up nice cracks and blocks on easier terrain for 10 meters and belay at a stance atop a block.P2: (4th) Downclimb off the other side of the block and continue up and left to the top of the cliff.
routes.Location: To find the start, walk left for 20-30 m from Knob Hill.  The route ascends a clean dihedral, which is directly below a blank face.  A bolt 5 meters off the ground marks the start.To descend, take the "easy way down."  In other words, follow small trails climbers left from the top of the route, always following the leftmost path.  The trails are faint at times, but as you pass a bluff and start to lose elevation, the trail will become well defined.  At one point, you will scramble down a steep slab for 10 meters.  A short jaunt will bring you back to your packs.
routes.Protection: Standard rack including small nuts and a black alien (or equivalent) for the crux and a #4 camalot for the belay.

routes.name: Jerry's Route
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rarely climbed.  Pitch 1 40m - Climb through the forest until you get to the ramp.  Pitch 2 40m - Climb the ramp.  Pitch 3 10m - Continue up the ramp to the top.
routes.Location: First right leaning ramp from the north end.

routes.name: The Joke
routes.FA:: John Turner, Dick Strachan in 1961 / Last pitch: Helmut Microys, Urs Kallen in 1966
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The Joke 5.9 (5.10)A superb route, popular in the 70s and 80s after achieving notoriety in Chris Jones' book.Although rarely climbed in recent years, the ageing fixed protection (one bolt and many pitons)was replaced in 2007 and much of the poison ivy was removed. The third pitch remainsdifficult to protect and hence remains serious. The use of two ropes is recommended - anddon’t fall!Start: At a 3m high pinnacle below a small tree, directly below a steep prominent ramp whichstops about 23m above the lake. 60m right of Ottawa Route.1. 5.9 (5.10) 40m Climb straight up for 3m to a tree (clip the bolt, not the tree), then traverse left 2mto a grassy ledge. Climb upwards and rightwards to an overhang, almost level with the bottomof the big ramp. Step left past a loose block, and up into a sentry box. Continue left to the baseof the ramp. Move left and up the ramp (protect with very small pieces) to a bolted belay on asmall ledge on the ramp’s outer edge.2. 5.6 30m Follow the outside edge of the ramp. Belay at two bolts on top of some suspectrocks at the edge of the ramp. Beware of poison ivy on this pitch!3. 5.9 35m Climb the inside edge of the ramp for about 6m until it is possible to traverse to theoutside edge of the ramp. Climb up until the inside corner is reached again just above theorange lichen. Follow the corner to the end of the ramp, then up a short vertical wall to a largeledge (two belay bolts).4. 5.7 15m From the middle of the ledge, follow a left-leaning crack to the base of a right-leaning ramp. Follow the ramp for 3m to an obvious exit up left to the top.History: FA: John Turner, Dick Strachan, 1961 (after several attempts and falls). The Jokewas first repeated in June 1966, by Helmut Microys and Urs Kallen, using pitch 4. ForTurner's last pitch, see below.34a The Last Laugh 5.10+ RThe original last pitch of The Joke. During 2007, when Kit Moore and Ray Rutitis werepreparing to replace old fixed protection on The Joke, Moore contacted John Turner to discussthe planned work. During those discussions, Turner asked why pitch 4 (on which he had his spectacular fall in 1960) was rated only 5.7 when he remembered it as being much harder. Heexplained that he had moved up and off the ramp earlier - at a large cedar tree above theorange lichen. After some exploration, Moore and Rutitis found Turner's original last pitch,complete with several old pitons. When Turner examined the photographs, he confirmed thatthis line was indeed the pitch on which he had fallen, and asked that this be named “The LastLaugh”. Several old pitons were replaced with a single bolt in 2008, and bolts were also placedby trees.
routes.Location: Route #34 in the Bon Echo Guidebook (between #33 Club Disciplinarian & #35 Punchline). Starts on a small pinnacle (gear anchor) off the water below a small tree.
routes.Protection: Bolted anchors on Pitch 1, 2, & 3. A few hangers spread throughout route.

routes.name: Knob Hill
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start at a clearing in the trees, about 5m north of the narrows, beneath an obvious tree about 20m up.  Pitch 1 - 20m - Climb up the steep face on good holds to a tree.  Pitch 2 - 25m - Move left and up to the huge ledge.  Pitch 3 - 20m - Traverse right (crux) and continue straight up on good holds to the top.

routes.name: Mac's Book
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the open book and slab to the top.
routes.Location: Climb 10m up from the top of Formication and then cross right to the obvious open book.

routes.name: Mazinaw Monster
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Mazinaw Monster: Cool rock features and great gear. Lots of lichen and a huge loose rock on a ledge near the top of the first pitch. Not in Bon Echo park.P1 - Up crack and short falls to quartzite ledge. Step left above loose boulder and into splitter face crack. Belay on ledge off gear (5.9 20m).P2 - Up short crack (5.5 10m).Descent: Hike to steep gully and down.Gear: Single rack .5 to 3. 60m rope.Named after the fabled Mazinaw Lake Monster - kind of like the local Loch Ness.
routes.Location: About 200m south of dock for hiking trail.

routes.name: Mooning Boris
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Links the first pitch of Boris' Route with the third pitch of Moonday. Pitch 2 involves a traverse left across a grassy ledge and scramble to a belay ledge. Lichen the lichen.
routes.Location: Start as for Boris' Route.

routes.name: Old Faithful
routes.FA:: FA: Michael Rosenberger, Helmut Microys, Aug. 1974. FFA: Steve Adcock, Ron Halka, Aug 22nd 1982.
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start partway up the gully to the left of Rose Tattoo.Climb up to a ledge, then up through a quartz band into a corner beneath the large overhang. Step around the corner onto a blank face, passing a bolt. Climb up the face to a ledge, then angle rightwards to join a dihedral near the top of the cliff with a bolt. Climb the dihedral to the top.
routes.Location: Up the gully from Rose Tattoo.

routes.name: One Pine
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A splendid route for beginners.  Pitch 1 - 40m - Belay from the ledge 10m up, climb near or on the outside edge, move up to a wide ledge with a good belay.  Pitch 2 - 28m - Go more or less straight up.  Pitch 3 - 40m - Climb up and move right to an obvious crack below the "pine" tree. Climb the crack and scramble to the bolted belay above.
routes.Location: 120m north of the easy way down, at the bottom of a major ramp.
routes.Protection: Generally good gear placements with a few pitons.

routes.name: Paradise Lost
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Pitch 1 20m - Climb up behind the flake then move left and ascent past a bolt and slight right to a large pine tree.  Pitch 2 30m - Climb the ramp to the slabs and continue up the slabs to a belay stance.  Pitch 3 20m - Climb the slab to the top.
routes.Location: 10m left of the easy way down.
routes.Protection: Generally good gear. Large cams helpful. Can combine pitches 1 and 2.

routes.name: Persuasion
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A traverse connecting Top Secret and Ottawa Route.
routes.Location: Begins at the first belay station of Top Secret.

routes.name: Red Groove
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: An impressive route for beginners.  Get out of the boat and onto the belay ledge (crux). Climb the corner and chimney until forced onto the face by a constriction. Continue to the top.
routes.Location: 30m north of the easy way down.

routes.name: The Romp of the Geriatrics
routes.FA:: FA: Jim Mark, Roger Parsons, Helmut Microys, Sept 1976. FFA: Rob Chisnall, Ian McKay, Oct 11th 1983.
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on a small ledge just above the water in a cave. Same start as Conclusion.Pitch 1, 5.5 20mTraverse leftwards under a roof until it is possible to climb upwards. Climb up through a dihedral, then traverse under a large dead tree. Belay here to reduce rope drag and improve communication for the crux. Take care of the numerous large loose blocks around the anchor.Pitch 2, 5.11d or 5.5 C0, 25mClimb to a small crack that splits the roof. Ponder how anyone could climb this free at 5.11, or easily aid through. Escape upwards and right over mountains of lichen and loose rock to a belay ledge inside a juniper.Pitch 3, Easy 5th 10m.Scramble easily to the top.

routes.name: The Rose Tattoo
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Pitch 1. 15m Climb directly up the wall to a comfortable belay stance or continue to the top.  Pitch 2. 35m Move right past a piton up good holds on a left facing corner system before moving right at the top. A variation clips a bolt on Old Faithful near the top and continues up the corner system. Frequently done in one pitch.
routes.Location: Start between the narrows and Vertigenous where there are very few trees between the wall and the talus slope.

routes.name: Saturday Night and Sunday Morning
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rarely climbed.  Pitch 1 35m - Climb the ramp to an small belay stance.  Pitch 2 20m - Step right and climb up the face above (crux).  Pitch 3 20m - Traverse right and climb up the left side of a pinnacle. Belay on the other side.  Pitch 4 25m - Take any one of many possible finish to the top.
routes.Location: Start as for Garden Wall.
routes.Protection: Generally good gear placements. Lots of lichen.

routes.name: Saucer Lucy
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the dihedral on the right side of the birthday ridge slab. Climb to a rightwards sloping ledge, then back left via a thin crack to the roof and bolt (crux). Pull the roof then continue up the corner to the top.
routes.Location: Rightmost climbing on the birthday ridge slab.
routes.Protection: Small nuts and cams, one bolt

routes.name: The Slab
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The first pitch provides enjoyable climbing on small holds. The remainder of the climb is disappointing so it is common practice to rappel after the first pitch.
routes.Location: 30m right of Sweet Dreams ramp.
routes.Protection: Bolted rappel station at the top of first pitch.

routes.name: Sunday Delight
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the ramp to the top. 30m
routes.Location: 5m right of Afternoon Delight
routes.Protection: Poor gear lower down but improves further up.

routes.name: Sweet Dreams
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A Bon Echo classic, hands down.p1: Scramble up the slab easily to its end, sticking towards the right side. Gear anchor. 5.3p2: Move out left onto the face (look down, it's a fantastically exposed stance) and traverse left on good feet and somewhat crimpy hands. Use the pitons if you want, but at sometimes 50+ years old, they have a reputation for failure. Back them up. Continue to a right-facing corner and move up to a large ledge. Gear anchor (a variety of pitons are available as well). 5.6-5.7p3: The big one. Move up the wall to climber's right on the ledge. Clip the bolt a few metres up, towards the end of the crux. The difficulty tapers off a bit, but is fairly sustained. Follow the weakness first up and slightly left to a tree, then up and right to the top. Belay on gear on a big ledge or continue to the top. 5.9+, realistically 10a/b at the crux.
routes.Location: Sweet Dreams starts on the first pronounced slab ramp from the North end of the cliff.
routes.Protection: Standard rack to #2 Camalot. Full set of nuts.

routes.name: Thunder Crack
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The obvious lightning bolt finger crack. Belay from a boat (piton to anchor in the alcove). Climb the crack to the large ledge with a tree. A 2 bolt anchor can be used to lower back down.
routes.Location: The pinnacle to the right of crazy paving

routes.name: Top Secret
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rock fall has altered this route. It is now 5.7, and the new crux is protected with bolts.Pitch 1 27m - Climb up right, then through the newly exposed rock and move left and up to a small ledge.Pitch 2 18m - Step up and right and move further right onto the ramp. Go leftwards up the ramp.Pitch 3 20m - Scramble up to the top. Try to find the "secret" break in the rock at the top to unlock the best part of the route.
routes.Location: Start at the top of the scree covered buttress 25m above the lack.

routes.name: Vertigenous
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start below a large cedar tree on a ledge a third of the way up the wall.
routes.Protection: Bolted anchor at top of first and third pitch.

routes.name: Winking Crack
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Pitch 1 6m - Follow the obvious left-leaning crack over a bulge to a ledge with a cedar tree, below a leaning crack at the back of an overhang.  Pitch 2 25m - Climb the strenuous finger crack (crux) to a ledge with a cedar tree. Climb the overhang to the top.
routes.Location: Starts near the bottom of the easy way down.

routes.name: Womb At The Top
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Pitch 1 35m - climb up onto a block with a bush. Go up and left to a small tree then up and right to a sickly looking tree. Go up and right into the overhanging cleft known as the "womb". Most parties link the second pitch which scrambles up 10m to a bolted anchor.
routes.Location: 70m north of the narrows. Take the easy way down.

routes.name: Yellow Groove
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start at the first diagonal ramp south of Black Groove.  Rock fall (2011?) obliterated the first belay ledge. Climb the obvious fault, struggle behind the cedar and continue to the top. Watch for loose rock. 45m
routes.Location: Take the easy way down.
routes.Protection: Bolted anchor at the top. Generally good gear placements.

routes.name: Hyena Hairs
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Same start as Off-Leash, but the arete is IN. Work your way up the slab using the arete and top out to finish.

routes.name: Milk Teeth
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start low on the left side of the overhanging face using a sloping rail and a crimp. Make a big move to a very sharp right hand crimp and heel hook a crystal around the arete to throw your left hand to the sloping/pinchy arete. Follow better holds to the right and top out in the centre of the boulder.

routes.name: Off-Leash
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start standing using a left hand undercling and right hand rail. Establish yourself on the slab using small feet and AVOID using the arete to gain small crimpy holds and slopers. Top out to finish.

routes.name: Paw Slap
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: On the right side of the overhanging face, start matched on the better part of the long crimpy rail. Head out right to a better crimp and then throw your left hand up to a better hold. Use some fancy footwork to slap to a left hand sloper and use the arete to work your way up to some better holds to top out.

routes.name: VBB Dog
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the big foothold at the base of the boulder and walk your way up the slab to top out. Arete is OUT.

routes.name: Angel's Crest
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start and climb the right face of the arete
routes.Location: Behind boulder Malamute at the right of Dreamcatcher's route

routes.name: Banana Peel
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Go up the middle of the slab through the small shelf.
routes.Location: On the malamute boulder left of the mid cliff sign. The boulder problem faces the trail.

routes.name: Beautiful Odyssey
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: SDS matched on a good block with choice of feet. Follow the overhang to the right using ledges and increasingly slopey holds, top out on the arete at the apex of the overhang. Be wary of a block that is coming loose near the bottom of the arete as many people used this hold to move through the crux of the problem.   From the SDS move left and up the slab for the easier, but drier "Some Beautiful Things Remain"
routes.Location: Coming down the trail from the Gateway and Nine-Tenths areas this problem is located on the right, just off the path (on the backside of the Malaria boulder).
routes.Protection: Pad if desired, more importantly to keep your feet dry as this boulder is located at the edge of the swamp.

routes.name: Blackfly
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the left side of the slightly overhanging arete with a pinch for the right hand and a small edge for the left. Climb up the arete staying on the left side. Make use of the right facing corner and most likely a very high step for the right foot. While the crux is likely pulling off the high step the topout is slopey so be prepared to mantel it out.
routes.Location: On the Nine-Tenths boulder, on the same face as Bullfighter's Friend. The starting holds for Blackfly are on the right arete while Bullfighter starts to the left.  Downclimb the prominent crack on the trail-facing side of the boulder.
routes.Protection: A pad should be sufficient, a spotter if the topout bothers you.

routes.name: Bottom Up
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start and climb the blunt areteDépart assis et grimper l'arête.
routes.Location: The arete on the right detached bloc of GatewayL'arête sur le petit bloc détaché de Gateway

routes.name: The Bullfighter's Friend
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: SDS with hands matched on the bottom ledge in the series of "steps". Move up the obvious line trending towards the right. Various beta exists but most includes using the left facing corner and making a stretch to the sloping lip. Once established on the lip throw a heel up and mantle over the top.   A similar line "Ambassador of Kick-Yer-Assiter" (FA Denny Wong) follows the stepped edges but eliminates the lip, instead moving through the left facing corner and topping out further right.    Each variation is worth a try, these problems had previously been considered the "benchmark" for v4 in the area, but more recently both have been re-evaluated as V3. Might feel somewhat stiff for a 3 to some, especially if you don't have the reach to simply stick the lip from the blocky steps.
routes.Location: Located on the backside of the Nine-Tenths boulder, the boulder following The Gateway along the trail. Pretty prominent line on a great boulder.
routes.Protection: pad and spotter, the landing is decent but try not to flail off the top.

routes.name: Castaway
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start on a crimp in the small left facing corner and climb up
routes.Location: On the face of West Nile between a small roc close of the pond and the path from Nine-Tenths

routes.name: Cobra Crack
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Go up the offwidth. Many variations and possibilities.
routes.Location: Obvious offwidth on Malamute Boulder. On the left of the Mid Cliff sign.

routes.name: Coordination Tongue
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Vertical wall. Start on two small crimps slightly right of the middle of the wall. Move up and top out.
routes.Location: On the Marsh boulder, located on the side facing the marsh. There is a log platform preventing you from sinking into the marsh. Getting down you go to the other side of the boulder.

routes.name: Cruxifixion
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on a prominent flake with many options for feet. Move straight up through a cluster of holds (including a pinch, crimps, and an undercling) avoiding the good holds further to the right. Aim for the jug directly above this cluster that seems so close, and yet...   The first three ascents of this problem were all done in very different styles, so play around with different beta on the various holds until something clicks for you. Surprisingly all versions feel about V5.   Since the first few ascents one or two footholds have broken/crumbled making this problem more challenging. I've tried two of the three betas I know of and both were somewhat harder, I'm assuming the undercling beta (used for the FA) is similarly changed.
routes.Location: Starts on the prominent flake to the left of Westward Ho! on the West Nile Boulder.  As this problem is on the swamp facing side of the boulder it may be largely inaccessible once the snow melts and the water level rises.
routes.Protection: A pad or two, the landing is somewhat uneven.

routes.name: Dreamcatcher
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the face anywhere between the off-width crack and the left side of the face.
routes.Location: Boulder Malamute close of the cliff juste at the left of the indication "Mid-Cliff"

routes.name: Fear of Porcupines
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: SDS with right hand on a low thumbcatch sidepull and left higher up on a sloping edge with bad smeary feet. Make a couple moves up through sloping edges to grab the lip. Make a somewhat difficult and committing mantel to finish.
routes.Location: Located on the backside of the Phobia boulder, to the left of the "pit". Other problems start on a prominent ledge that runs across the boulder, the starting holds for this problem are to the right of this ledge.
routes.Protection: pad over the jumbled rock landing

routes.name: Featured Press
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the obvious crimp 6ft right of the arete. Pull on with difficulty and climb the slab to the top.A sit start is  possible with a lower crimp a few feet to the right and goes about the same grade.
routes.Location: In the middle of the Nine-Tenths boulder on the slab face.

routes.name: From Capetown to Cairo
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Stand start with a left hand undercling and right hand sidepull on the face (left) side of the arete. Make a move up through a crimp and edges to top out at the top of the arete. The crux seems to be making the first move without your feet popping off the wall. Stay to the left of the arete as the right side is another problem.   One of a few decent (and easier) problems on the Africa Boulder that isn't height dependent.
routes.Location: Start on the far right hand side of the Africa Boulder.

routes.name: Gate Traverse
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: SDS as for Gateway Arete and move up the arete to gain the obvious sloping ledge which traverses right along the length of the boulder. Various options and contrivances exist but for the V1 version continue along this edge until it ends at the right-facing corner. Climb up using edges to an easy mantel.   Another option is to traverse the edge to about midway on the boulder and fire up to the lip linking into Put All Your Fears To Rest. This involves a satisfying dyno move and a slightly more challenging mantel.
routes.Location: On the trail facing side of the Gateway Boulder.

routes.name: The Gate
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: SDS on a left hand pinch and right hand edge. Move directly up through sloping edges, crux seems to be sticking a small slot/crimp. Good movement on a slightly overhanging boulder makes for a nice warmup.
routes.Location: Located on the left hand side of the first boulder encountered (the gateway boulder) if you take the new trail below the cliff and talus.
routes.Protection: pad if you like, the landing is good and the problem not very high

routes.name: Gatekeeper
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: SDS on the large incut ledge under the short roof. Using sloping holds go direct over the lip and up the crack to a top out jug. Careful footwork makes this go fairly easily.
routes.Location: The first boulder encountered when following the main path below the talus and alongside the swamp. Found to the left of the trail just before heading up the slight rise that goes to the Gateway Boulder. The start hold is located under the obvious roof.

routes.name: Gateway Arete
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: SDS on the left side of the Gateway Boulder at the base of the arete. Move up large edges, some slightly sloping, to the lip, top out. Decent climbing but very short.
routes.Location: On the far left of the trail facing side of the Gateway Boulder, the first obvious boulder encountered if following the new trail below the cliff and talus.

routes.name: Gatineau Special
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start low to the left of Ken's Super Slab on a low undercling and power out and right to some slopey crimps before topping out on the slab. Pay attention to your feet on the starting moves because it feels much harder if you don't place them correctly. The undercling move is almost all bicep...
routes.Location: Left and underneath of Ken's Super Slab

routes.name: The Grand Wall
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Go up a shallow corner with angular holds. The climb is easy. The top out may make you think twice.
routes.Location: Papoose Boulder on right hand side of passage to mid cliff.

routes.name: The High Road
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: SDS on the jug flake under the roof as per November. Move up and left until you hit the sloping lip. Where November uses the huge jug at the lip and then tops out through the blocky corner, High Road traverses left along the lip using slopes, crimps, and powerful heels to top out at the far left side of the boulder. While it might be possible to top out at any point, fight through the forearm pump until you reach edges and jugs to top out on the left side.   A great problem that feels less contrived than it sounds, the movement is excellent and the length is somewhat unique for Calabogie. An edge about a foot above the lip has been cleaned/dug out fairly recently which may make the problem easier, so if you want to do the original line just avoid this seam.   This problem traverses along the lip of the boulder, the heavily chalked sloping, diagonal ledge running under the roof is the Low Road V8, which looks excellent but is also harder.
routes.Location: Starts as per November under the roof of the Frigid Cave Boulder, behind and to the left of the Gateway Boulder
routes.Protection: a few pads or a spotter to move one in order to cover the blocks resting under the cruxes.

routes.name: Hispanic Panic
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Stand start on good foot holds and move up the slab using a couple crimps, the obvious ledge, and the smooth arete.
routes.Location: On the back of the Gate boulder, the obvious slab.

routes.name: Horde Juggernaut in the Fog
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Stand start on crimps and/or the blocky arete with choice of slippery feet. Make some moves on perfect (if slightly sharp) crimps for the left hand and slap up the arete with your right. Grap a left facing corner above the lip and top out. Excellent problem which requires some technique.   Eliminating the arete and beginning on crimps a bit further left (right hand is on the crimp used for the left hand when starting Horde Juggernaut...) is Coordination Tongue (V6)    This face catches far more sun than any other at Calabogie, making it an ideal spot on cold days, but the crimps get slick quickly in the summer.
routes.Location: Located on the swamp facing side of the Marsh Boulder. From the clearing in the highball area head through the passage to the right of the Apathy Boulder (the scooped slab with a diagonal crack running across the top). The Marsh Boulder is to your right so walk towards the marsh and turn right onto trampled down reeds to find this sun-baked, orange face.
routes.Protection: pad, watch the somewhat uneven landing as this boulder is about as close to the marsh as you can get.

routes.name: Jenn Queen of the Nile
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb arret on the left of the boulder to peak
routes.Location: Route is before new Squamish and after the great roof in the area marked as "Talus Field" in old PDF guide.

routes.name: The Journey
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Located about 40 yards past and just uphill from the Gateway Boulder, this problem is a great "one move wonder" dyno off of small crimps to the lip. The two obvious crimps look better than they feel and you have to use your feet just right to get enough momentum with your left hand to stick the lip.
routes.Location: 40 yards past and about 15 yards uphill from the Gateway Boulder.

routes.name: Ken's Super Slab
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Stand start by carefully pulling onto the slab. Various beta exists to accomplish this, but common beta (and the style of the FA) uses a high right foot at the lip, high right hand on a sharp sidepull that can also be crimped to some degree, and low left hand against the slab to create some tension.   Once your left foot is established, follow the left-trending feature that can be used as a sidepull/undercling for the left hand and gain thin crimps for the right. Technical footwork will bring you to the top of a great slab.
routes.Location: Located on the far right of the Nine-tenths boulder on the opposite side of Bullfighter's Friend. If you are following the trail from The Gateway the slab will be almost facing you as you approach Nine-Tenths.

routes.name: Last Meal
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Stand start on thin but obvious crimps on the right side of the highball boulder. Follow the line of crimps direct, making a series of long movements to gain rounded holds, finishing with a somewhat highball mantel. Creative use of a prominent crimp flake, and more importantly technical footwork will bring success!   Treat the prominent crimp flake (gained with the first move after pulling on) with care, it flexes noticeably on its underside.   I thought this was an excellent technical crimp line, only two stars because of the flexing crimp.
routes.Location: Located on the right hand side of the Highball boulder, with the starting crimps a few feet right of Waiting in Line for the Electric Chair.  As with the other problems on this boulder the downclimb is a bit tricky.
routes.Protection: pad(s) and a spotter  This problem is not as high as Electric Chair but is fairly sustained near the top and has an angled landing.  The spotter will especially come in handy if the flexing hold decides to snap off.

routes.name: Lost at Sea
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: SDS (carefully) on a finger-jug under the short roof. Using heel and toe trickery to keep body tension move low and right through a series of decent edges. Once established on the highest good edge either move up and slightly right to an interesting crimp/pinch hold before locking off to the lip, or just dyno it out.  Really nice movement on this one and the presence of tepid bog-water under the beginning moves adds a bit of spice to this otherwise ass-dragging problem.   If you would rather not risk a soaker, start standing on the good edge and finish with the pinch move or the dyno. This is "Rescue" and goes at about V1.
routes.Location: On the swamp facing side of the West Nile Boulder. Head around the boulder until you find a V shaped indent in the boulder that forms a small overhang on either side. The starting hold for Lost at Sea can be found under the right hand side overhang.   This problem is only really accessible in the early spring before the snow and ice melts, raising the water level of the swamp.
routes.Protection: Pad and maybe a tarp depending on the water level.

routes.name: Malaria
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start low under a small cave with right hand at the lip (low start is V5). Once you hit the rail you have to match and move out left for the pumpy and awkward lip traverse for which there is multiple beta. Some people try to heel hook and work their way out the lip before the awkward mantle. I campused through this section as my heel felt insecure but either way its hard.   We need some pics of this....
routes.Location: Near the pond, obvious roof problem starting low.
routes.Protection: Make sure you get a pad low to protect your back from the sharp slab on the opening move.

routes.name: Malaria Low
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Low start to Malaria.   Start low under a small cave below the high start to Malaria. Get your left heel on or just flag it and use a far right foot well underneath to do the lock off move to the lip. Now finish Malaria.   First move is a bit of a backscratcher with a rock close behind your back. Be careful.
routes.Protection: Couple pads - one down low because there is a rock behind you that wants to gouge your back.

routes.name: Manteling 101
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start in the short left facing corner. Climb up to the lip and the easy mantle.Départ assis dans le coin. Grimper sur la bordure et rétablir sur le haut du bloc.
routes.Location: Dans le petit coin sur la droite du bloc Gateway

routes.name: Manteling 201
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start 5 feet left of Manteling 101. Climb up the well featured face then a mantle.Départ assis 5 pieds à côté de Manteling 101. Grimper droit sur la face avec plusieurs bonnes prises et se rétablir sur la vire.
routes.Location: Between Put All Your Fears to Rest and Manteling 101Entre Put All Your Fears to Rest et Manteling 101

routes.name: November
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: SDS matched on an obvious flake with decent feet. November moves to the left following the rail feature and incorporating a sharp crimp for the left hand and a potentially painful heel hook. From here a big move will take you to a huge jug at the lip. The topout was cleaned off this past fall, adding some enjoyable moves to get established above the lip.
routes.Location: Located under the small roof on the boulder behind and to the left of the Gateway boulder.
routes.Protection: A pad and/or a spotter to protect a rock and tree which can become dangers if you swing off or miss the big jug.

routes.name: Orbital Insertion
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Stand start matched on a flake just under the lip with your choice of a number of slippery edges for feet. Make a few moves up through right facing incut crimps in order to slap to the blunt arete which runs up the centre of the boulder. Finish up the arete as per Orbital Traverse.   A number of options exist to move through the crimps to the arete all of which are somewhat awkward.   It might also be possible to sit start this problem by moving up through the sloping edges that are eventually used for feet. This would be considerably harder than the stand.
routes.Location: Starts just under the lip to the right of the blunt arete in the middle of the Orbital Boulder. The Orbital Traverse begins on the far left hand side of this boulder and moves up through blocky corners to the arete. From the arete look down and to the right, the starting flake should be fairly obvious.   The Orbital Boulder is located just to the right of the Tigerlily Boulder, near the end of the main approach trail. From the Highball/Malaria area follow the trail through some jumbled boulders (The Slab and Africa Boulders are found here) for a few minutes. The Tigerlily Boulder is tall and has a prominent ledge running through it.

routes.name: The Orbital Traverse
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: SDS at the lowest point of the left end of the boulder (closest to the Tigerlily Boulder), follow the series of rising, blocky corners until the blunt, sloping arete around the centre. Make moves to edges higher up and roll over the lip, finishing up the low angle slab. The traverse right can be somewhat pumpy if you move slowly with the crux coming at the move to the edges once the underside of the boulder steepens.
routes.Location: On the shorter boulder next to the Tigerlily Boulder. The Tigerlily Area is the second last area with established problems if you are following the lower trail along the swamp which begins at The Gateway Boulder. Tigerlily is a very  prominent and aesthetic line and Orbital Traverse stands out as well with its blocky corners.
routes.Protection: pad for the crux if desired

routes.name: Pharaohs Peak
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: SDS on left of large crack. Reach up to round nub, traverse right to triangle crimp and gain the upper peak. Avoid arret.
routes.Location: Route is before new Squamish and after the great roof in the area marked as "Talus Field" in old PDF guide.

routes.name: Prisoner of Gravity
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Great line on the backside of the Highball Boulder - start on some high crimps and work your way up a precarious and delicate face. The crux is super cryptic and the feet are horrible at first. I am not going to give away beta here in part because I had to work out the sequence over multiple visits.; )Note...This problem is listed in the online guide as V4. The current consensus (other than the guidebook) is V5/6. I would call it a stout V5+ or V6 (depending on the area). The top moves are not trivial and involve a large dyno with your feet about 10 feet OTD so pad up.
routes.Location: Backside of the highball boulder

routes.name: Put All Your Fears To Rest
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: SDS/Crouch start along the sloping ledge which runs across the Gateway Boulder. Dyno or deadpoint to the lip and mantel.    This problem can vary greatly in difficulty depending on where you choose to begin, with the most challenging version moving direct through the blankest section of the face about midway along the boulder. As the problem is very short, dynoing this blank section is by far the most satisfying option.
routes.Location: On the Gateway Boulder, about midway along the sloping rail which runs across the boulder.

routes.name: Running of the Bulls
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb up the featured face
routes.Location: Between the arete and tree in front of you when you arrived by Gateway path

routes.name: Sahara
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Stand start on the undercling rail and a very high crimp/pinch around the middle of the boulder. Establish your feet then pull up hard on the crimp/pinch in order to reach an assortment of gaston/crimps that seem to be facing the wrong way. From here find somewhere to smear your feet and make a toss for a ledge at the top of the boulder, top out.   The starting undercling recently broke, not really changing the difficulty but potentially leaving this problem even more height dependent with the loss of a foothold. If you are under 5'10" this will probably feel harder than v4.
routes.Location: Start around the middle of the Africa Boulder, the (broken) undercling and high crimp/pinch are fairly obvious. The Africa Boulder is found just before the Tigerlily Area if you are following the trail from the Highball area. The boulder is a vertical face with a blank section running through its centre.

routes.name: Scratching Post Direct
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Stand start on a crimp and a sloping edge around chest height just right of a tree. Move up and right through decent but somewhat hidden holds with some tricky, slick feet.   The original and full line starts on the left arete and traverses into the start holds for the direct version. If you can squeeze past the tree this might still go but doesn't add much value or challenge.
routes.Location: On the backside of the Apathy Boulder. The Apathy boulder is opposite the Malaria Boulder and the problem West Nile Direct, it has a scooped face with a decent V0. From the "clearing" walk around on the right or through a tunnel on the left to find the starting holds for this problem in the middle of the boulder.

routes.name: Solid Ether
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Stand or SDS, either way the starting holds are sidepulls found along the edges of the prominent bulge feature. While both versions require a wide wingspan, the SDS is more height dependent feeling like v4 while the stand is closer to v3.   Squeeze and slap your way upwards using edge sidepulls until high crimps allow for an exit to the right into the depression, or straight up. Heading direct feels less secure and may need a scrub first.
routes.Location: Located on the cliff-facing side of the Highball boulder. From "Waiting in Line..." go around the right side of the boulder, this problem is the first you will encounter.   This area is also the common downclimb for the Highball boulder, it is a good idea to get a feel for the problems here before attempting Waiting in Line...
routes.Protection: At least one pad is nice, a spotter can protect against the uneven landing further back as well.

routes.name: The Survivalist
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
