routes.Description: Left hand start of Electric Chair. Do some hard V4+ moves starting on the left hand side of the boulder that Electric Chair is on. There are some obvious awkward side pulls and poor feet to start. Pull up and into a poor gaston crimp and then cross over your left hand into some bad crimps that are used for the footholds on Electric Chair (V4/5). Now traverse right into Electric Chair, shake out and save some energy for the final left lock-off move on Electric Chair that goes to the lip. Make another move to get to the very top which is a Thank God jug!
routes.Protection: Lots of pads. I took the fall up around 15 feet in the iron cross section which was wild but fun. A fall on the final moves of Electric Chair would be sketchy but probably ok (?) with enough pads and good spotters.

routes.name: The Sword in the Stone
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This is a really nice line next to the right of Sahara.Start with two strange underclings and some really awkward splayed feet - one far out left, one on a tiny dimple beneath you. Now pull up into an extremely sharp crimp (the "sword" crimp), get a poor gaston for your right, shuffle your feet a bit and dead point for an obvious pinch crimp up over the lip before slapping the top.The sequence is very aesthetic. A contender for the best V6 in the area.
routes.Location: Just before you get to Tiger Lily and Orbital Traverse
routes.Protection: Couple pads tops. I used an extra pad wrapped around the tree behind the problem as you can fall into it.

routes.name: Theorem
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start with an undercling and your right foot heel hooked on the rail near the start of Gatineau Special. You can get a heel-toe cam thing going. Now do the big left hand move to the crimp at the start of Twisted. Option 1: Match the obvious left hand crimp and finish Twisted as usual (*hard).Option  2: Right hand to the side pull crimp. Heel hook your left foot onto the rail near the start of Twisted and bump your left hand hard to the good sloper hold at the lip and finish as per Twisted.
routes.Location: Sit down start to Twisted by Design.

routes.name: Tigerlily
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit down start, start climbing the left facing dihedral and continue up to top out. There are branches you have to crawl under at the top out.   youtube.com/watch?v=CCJa-eb…
routes.Location: The boulder is located at the back of the Calabogie bouldering area. There is a swamp nearyby. You can get down the boulder at the back.

routes.name: Trade Route to India
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Stand start matched on a good undercling rail with your choice of solid feet. A big move, likely a dyno unless you are particularly tall, leads roughly straight up to an angled, arrow-shaped rail. Match, and head straight up to the lip, this time relying on your rubber for footing.   V4 in the guide, but this grade is "severely height dependent".
routes.Location: Located on the Africa Boulder, found along the trail just before the Tigerlily Boulder if you are coming from the Highball area. The boulder is a fairly obvious vertical face with a blank mid section. Trade Route... starts a few feet in from the left arete, wherever the undercling feels best to you.

routes.name: Trench Warfare
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: SDS on the same good flake as "November". Instead of moving to the left up the rail make a stab out right to a small, sharp crimp. As for November the crux comes reaching for a big jug at the lip of the boulder. Once you stick the (rightmost) jug, pull the lip and top out directly up the slab using small crimps and some mantel powers.
routes.Location: Located under the small roof on the boulder behind and to the left of the Gateway Boulder.
routes.Protection: Same as November, pad and/or spotter to watch for a swing backwards into rocks and a tree.

routes.name: Twisted By Design
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: SDS matched on an obvious crimp just under the lip of a small overhang with a number of options for feet. Make a hard pull over the lip to a uneven ledge, then a series of awkward, bunched cross moves to get established on the arete. Short and tough.   A harder extension begins under the overhang to the right on a hidden undercling and traverses into the starting crimp. This is The Theorem V7.
routes.Location: This problem is located on the trail facing arete on the Nine-Tenths boulder. If you are following the trail from the Gateway boulder this is the first problem encountered. The start hold is well chalked for Calabogie and obvious.

routes.name: The Ultimate Everything
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start with right hand on positive side pull crimp and right hand on sloper sidepull. Go up to the ledges leftwards to a good hold and top out.
routes.Location: Right next to the tree left of the blunt arete. On Papoose boulder on right side of passage to mid cliff.

routes.name: Waiting in Line for the Electric Chair
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Super highball line on the backside of Malaria. This line is tall and starts on an obvious set of crimps on the right hand side of the obvious main face of the tall boulder. Beware though as the crux comes at the very end about 17-18 feet OTD - big lock off move. Once you hit the lip with your left hand before to adjust your feet and move again right to the top which is a huge jug...

routes.name: Waterlily
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Stand start on the lowest point of the prominent ledge which rises up and left into Tigerlily. This eliminates the difficult SDS and opening moves of Tigerlily but retains a big move off the ledge to a sidepull jug and the delicate finishing moves on somewhat hidden edges.    The topout may require some cleaning and care around the branches from a nearby tree. The downclimb should also be done with care as the backside of the boulder is covered in debris and a lot of moss.
routes.Location: On the Tigerlily Boulder, the second last area with established problems if following the trail from the Gateway Boulder along the swamp. The starting ledge is obvious.
routes.Protection: Pads for the uneven, rocky landing, and a spotter would help confidence as you move off of the juggy rail to smaller holds.

routes.name: West Nile Direct
routes.FA:: Jody Miall (the full West Nile line)
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Direct start to the longer West Nile V7ish. SDS matched on a sloping edge underneath the roof. Some start with feet deeper under the short roof, but a more interesting beta involves facing feet outwards and lifting off the ground using a toe hook on a triangular ledge at the lip. Bring hands to feet and continue upwards along the slopey corner and top out.    The full line starting at the far right of the roof section near Malaria traverses along ledges to link up with West Nile Direct. The grade is unconfirmed as far as I know, and beta is unclear at this point.
routes.Location: On the far left end of the Malaria Boulder just before the Westward Ho! face. The starting holds (especially the ledge at the lip) should be visible from the clearing which is home to the Highball and Malaria Boulders. Beautiful Odyssey is on the opposite side of this boulder and Malaria is to the right of this problem.
routes.Protection: Crash pads under the roof to protect your tailbone from some rock.

routes.name: Westward Ho!
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Stand start with holds around waist to chest height. Head straight up. The moves get slightly longer as you make your way higher. Highly featured face with interesting colour and some crystal-like holds.
routes.Location: Located on one of the swamp-facing sides of the Malaria boulder. Just to the left of West Nile Direct, and around the corner from Beautiful Odyssey. The ground can get a bit wet so watch your feet. Nice view of the swamp from the top on a warm, fall day.

routes.name: Wolf in Sheep's Clothing
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the far backside of the Chief boulder in a low cave. You can start on the lowest sloping crimp (full value) or the slightly higher rail (original start). Work your way up and out the cave using a big left hand lock off to an ok crimp, deadpoint to a poor half pad sloping right hand crimp that looks unusable then to the lip and awkward mantle.* note - its hard to avoid dabbing on this

routes.name: Yellow Fever
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: SDS matched on the obvious ledge at the lip. Move up over the right of the bulge using sloping holds at the lip. Mantel tenuously over the bulge and top out on a lichen covered slab.    The mantel and top out can seem unlikely, especially with the lichen, but the needed holds are all there and clean.    Essentially this problem is the right exit for West Nile Direct, eliminating the first difficult move to get established on the ledge.
routes.Location: Located near the swamp on the Malaria Boulder. Start on the obvious ledge in the West Nile line and head right.
routes.Protection: pad for the slightly uneven, rocky landing

routes.name: Addition to Tradition
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Long horizontal roof traverse that transitions onto a slab finish. Great gear and a fun lead!
routes.Location: Easy to identify right running roof early in the far end sector. Located left of the last easy down climb.

routes.name: Applicator
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The crux is at the roof, the 3rd bolt. Beneath the roof is a nice finger pocket for the left hand and then a nice layback for the right. Just above the roof line is a good right hand and then fire up and to the right of the 3rd bolt by a couple feet for a very good jug.
routes.Location: Stone marker on ground

routes.name: Bad Roady
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Bolted with a single cam placement this route follows a slightly tech start to a fun roof.
routes.Location: This climb has the usual bogie style route sign at the bottom via a painted rock. It is the right most climb under the main roof that starts this sector.
routes.Protection: Bolted but requires a cam placement.

routes.name: Drilled
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Marker at the bottom of the climb. Easy climbing to a roof. Mantle over it (crux) to easier climbing.
routes.Location: Stone marker on bottom of climb

routes.name: E.B.
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: 5.9 Climbing to very high first bolt. Find a way over the first bulge, cruise though some easy slab moves to the upward trending right dihedral. When the dihedral runs out get your slab game on for some delicate thin slab up to easy ground and a fun overhanging exit.
routes.Location: Directly right of Squanchy

routes.name: Eagles Nest Top Out
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Easy slab climb that tops out near the lookout.
routes.Protection: Glue in bolts with fixed anchors.

routes.name: Hawt for Hilti
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Nice face climbing with a slightly slab crux near the end. Proper feet and good beta will make this one feel easier.
routes.Location: Route directly left of Squanchy

routes.name: Head Arete
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Bolted vertical face climb with a left arete that leads into slab near the top requiring gear for protection.
routes.Location: First climb (climbers left) when entering the far end section.

routes.name: It's 4:20 Somewhere
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Keep some energy for the little crux on the slab at the end.
routes.Location: Next to On the Road Again

routes.name: Ivkovic's Arête
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on a ramp and then climb the arête.
routes.Location: Stone marker on ground

routes.name: Neat Cafe
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A thin vertical crack to the first bolt that leads into a crux bulge with decent side pull edges and hidden crimps. Head left to the third bolt below a roof feature, then up and to the right to the anchors. Send it, then visit the actual Cafe in Burnstown its named after for some post send beverages.
routes.Location: Obvious arête feature early in the far end sector. Name plate at base reads "Neat Cafe 5.11b S".

routes.name: On the road again
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Some face climbing past a bulge and then a delicate slab finish. Fun and exciting throughout.
routes.Location: Stone marker on the ground

routes.name: Pink Eye
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sequency corner to a good rest. An easier middle section leads to a second rest stance. Fire the overhanging dihedral to a hero clip at the lip. Pro tip: warm up both shoulders and consider stick clipping the 3rd draw.
routes.Location: Starts in the overhanging dihedral.

routes.name: Pussy Whipped
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Bouldery start with two bolts, then head right along a nice finger crack to an arête. Pull around the arête then head up onto the slab above that terminates into a dihedral with a prow. Clip the last bolt and head up easy slab to the anchors.
routes.Location: Located just above a crumbling limestone like boulder at the base. Easy to spot due to the striking right traverse on the overhanging face.
routes.Protection: Four bolts and fixed anchors. Singles in hand to finger sized cams.

routes.name: Res-Erection
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Crimp and layback into the roof. Finger the pocket and fire for the finger lock.
routes.Location: Stone marker on ground

routes.name: Squanchy
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb a thin face (crux) past three bolts to a ledge. Then up a corner, to an arete, a short hand-traverse to a rest, then through a final small roof to the anchors.
routes.Location: Stone marker on the ground

routes.name: Strolsma
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The same thin start as Neat Cafe but heads right at the good ledge. Enjoy the rest on a short section of easy slab before the vertical jug climbing. Terrain continues to steepen until slightly overhanging as it narrows up the shoot.

routes.name: Tidy Diner
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Prominent vertical orange face with a rather snakes and ladders approach to climbing.
routes.Location: Look for the long slab start to a high first bolt right of she got drilled.

routes.name: Twinkie
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The lower half of Brick Top and currently the first route encountered when arriving at the Core Values Sector. All routes in this sector have markers at their base. Climb the vertical face passing several cruxes, but the most important one is between the 3rd and 4th bolt
routes.Location: There is a flat diamond surface in the middle of the route.
routes.Protection: 5 bolts and an anchor with 1 ring

routes.name: Ain't No Wifey
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Great first lead for new climbers, easy climbing and well protected.
routes.Location: Located twelve feet left of cheat stick and can be identified by a marked stone at base reading "Ain't No Wifey 5.8S". Climb begins right of a small maple tree located at the base and trends left.
routes.Protection: Glue in bolts and fixed anchors

routes.name: Boulder Dash
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Several small roofs and a tricky pull into a small dihedral before giving way to easier climbing to the anchors.
routes.Location: Name-stone reads "Boulder Dash 5.9 S" at the base.

routes.name: Cheat Stick
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Another classic beginner lead climb with nuanced movement between bolts four and five.
routes.Location: Located twelve feet right of Ain't No Wifey and can be identified by a marked stone at base reading "Cheat Stick 5.8S".
routes.Protection: Six glue in bolts and fixed anchors.

routes.name: City Folk
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Easy slab/face climbing gives way to an imposing roof. Enjoy a short bouldery mantel before the chains.
routes.Location: Identified by a marked stone at base reading "City Folk 5.11a S".

routes.name: Desert Trip
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Nice mix of terrain featuring a bulge and a fun mantle before the anchors.
routes.Location: Runs on the right side of the bulge shared with "Tree Hugger" (located to the left of bulge). Route can be identified by a marked stone at base "Tree Hugger 5.9S".
routes.Protection: Glue in bolts and fixed anchors.

routes.name: Dogma
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Hard start variation of Karma Points. Makes use of the roof arête to the left to give a bouldery start.
routes.Location: Located left of the dihedral feature used in Karma Points. Shares anchors as well, but keep left of the bolts until the end.
routes.Protection: Four glue in bolts and fixed anchors.

routes.name: Hakuna-Matata
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Optional finger sized cam may be placed near the end of the climb in a horizontal crack to reduce runout.
routes.Location: Route can be identified by a marked stone at base "Hakuna-Matata". Located 4 feet right of "Moje Zlato".

routes.name: Huffin Glue Ins
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Bouldery start that eventually gives way to easy slab climbing after bolt 3. Belayer may want a loose tether to small tree at base to prevent stepping of narrow base.
routes.Location: Route can be identified by a marked stone at base "Huffin Glue Ins 5.10b S". Located 6 feet right of "Hakuna-Matata".

routes.name: The Hulk
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Stride up the relaxed ramp up to the first bolt and prepare to send the vertical face. Don't get too psyched after sending the crux main wall and miss the anchors up and to the left. Shirt off, torn jean shorts and pumped forearms are a must for a send in the proper style.
routes.Location: Easily identifiable by its proud vertical face that just to the right of a proud overhang.

routes.name: Karma Points
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Juggy start with a slab face the follows a line up the dihedral.
routes.Location: Shares anchors and bolts with Dogma but hugs the dihedral on the right.

routes.name: Moje Zlato
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Bolted line that follows a nice dihedral featuring some stemming, face climbing and small mantel.
routes.Location: Route can be identified by a marked stone at base "Moje Zlato 5.9S". Located 4 feet left of "Hakuna-Matata".
routes.Protection: 4 Bolts and fixed anchor (single ring with chain backup).

routes.name: Nocturnal Tendencies
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Gain the slab, climb up the low angle dihedral till you can stand on a block on the left. Pull up to the next ledge, then start by working the small roof up to the big roof with a V-notch. Pull through the big roof using holds on either side of the V-notch. (Chains hang through the top of the notch to make it safe for lowering and top rope.)
routes.Location: Between Too Many Puppies and Tree Hugger
routes.Protection: 6 bolts and bolted anchors with chains and rap rings.

routes.name: Rock Hugger
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Multi roof looking climb that really never requires a mantel. A combination of slab and juggy climbing.
routes.Location: Climbers left most route on the mid sector, just 10 feet left of "Too Many Puppies". The route nearly converges with the slab of Too Many puppies.

routes.name: Stage Left
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start in a slight crevice before moving up and right into the main face climbing. While the plaque lists this as "M" for mixed, there is no need place any gear as the minimal "runout" is over easy terrain.
routes.Location: Located just six feet left of stage right and can be identified by a marked stone at base reading "Stage Left 5.8M".

routes.name: Stage Right
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Cruiser slab climb with easy grabs.
routes.Location: Located just six feet right of stage left and can be identified by a marked stone at base reading "Stage Right 5.7S".

routes.name: Steve As' Route
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climbs the obvious crack/corner system.
routes.Location: Obvious crack/corner system between Boulder Dash and Moje Zlato.
routes.Protection: Fixed anchors shared with Moje Zlato.

routes.name: Too Many Puppies
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Short juggy face climb start gives way to the prominent slab climb that follows to the right of the arête. Route can be identified by a marked stone at base "Too Many Puppies 5.8S".
routes.Protection: Glue in bolts and fixed anchors

routes.name: Tree Hugger
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Smooth 5.8 climbing with an easier roof than Desert Trip provided you scout out the good jugs.
routes.Location: Just 6 feet left of Desert Trip, second bolted line in the sector. Has a name plaque reading "Tree Hugger 5.8 S" below it.

routes.name: Breakfast Beer
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Slab start that traverses right before heading up and slightly left the rest of the way to the anchors. Low crux, but no harder than 5.8 despite the 5.10a grade.
routes.Location: Has name stone at base reading "Breakfast Beer 5.10a S"

routes.name: Breakfast in Vienna
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A few jugs on the face before a decent mantel on the roof. Mantel is slightly run out, so its possible to plug some gear in to protect it better. Afterwards its quick and easy slab climbing to the anchors. While listed as a trad/mixed climb, its possible to sport climb it if confident at 5.9.

routes.name: Slippery When Wet
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Roof crux

routes.name: Assimilation
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This route more closely follows the bolted line and makes use of the lower crimpy crux. Must avoid the left dihedral until the end of the climb.
routes.Location: Located on the same north facing wall as several other variations and is opposite to popular top rope routes; In the Black, Black Book and Facing the Black. The rock is riddled with good edges and horizontal hand cracks.

routes.name: Assimilation Direct
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This route more closely follows the bolted line and makes use of the lower crimpy crux. Must avoid the left dihedral completely. Unlike the other two lines, the end features slaby face climbing with no aid from the roof on the left.
routes.Location: Located on the same north facing wall as several other variations and is opposite to popular top rope routes; In the Black, Black Book and Facing the Black. The rock is riddled with good edges and horizontal hand cracks.

routes.name: Assimilation Indirect
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Left most line that follows the easiest weakness in the rock.
routes.Location: Located on the same north facing wall as several other variations and is opposite to popular top rope routes; In the Black, Black Book and Facing the Black. The rock is riddled with good edges and horizontal hand cracks.
routes.Protection: Bolted with an anchor shared with two other harder variations.

routes.name: Awkward Overhangs
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This route features two overhangs, a small slab section, a small traversal and a mantel and starts near the base of a small pine tree that grows out of a small outcropping at the bottom. Alpine draw on bolt five might be handy for reducing rope drag on the overhang.
routes.Location: First route to the right of easy way down, roughly 10 feet down the path heading south.
routes.Protection: Six bolts with two fixed anchors.

routes.name: Cool But Concerned
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A early small roof protected by a single bolt followed by several horizontal finger and hand cracks. Top out on thin vertical and horizontal finger cracks on a slightly slab face.
routes.Location: Located roughly twenty feet left of second easy way down, this wall features several closely grouped trad climbs. A small roof with a single bolt will identify the start of this climb.

routes.name: Cosmic Teacup
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Fun mantle to start followed by what initially feels like a blank slabby face. Second bolt can be tricky and exposed. Lost after the fourth bolt? Don't worry the anchor is hidden directly over the top of the ridge and getting there is easier than the previous few bolts.
routes.Location: Starts on the same shelf feature as "Pull Up" but runs left.
routes.Protection: Four bolts and a fixed anchor with rings.

routes.name: Flaky Flake
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Great first trad lead with easy climbing featuring bomb placements.
routes.Location: Left most line in the main amphitheater like wall that features many trad climbs towards the far end of the easy way down sector.

routes.name: La Madrina
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Was a trad route but has unfortunately been bolted despite copious and good protection available. Follows the arête out right before pulling in from under a small roof. Follow a short face climb before traversing a nice horizontal hand crack below the finish.
routes.Location: Located on the same north facing wall as several other variations and is opposite to popular top rope routes; In the Black, Black Book and Facing the Black. The rock is riddled with good edges and horizontal hand cracks.
routes.Protection: Bolted with fixed anchors, but a very easy trad lead due to good pro.

routes.name: Locutus
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Slabby start into an awkward dihedral like mantel.
routes.Location: Located directly right of Tea. Earl Grey. Hot.

routes.name: Make it So
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Broken corner, then up to the slab.Stick clip advised for the first bold.
routes.Location: Lefmost route of this section of the wall

routes.name: The Next Generation
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Easy slab start that quickly escalates in difficulty and verticality at the first bolt. Very sustained technical climbing on small crimps featuring traversals following the only weaknesses in the rock.
routes.Location: Roughly 150 meters left (North) of the second easy way down. Easily identifiable by locating the pine tree growing out of the rock face with an exposed root system. The route starts just to the right of this tree.

routes.name: Nutty Nuts
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Similar start to Flaky Flake, but follows a vertical finger crack half way up before heading right of a tree near the anchor.
routes.Location: Second line right from the left most side of the main amphitheater like wall that features many trad climbs towards the far end of the easy way down sector.

routes.name: Pull Up
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Starts on a nice shelf with an immediate mantle followed by lovely slab with a slight dihedral right.
routes.Location: Starts on the same shelf feature as "Pull Up" but runs right.
routes.Protection: Four bolts and a fixed anchor with rings.

routes.name: Real Men Don't Place Pro
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Short slab section that meets at the base of a roof. Follow a short leftward traverse to a bolt protecting the crux. A balancey crux gives way to an easy slab dihedral to the anchors. The anchors are simply two bolts with no rings, walk off or make use of the anchors on "The Next Generation" t the left to lower when cleaning.
routes.Location: Located on the same propionate feature as "The Next Generation" above a large boulder field.
routes.Protection: Mixed climb with a single crux bolt. Two bolts for the anchor, but no rap rings present.

routes.name: Tea. Earl Grey. Hot.
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: An easy slabby start leading to a well protected mantle that meets with the dihedral. Well protected and not sustained.

routes.name: Warbird
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Upward to the mini-roof
routes.Location: Right of Make It So.

routes.name: Breakfast Cookie
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A wondering line featuring several small traversals and technical movements around small arêtes. A real 5.10b, stiffer than most in the area.
routes.Location: Right of Spruce Root Shute, and first bolted line in Star Trek Sector. Identifiable by budgey orange rock face and the identifier rock at the base reading "Breakfast Cookie 5.10a S".

routes.name: FF Direct
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Roof variation of First Flight, adds a punchy bottom to easier slab uptop.
routes.Location: Located in middle of the easy sport climbing wall near the end of the Star Trek Sector.

routes.name: First Flight
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start below a small roof and climb up, bypassing it to the right before heading left along the base of the second larger roof. Once clear blast up easy slab to the anchors. Possible to just sport lead it with a bit of runout between bolts 2 and 3. A single finger/hand sized cam placement will greatly reduce the ground fall potential under the roof.
routes.Location: Located mid wall below and slightly right of a small bolted roof.

routes.name: Homeward Bound
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Mixed line up slightly overhanging rock featuring great movement.
routes.Location: Located centrally in the left section of the main theatre area of the Star Trek Sector. Identifiable by the broken bolted line that is broken up by large rock bulge and horizontal cracks.

routes.name: I'm a Doctor, Not a Climber
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Mixed route with a bolted slightly bouldery start. Easy protection up top with several horizontal finger/hand cracks.
routes.Location: First bolted line (3 bolts) on the left of the face.

routes.name: Lobotomy
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Imagination is required to make use of an invisible smear. Don't be discouraged by the initial boulder problem as easier terrain leads to the anchors.
routes.Location: Starts up the prominent undercling feature.

routes.name: Phasers on Kill
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Follow the obvious hand crack vertical for twenty feet. Slight traverse left before entering a right overhanging dihedral near the top.
routes.Location: Vertical crack system located just left of the large overhanging wall. Should be 10 feet left of an osprey next, left of a bolted mixed line.

routes.name: Phasers on Kill Direct
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Mixed route on thinner features located on the bolted arête. Rejoin the Phasers on Kill trad line after bolt 2, with a slight traverse left before entering an overhanging dihedral near the top.
routes.Location: Located on the bolted arête between Phasers on Kill and Phasers on Stun trad lines.

routes.name: Phasers on Stun
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A juggy start leads into a crack system that widens with elevation. A right curving hand crack sits above a bulge, pull this and continue vertical until under a small roof in a slight dihedral. Head slightly up and very left, traversing across a thin hand crack with precise feet. A quick blast vertical using several horizontal hand cracks now gets you at the anchors.
routes.Location: Second from the left in the crack riddled wall left of the large overhanging section that is dominated by hard sport climbs. The first trad line right of the bolted start of tipytoe.
routes.Protection: Thin pro down low, but lots after about 12 feet. Fixed anchors shared with tipytoe.

routes.name: Spruce Root Chute
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Slightly runout start gives way to some good pro at the base of the v-notch. Continue up the notch into a flared chimney.
routes.Location: The first climb in Star Trek Sector. Identifiable as a v-notch left of Breakfast Cookie.
routes.Protection: No anchors, so walk off or rap on Breakfast Cookie to the right.

routes.name: Vulcan Mind
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A bouldery start for two bolts before traversing hard right for 20 feet along a horizontal finger to hand crack. Place a cam (~0.5) before heading up the face to a nice ledge rest above. A short jug haul left, up then right will get you to the anchor. Very stiff for the grade, especially by Calabogie standards.
routes.Location: Easy to locate traverse under a large roof.

routes.name: River Rapids
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Essentially River Run with a difficult lower section added.
routes.Location: Descend from where River Run begins, start in the same position as that climb with your hands and heel suspending your body off the ground. Then you'll traverse into River Run and complete both climbs.

routes.name: River Run
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The crux is transitioning from the lower section on the start hold to the higher section above it without cutting loose. After that, the traverse is quite simple.
routes.Location: It starts with your heel and two hands hanging on the hold in the red circle in the photo, so your body's suspended off the ground. Traverse to the right and top out at the green circle.

routes.name: Secret Garden
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Up to the ledge, then left to the face.  Coming over the face feels a bit exposed, and looks runout, but once you start the moves, you can see another bolt.  There looks to be another bolt line up to the right, which might be Garden Party, but not certain.
routes.Location: Starts at the "C4" painted on the wall.

routes.name: Holy Roller
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: An endurance run.  Small pockets and bad feet guard easier but long run to chains.
routes.Location: B3

routes.name: The Isolated Beauty
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Bolted trad climbing.  Steep pockets, fingerlocks and slab in one!
routes.Location: B5

routes.name: Ivy League
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Solid for the grade, long at that. Hard mantle move guards the chains
routes.Location: B2

routes.name: October
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sustained crack with good gear.
routes.Location: Starts at letter C.

routes.name: Pilgrim
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb through steep and powerful stone, a few cruxes await. Don't get your hopes up quite yet, the slab cruz might spit you off.
routes.Location: D3

routes.name: Piso Mojado
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Difficult slab to a roof where the business starts.  pull some hard moves to a 2nd roof and chains.
routes.Location: B6

routes.name: Quaglia
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Relatively short and bouldery, tiny crimps and hard pulls.
routes.Location: B4

routes.name: Sanctuary
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: As good as it gets!  A powerful and tech flared dihedral.
routes.Location: B7

routes.name: Sidewinder
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the crack at C1, traverse right for some reason and lead out on easy holds.
routes.Location: C1

routes.name: Swept Away
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start at letter B on the rock.  Climb through bulges and small roofs trending left.  Finish up in the notch left of the final roof.
routes.Location: First route left of the ladder.

routes.name: Whoopass
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the inside face of the chimney.  Trend left onto the arete and get your whoopass.  Don't stem!
routes.Location: D1

routes.name: Wysteria
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Good rests with a mixture of holds.  One of the easiest routes on this wall, good warmup.
routes.Location: Start at B1

routes.name: Foreigner
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: One of a kind at Cape Croker, Powerful solution pockets and stemming.

routes.name: The Big Cheese Traverse
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The Big Cheese trav. starts on the south side of the boulder on two blocky holds. Some big opening moves and heels or toes helps do what you have to to match the arete and swing the feet around then head up the west side of the boulder for a nice Topo.
routes.Location: Wrapping around the big cheese block

routes.name: Cheese Grater
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: First move is the crux. sit start and pull on the same as "the big cheese traverse" but instead of heading left. head straight up. and Topo.
routes.Location: big cheese boulder. same start as big cheese traverse.

routes.name: Straight Cheesin'
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Straight Cheesin' is just right of "The big cheese traverse." and also just right of the dead tree. Low start in the dihedral work your way up over the lip through a cool half moon sloper pinch then up through the slab onsite smaller edges.
routes.Location: Right of the dead tree on "the big cheese block" sit start

routes.name: Papa's Easy Chair
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start low dow on the arete LH on undercling on the arete and RH undercling under the face. work around the bulge and paddle up through good holds. the first move is the toughest.
routes.Location: sit start low left on the arete

routes.name: Cheese Curd
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: sit start low on opposing compression rails. workup to the lip the toss a left heel and pull on a couple heinous crimps to gain the bulge.
routes.Location: 10' below the Brea end of big cheese block

routes.name: A Hobo's View
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)

routes.name: Bad But True
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Small Pro

routes.name: Baywatch
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: First bolted route when entering from western side.Up to crack and large mantle, then onto sharp headwall and left traverse to anchors.

routes.name: The Bear
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)

routes.name: Blood and Honey
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb up sidepulls and vary right to a few crimps into better holds above.

routes.name: Blood Donor
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)

routes.name: Brown Streak Boogie
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Nice crimps to start, after clipping second bolt your left hand will find a deep under cling which with the right feet will allow you to reach out to a small jug pocket, up to the right. Crossing and bumping our way to the top.

routes.name: Buckets for Day
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)

routes.name: Character Ranch / Interrobang Project
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Up to the big roof slab as you go up the prow of the sharp arrete then left to the anchors.
routes.Protection: 4 Bolts + ring chain anchors directly above

routes.name: Coexist
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)

routes.name: Dick Flex
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)

routes.name: DILLIGAF
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the arrete to the V corner, up the face into a chimney.Stick clip.
routes.Protection: 5 Bolts and 2 at the anchor, one side need a ring or quick link on end of the chain.

routes.name: Dream On
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Good cloning to a arete bulge, star fish through a V8 ish boulder

routes.name: Fat Lip
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Side pull and mantle up onto a slope ledge, using a right hand side pull the small crack, work your hands up, clipping as you go, pinching and pulling your way to the top.

routes.name: Grapes of Wrath
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)

routes.name: Gun Show
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb up to underneath the roof. Clip the bolt under the roof and either go left and dyno to a good rail or make some fun moves from below, going right and back left to the bolt over the lip.

routes.name: Hopium
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Bouldery & short with thing crux near the top.

routes.name: If it Grows, it Goes
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Dirty ledge be careful

routes.name: Indecisive
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Flow up a little seam, mix in some Crimps and side pull to a small roof finish.

routes.name: Just Do it
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Lots of stuff to grab, pinch, crimp, jug and brake off.Fun side pull start, go left to third bolt, then sail up to the anchors.

routes.name: Knock Your Block Off
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Blocky, Jugy

routes.name: Man Handle
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Jug and crimp your way up with a fun slab / balance move just past the "Man Handle", then smooth sailing up to the anchor.
routes.Protection: 4 Bolts + permeant carabiners for lowering only (do not top rope on these!)

routes.name: Marco Polo
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Corner Crack

routes.name: Mr. Humpty
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Hand jam the small vertical crack, up to a right hand pinchy jug match, then right hand up to a Juggy side pull pole, getting your third clip.  Left hand up to a crimp, matching slightly lower with the right hand, finding good feet under the roof to reach up to a shelf, matching on the shelf and humping your way over the ledge, using a horizontal crack position yourself to use a right side pole, exploding and bumping up to a left-hand up to a jugy clipping the finish.
routes.Protection: 5 bolts and a top carabiner, the 5th bolt was added between the top and 4th.

routes.name: One Knuckle Deep
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)

routes.name: One Moose Minimum
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Cool side pulls and pinch moves to the top

routes.name: Owl Toe (Open Project)
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Open Project Grade not known
routes.Protection: Bolts and same anchor with The Owl

routes.name: The Owl
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: First sport climb established in the crag. Power endurance line.Up through challenging interlocks, traverse left along solid crimps ending with some big moves to a jug rail.
routes.Protection: 4 Bolts and same anchor with Owl Toe

routes.name: Peanut Gallery
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)

routes.name: Pika-Chu
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Side pulls and cool crosses in the middle
routes.Protection: 4 Bolts and the anchor is technically redundant ish but really not the point, use discretion.

routes.name: The Poacher Direct
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Harder line directly through roof bulge with 3 crimpy moves to a bomber jug.

routes.name: The Poacher
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Stick to the right, then traverse back left to the anchors.

routes.name: Polar Bear Love
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Do something to get over the roof?

routes.name: Purple Queen
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Left most route on second wall at "The Great Roof".Easy climbing up to a ledge, then go to a small two finger pocket crux with left hand. Sidepull and go up to good holds above. At fourth bolt traverse right and finish on shared top anchors.
routes.Protection: 4 Bolts + shared chain-ring top anchor with Character Ranch / Interrobang to the right.

routes.name: Queen Bee
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)

routes.name: Rai-Chu
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Protection: Bolts and the anchor is technically redundant ish but really not the point, use discretion.

routes.name: Riff Raff
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Nice corner crack to a small chockstone.
routes.Protection: Crack it up, top out or try to use the anchor on Strong Side

routes.name: Soup Kitchen
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)

routes.name: Strong Side
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start left on the slab between a corner crack and the arrete, to the tinker below a small roof finishing on a jug above the draws.

routes.name: The Toe
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Left of the Owl and up the hill from K9 boulder.Straight up side pulls mantle onto large "toe" feature, good holds or biggest dyno of your lift to an okay jug.

routes.name: Topophilia
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)

routes.name: Tough Guy Shot
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the corner through a series of pinches and side pulls
routes.Protection: 4 Bolts, November 2023 there was a carabiner at the top not permit not permanent.

routes.name: The Weasel
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Needs a bath.

routes.name: Welcome Wagon
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Farthest right bolted route

routes.name: What's Urs is Mine
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Crux is at the start
routes.Protection: Its trad, but the first gear is about 4m up

routes.name: K9 arete
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: K9 is a low sds on the arete. RH on flat crimp LH on pinch. bust up to a jug then cruise your way to a nice height Topo.
routes.Location: on the arete of the k9 boulder

routes.name: The Devils Lettuce
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The Devils Lettuce is a campus problem at the far left end of the mystery machine face. hang start on obvious pocket jug, power up to the only hold on the face then move through it to the ledgey Topo.
routes.Location: left of mystery machine ext left

routes.name: Mystery Machine
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Stand start in obvious jug pocket. work up through some very nice holds and a shallow dish crux hold. pure line
routes.Location: middle of the south face of mystery machine

routes.name: Mystery Machine Ext. Left
routes.FA:: Tom C & Danny P cleaned together and sent back to back
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Mystery Machine left ext starts the same as mystery machine on the jug at the bottom of the face and in the dish pocket. move up and left into the sloper rail stab out left to a good but far pocket. really cool gym like move. almost as cool as the straight up line.
routes.Location: same start as MM but heads left to a pocket then up to Topo

routes.name: Scooby Doo
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Scooby Doo lies around the back side (west) side of the mystery machine block. start on flat ledgey crimps the first move is the toughest blast up to a side pull then back into some solid crimps to the Topo.
routes.Location: left of "the devils Lettuce"

routes.name: Those Meddling Kids
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Those meddling kids is a dyno on the east end of the mystery machine boulder.
routes.Location: right (east) face of mystery machine boulder

routes.name: The path of totality
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start up the arete at the left end of the roof. When you reach the face go to a crimp and do a big move to another crimp then bump to a big jug and it’s all good holds around the bulge and for the topo too.
routes.Location: The start is at the left end of the big roof. I will add pics to make it easier too

routes.name: Cubby
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Up the tall slab starting on the crack right to left, tricky top moves

routes.name: Grizzly
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Up the obvious prow, tall, high crux and top out will need more cleaning
routes.Location: Prow above the trail

routes.name: Kennisis Crack
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Follow the splitter 5.10 hand crack to a tricky top

routes.name: Woodsy
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: On the far side is a rising slopper rail, dirty in spring

routes.name: Bamboo Bump
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Up the amazing arete on the right side of the boulder

routes.name: Fuzzy Wuzzy
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Up jugs on the face - lots of options

routes.name: Koala Traverse
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Traverse left to right

routes.name: Stitch
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Thin crimps left to right

routes.name: Chitta Locca
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Retrobolted, start on Mystery and move right.

routes.name: Gwelp
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: sharp and awkward.crux at the anchors.Sandbagged.Need I say more?

routes.name: Iron Maiden
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb a pillar to a crack. (Retrobolted 2023)

routes.name: Mystery of a Lost History
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This route had old chopped bolts with origins unknown.  classic for DG. Bouldery and thin.

routes.name: Nifty Nook
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: short overhanging hang crack.  There is an extension of the same grade that goes higher.

routes.name: Riders of the Storm
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Techy, bring some dyno strength.

routes.name: Soul Food
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Stick clip the first bolt and start at the base of the arete. Pull through a boulder problem to reach the first ledge, where you can milk a knee bar rest. From there, tackle a series of thin, technical, and fingery moves to gain a small rest. The stout, reachy, and powerful crux boulder problem grants access to jugs that lead up to the anchors. Though short, this route offers beautiful climbing on stunning rock, all in a picturesque position within the gulley. Its over all too quick, but it sure was sweet while it lasted.

routes.name: Tough Day at the Office
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Lestmost sport route, next to pillar.  Start left of the pillar and traverse up and right,  plenty of horizontals with some big moves along the way.  Climbing may feel easier of you're taller.  A broken hold about halfway up has yielded a more difficult crux.

routes.name: Zoot Suit
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Hard but good placements.  Climb the lightning bolt 3m left of Tough Day At The Office.

routes.name: The Argument
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Fingercrack leads to a face on the left wall.

routes.name: Back Door
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Wide crack on the right side of the gully.

routes.name: Inside the Gully | 1732
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Stick Clip, crank the pockets on the arete to anchors.
routes.Location: 2m right of Still Waters.

routes.name: Still Waters
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Face climbing on gear. Exit to the left.
routes.Location: Right of Back Door.

routes.name: 25 and Spanish
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: An old route of unknown origin that is best avoided, sporadic bolt spacing makes this a dangerous endeavor.
routes.Protection: 2 bolts and anchors from right trad route

routes.name: 45 and French
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Pull the small roof to a crux halfway up, into easier climbing and anchors.

routes.name: If You Get the Daughter
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Old bolted variation of Mad River Crack,  albeit bolts were chopped. Climb left and join the Mad River Crack.

routes.name: Mack the Knife
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Fantastic arete climbing.   Originally put up on gear at 5.9.

routes.name: Mad River Crack
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Wide crack between Gully and Dank Wall

routes.name: Betty
routes.FA:: FA - Rick Susgin, Steve De Maio (1984); Equipper - Randy Kielbasiewicz
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Traverse right on a large block, and move up on decent holds, finishing with a nice view of the park if you peek over the top.
routes.Location: The route right of Motor Boat and rightmost bolted route on the island.

routes.name: Dancing in the Dragon's Jaw
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Diagonal weakness left of Veronica.  Hands on wide crack to the top. Walk off the back.

routes.name: Doin' The Motor Boat
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The bolted arete on the island's left side when coming down the descent gully. This climb used to be 5.8 until a hold broke, making the start a bit awkward. It's still a good arete climb and worth doing for those breaking into the grade.
routes.Location: Start as per Veronica and up the arete.

routes.name: Really Extreme! Ok, Pretty Extreme
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Crux moves to start leads to a high bolt, easier climbing above.

routes.name: Veronica
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Left side of the Arete, use it or don't.  Easier if you do, harder if you don't and will seem like a stretched out sandbag if you miss the solution hold and don't check your feet.
routes.Location: On the big block, left of the Arete "Doin' The Motorboat"

routes.name: Agony of da Feet
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the flake to a ledge and follow the less than ideal rock to the top.

routes.name: Dancing With the Devil
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Technical slab, nail a slopey dish crux and boogie down to the anchors.

routes.name: Darcy's Crack
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: On the right side of the crevice, climb the left side of the flake and follow up on pockets.

routes.name: The Happiness Machine
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Follow the scoopy crimps, perpetually wet and insecure.

routes.name: How Elevators Changed Paris
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the thin seam, crimp the face, mantle the head.

routes.name: Scarface
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Small problem on a smaller boulder.  An old bolt shows the way.

routes.name: Squeeze Ur Mout To Da Right An Say Ouin
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Access the small roof by way of a set of boulder moves.
routes.Location: First route on the wall

routes.name: 2020 Division
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Split like Olympic aspirations, first half consists of technical sequences while the top is overhanging and mildly easier. Stick clip it.

routes.name: Attention All Shoppers
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: barely retrobolted after a gear ascent.  Heady and commiting.

routes.name: Bolts left Your Wife
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Slab climb featuring finger cracks and several hidden finger pockets.
routes.Location: First bolted line on the north west face of the "tight Canyon" area. Located right of the arête that Mullicious is on.

routes.name: Chance
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Per FA: Located immediately right of Lucky. Tech slab crux that really tests your rubbery trust. Originally placed the anchors prior to the roof, but extended for some added fun and difficulty. This route will not be everyones cup of tea.

routes.name: Dirty 11
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: An old dirty 11 seldom travelled.

routes.name: Does She Go?
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the corner left of "EBTKS".
routes.Protection: Anchors up and to the left (not shared with EBTKS)

routes.name: Donald Pump
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: V3 boulder problem to 2nd bolt, proceed towards the seam.  Potential dyno is necessary depending on height to gain the overhanging headwall.  Cruise the dihedral to anchors.

routes.name: Everything But The Kitchen Sink
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Little bit if everything, except the kitchen sink.

routes.name: Hillary Crimpin'
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: V2 boulder problem, with a blank slab section to a large overhang delivers a little bit of everything.

routes.name: The Invasion
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the roof into a corner, and pull some more roof.  Follow the cracks and corners to anchors.

routes.name: Kansai
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb up the face on good holds towards the first bolt.  When the holds disappear you will be acutely aware of the fact that the first bolt is still well out of reach, and the distance to the ground will make you think long and hard about the consequences of a fall.  Pull the crux, then clip the first bolt.  You're not home free; there's still groundfall potential above, but at least the climbing will be easier.  Continue up the face and into a corner system on well spaced bolts to the chains.This was a trad route until it was retrobolted.  It's still not a sport route, though.
routes.Location: At least one of the guidebooks places this route to the right of a tree that is no longer standing (it's on the ground).  Located approximately 50 meters left of prowject (the prominent, severely overhanging, arete).
routes.Protection: Bolts.  Stick clip recommended.  However if you're worried about falling on a 5.10a, you'd probably be better off just hopping on something else.

routes.name: Limited Time Offer
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Previously the "Barnes Project". Dennis started the line in 2016 but did not finish the top. The line now goes all the way to the top and enjoys a fun upper headwall and great view of the valley. 5.11 technical face and arete climbing leads to a no-hands rest. Rest, then enjoy the cool moves and exposure as you solve the overhanging headwall boulder to reach the anchors. Rock is much more solid than it looks. Missing bolt 1, bring a stick clip.
routes.Location: Bolted line right of Prowject

routes.name: Lucky
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Technical slab with a steep roof finish

routes.name: Mullicious
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: slab arete, fun climbing.

routes.name: The Prowject
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Immensely fun, steep, and long boulder problem, that packs in a lot of moves for its relative height. Start with a big move out left and then up to an edge before traversing right. Pull a perfect gym boulder move to a good crimp, traverse back left, then work that knee bar rest. Unfold from the knee bar, fight to get to the sloper jugs, then big fun compression moves to decent holds will take you to the top - if you don't pump off! As DG is characterized by near vertical or slightly overhung techy face climbing, this steep, bouldery gem with consistent, burly, fun movement on fantastic rock is a true prize.Just look at that prow!

routes.name: Ramek
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Finger crack on a short angled wall. Left of Say No More.

routes.name: Say No More
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Left trending hand crack on the right side of the crevice.

routes.name: Scream Dream
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Great route just right of Prowject.  Finishes half way up the wall.

routes.name: Shibuya (Dirty but Fun)
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the discontinuous right trending crack system for three bolts to a rest.  Finish with big holds on steepish rock.
routes.Location: At least one of the guidebooks places this route to the left of a tree that is no longer standing (it's on the ground).  Located approximately 50 meters left of prowject (the prominent, severely overhanging, arete).

routes.name: Silly Putty
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Nice crack 5m right of the canyon exit, Follow up to a V shaped groove. Hard for the grade.

routes.name: Thunder in Paradise
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Tight Canyon area. Roughly 20-ft climber's right of Everything But The Kitchen Sink. Large flake at the start of the route. Climbs into the large cave-like ledge and up. 5 bolts.

routes.name: Thursites
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Dingy, dirty crack right of Your Wife.

routes.name: Turd Overhang (Thamel)
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb by an arete, but wouldn't call it an arete climb.  There is a wall behind you so be careful if you whip.To avoid swinging, it is recommended you clip into the second draw from the ground (ie skip the first draw) or unclip the first draw from the ground after you clip into the second draw.By the second bolt there are rocks that look loose.  They are fine to pull on, but make sure you double check by tapping the rocks.There is a nice ledge right before the anchors.
routes.Location: Start is just left of the arete/at the arete.

routes.name: Water Over the Bridge
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: As per Nathan Lewis: Bolted line with a long chain 2/3 the way up. Chose one of two entrances — tech the face for a sport climbers paradise, or lie back the crack with the tree at the bottom and use the right of two bolts to merge with the direct line — grade doesn’t change. Fun flowy climbing with interesting movement to a tricky thin boulder problem guarding the chains. Often wet at the bottom.

routes.name: We'll Burn That Bridge (when we get to it)
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Newly bolted route right of Kansai that starts in a short dihedral before continuing into several flakes and ledges in the same crack system. I have not confirmed the grade but was told 5.11a, feel free to update.5.11a. Climb up on to the ledge on the right and traverse left to the flake where you'll meet the crux before getting to a nice ledge for a rest. Rest well at the ledge before making your way up to the anchors on easy climbing.Watch out for the tree at the first ledge. Rope will sometimes get stuck at the tree when cleaning and pulling the rope in the end.

routes.name: Your Wife
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Follow the face and cracks on the left side.

routes.name: 17 Seconds
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start as per Dust In The Wind, move right up to a bolt and to a notch, enjoy the easier stuff to the top.

routes.name: Capital C
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The left route option to Deep Green,  small variation on a different crack system leads to the same anchors.

routes.name: Does Not Deepthroat
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A route requiring trad experience, Thin climbing up a seam.

routes.name: Dust In The Wind
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Thin crack to some steep moves and a bolt,  Traverse left to reach the anchors on Deep Green.

routes.name: Eco Terrorist
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Follow the flake system right of Sorry Squire to the anchors on I Scratched.

routes.name: From The Edge of the Deep Green Sea
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb a shallow crack to a bolt and traverse another crack horizontally, climb the dihedral to anchors.

routes.name: The Grand Master, The Bar Tender and the Clown
routes.FA:: Randy Kielbasiewicz, Graeme Knight, Chris Lovett
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the blocky start to a ledge, clip the bolt.  Keep following the crack and face to anchors.

routes.name: I Scratched The Record
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Old schol class.  Follow the obvious finger crack left leaning to the top.  Pull the roof and grab chains.

routes.name: Just Joshin'
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the blocky flake of The Grand Master, move right and follow the first crack system to the top.

routes.name: Noncreative Garbage
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the awesome flake to a roof, move right to another crack and towards to anchors.

routes.name: Rideout Crescent
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Follow the undercling right of I Scratched, turns to layback and anchors.

routes.name: Sorry Squire
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the blocky flake of The Grand Master, move right and follow the second crack system to the top.

routes.name: That's His Blood
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on That's My Blood, traverse right to the next groove and proceed.

routes.name: That's My Blood
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Short route that quits due to rock quality.
routes.Location: 8m right of 17 seconds, find the groove.

routes.name: Bosch Goggles
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Bolted adventure, not a great route and not recommended unless you like type 2 fun.

routes.name: Bye Bye Mother
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Further right from Helios, you'll come across a fun-looking corner splitter just left of 3 large trees near the wall and Peach Sparrow. Good and relatively safe practice route for bigger gear placement.

routes.name: Catch the Sun
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Powerful body tension on thin moves, balance your way to a headwall crux that tends to chew up and spit out.

routes.name: Connection Error
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Per FA -“Immediately right of Over Easy, 3rd route right of Peach Sparrow. Equipped right before the first lockdown in 2020. Tricky slab leads to rest and then a very hard v6 boulder. Cruiser top. Name refers to our connected life, yet lack of connection. Also, when you fall, its a connection error. Is that a Dad joke? Unsure of grade.”

routes.name: Helios
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Just right of Ra. Climb trends rightwards. A good beginner option.

routes.name: Legends of the Fall
routes.FA:: Unknown, first recorded ascent: Mark Smith (2011-2012)
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: An amazing mix of technical footwork and powerful, fingery climbing on a slightly overhanging wall. Boulder up to the "horn of Africa" feature and then head leftwards onto the face. Making your way up to the tooth on the prominent bulge will require precise footwork and strong fingers. A surprisingly good hold after gives you a chance to catch your breath and muster some power for the finish.   Like some of the other routes at Devil's Glen, this was originally bolted (very spaciously) by an unknown climber before the modern discovery of the crag.
routes.Location: The second route to the right of the dirty squeeze chimney at the left of the Sun Worshipper Wall. Identifiable by a flattened hanger near the base.

routes.name: Noobs on Parade
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Route climbers right of peach sparrow.

routes.name: Over Easy
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Face climbing to a roof encounter with a perma-draw/chain. 2nd route climbers right of peach sparrow.

routes.name: Peach Sparrow
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Big reaches on bomber holds, hard part is at the chains.

routes.name: Pinball Wizard
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Squeeez Chimney.Also a 5.0 descent before the FA.
routes.Location: Far left of the Sun Worshiper Wall

routes.name: Pyralgina
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Deadpoints and body tension to a good rest. Then pull through a short but exciting finish.
routes.Location: 25m left of Sun Worshipper wall

routes.name: Ra
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Just right of Sun Worshipper. Start off on the large disjointed crack, follow the line through to a mini crack before the anchors.

routes.name: Rise
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: An old deviation of Catch The Sun, taken out by FA.  Listed for historical purposes.

routes.name: Sun Worshipper
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The left of the "easier" slab routes. Climb up and delicately traverse left. A cryptic crux waits here. Figure it out and head into easier climbing and laybacks near the anchors.

routes.name: Sunny Chibas
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Early boulder sequence and a big-ish move leads to easier climbing above
routes.Location: Starts on the first bolt line left of Sun Worshipper. Uses old anchors for Rise

routes.name: Temple of The Sun
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Bolted crack line. little chossy up top.

routes.name: Wild Fire
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Stick clip the first bolt and then unlock the friction friendly boulder problem (physical crux), before a rest where you can now turn your attention to the rest of the route above. From the rest, traverse left to clip the second bolt and then climb straight up through a series of crimpy, technical, and flowy moves before the redpoint crux. Use the mono or don't, dealers choice, then a couple of crimps lead to a big move before traversing left along a disjointed rail. Follow the rail past the second last bolt to a final boulder which leads you back right and to the rings. Shade hits the wall later in the day, which is preferred for all those small holds.

routes.name: Against All Odds
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Steep V-groove climb that looks insane from the ground.
routes.Location: FInd the V groove right of Left hand.

routes.name: Beware the Ginsu
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Awesome face and arete climbing.
