routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Right side of the wall beginning on a ledge part way up.

routes.name: Rumba
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb 20 feet to gain the "A" Dance Ledge. Then continue straight up the middle bolted route (tricky high step to reach the small ledge) and then continue through a bulge (crux) to a small roof.
routes.Location: The middle bolted route on the "A" Dance Ledge. Between the Zoomba Wall and Climb Story
routes.Protection: Bolted to a 2 bolt anchor with rings

routes.name: Sian
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Starts in a crack just left of Shake a Leg. Head right from the ledge higher up, up the stem corner.
routes.Location: Paint #17A

routes.name: Spring Thaw
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the chimney to the large ledge with a cedar. From here either climb the crack or chimney to the top. Can be broken into 2 pitches.
routes.Location: Chimney between Mambo and Samba
routes.Protection: Standard rack. Large cams can be useful.

routes.name: Stupid Tree
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The only route that isn't polished. On the left side of the Zoomba Wall is a nice crack with a tree growing out of it.
routes.Location: Zoomba Wall

routes.name: Tango
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The official start is on a ledge 25ft off the ground. Difficult start leads to sustained climbing on small holds. Be careful when clipping as leaving the ledge.
routes.Location: Located immediately to the right to a grassy gulley. 25ft of easy climbing up from the ground to the ledge. (The start on the ground is to the left of Twist/Two to Tango).From the ledge, there are two hard bolted lines. Tango is the one on the right.

routes.name: Twist
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Shared start with Two to Tango. Keep to the right.

routes.name: Two to Tango
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Shared start with Twist. Keep to the left.

routes.name: Upwardly Mobile
routes.Shared By::
Randy Von Zee
            on Jun 16, 2018

                             · Updates
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: First bolt is on right facing rock, which can be clipped from ledge. Traverse and move up. This route shares a short crux section with Climb Story, but then ventures to the left again. Finish on the black rock just behind the cedar at the top.
routes.Location: On a ledge behind some plants.

routes.name: Winter Carnival
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb chimney system on left side of the Party On Pinnacle.
routes.Location: On a pinnacle located about 100m climber's left of Curved Chimney. The pinnacle is atop a steep hill above the base trail.
routes.Protection: std rack. No anchor at top

routes.name: Zoomba
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Left of Foxtrot. Reachy.

routes.name: The 5.10
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Ok, we do not know the true name of this route, but did meet the first ascensionist.  We named the route after this person's entertaining, never ending inflation of this route's grade, which has made for hilarious conversation.   The route is approximately 30 meters to the right of marker '19.'  8 bolts.  Wrap at the Fixe anchor.  Very well protected and fun to climb.  The last move before the wrap station is most likely more 5.7 if you go direct.  Going left is easier . . .

routes.name: Bam, Pow, Zowie
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Easy climbing to a hard crux at the chains. One of the bolts mid route is quite rusted.
routes.Location: Sport route that the starts up the gully to the left of the long wait.

routes.name: Crazy Doctor
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Steep face climbing.
routes.Location: Left of the south gully of Ivory Tower.

routes.name: The Long Wait
routes.Shared By::
Randy Von Zee
            on Jun 16, 2018

                             · Updates
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Overhanging route on small blocks. Some manteling required, to reach a face above.

routes.name: Zona Rosa
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Run out bolts on the right of the south gully of Ivory Tower.

routes.name: Goose
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This route is located on an open rock face to the left of Omen.  Omen is at yellow mark '26a.'  Climb the obvious bolt line (7) to the Fixe anchor.

routes.name: Irreplaceable
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Follow the line of the crack up to the overhang. Stay to the left for almost the entire climb, often using the arete. Big reaches to bolts.
routes.Location: To the left of #27.

routes.name: Unity
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Bolted route that climbs up the blocky face right if the Ivory Tower feature. An interesting crux at the shallow dihedral leads to easier climbing to the top. Flexibility will help if you're a bit stumped.

routes.name: Access
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A short bolted route going up a series of jugs on an overhang to finish at a ledge with a possible connection to Doible feature (trad). No anchors, lower from the last bolt.
routes.Location: 2m right of paint #31, 5m left of rescue #18

routes.name: Aftershock
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The first sport sport (retro-bolted) route on the Geound Zero Ledge. Climb through the top overhang for full value, or dodge right to avoid the difficulties.

routes.name: Double Feature
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb thin corners left of Access to a bolt/pin belay (5.9R). Or climb Access (5.10a sport) or The Hawk’s Nest (5.6 trad - bottom of Mephisto) to the same belay. The second pitch climbs the 5.8 crack on the right side of the ledge to the Mephisto anchors.

routes.name: Ground Zero
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start by climbing 7 meters up the face to a ledge below a huge corner.  Climb the crack system to a stance beneath an imposing overhanging offwidth/chimney.  Place a large cam and commit; you won't get any more gear until established above the roof.  Once you have surmounted the roof, climb easier wide cracks in the corner to anchors just below the cliff top.  A 60m rope will reach the ground.
routes.Location: The climb starts at Rescue plaque #19
routes.Protection: Trad gear with emphasis on larger pieces.  Doubles of 2-4 camalots would probably be useful.  There are bolted anchors.

routes.name: Mephisto
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Mixed route. From the ground, trad climb 5.6 terrain to the first bolt. Not recommended due to fall potential. Possible to do on TR if set up after climbing Double Feature.
routes.Location: Start left of paint #31
routes.Protection: Mixed route, bolted at too 1/3 of route.

routes.name: The Cat's Tail
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Starts in the bottom of the short chasm at mark 35a. Follows the finger crack through the corner and up to a hand crack. Follow the layback section then up through the flakes before working left. Up through the roof to bolted top anchors. The original route ending went left across the face from here, around the corner to an obvious finish. The top anchors were placed to reduce stress on the cedars.
routes.Protection: The route eats pro.  Small nuts to #3 camalot.

routes.name: The Cat's Whisker
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: up a wide blocky crack ending on a vegetated ledge.
routes.Location: Large blocky crack on the Cat's Tail Wall

routes.name: Dogleg
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start by scrambling up large cracks that form a huge corner to the right of Swan's Song.  Be sure to bring your spider wand (a.k.a. a large stick).  When you reach the huge roof make a hard left, hand traversing on the slimy crack (beware of falling on cams placed in this fissure). At the end of the traverse, head straight up on clean cracks.  Alternatively, if the spiders (or the cracks) look too menacing, you can easily escape onto the upper portion of Swan Song.  Either way, finish at the Swan Song anchors.
routes.Location: The huge corner on the right side of the Cat's Tail area, 20 meters right of Rescue #16.
routes.Protection: Single rack to 4 inches. Nuts are useful.  Long slings are indespensable.

routes.name: The Graduate
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: One of Nemo's best climbs at the grade. This is the bolted line a couple of meters right of Cat's Tail. Climb the face right of the arête, and the face above, until you can get a closer look at the large roof. Take a deep breath. Exit the roof using the obvious crack. Fight the pump to the top.
routes.Location: 43.425825, -79.884084In between 5.9 Cat's Tail and 5.10d Peanut Butter BrothersThe route start where the red # 16 is
routes.Protection: 12 Bolts + AnchorsPermadraw under the roof and fixed anchor carabineers to facilitate cleaning added August 2024

routes.name: Knees and Elbows
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb chimney gully left of Cat's Tail. Option to climb through a hidden cave system.
routes.Location: Right of Ray's Ceiling

routes.name: O. The Cat's Tail | 1040
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Scramble to the top of a pillar near the face. Make the first clip by reaching across the gap from here. There are two variations at the third clip. Heading left is easier than straight up (5.12). Don't fall going for the second bolt.

routes.name: Ray's Ceiling
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb wide crack into a chimney, and rappel off anchors for Female Belay.
routes.Location: Right of Old Route.

routes.name: Route 66
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start in the chimney behind the pillar. Shares the first few draws with Route 69, but takes the right trending line through steeper terrain. Some big moves lead to more techy face climbing.
routes.Location: To the left of Female Belay Slave behind the pillar.

routes.name: Route 77
routes.Shared By::
Randy Von Zee
            on Jul 3, 2018

                             · Updates
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Bolted by Karl in his 77th year, a nice (5.10b) branching off of Route 66 (5.10d). Start by scrambling up easy 3rd class terrain between the pillar and the wall to a bolt. Climb up Route 66 until you reach the third or fourth bolt and start traversing out left (a couple of long draws will help with rope drag). Work your way out of the traverse and climb through the right side of the roof. Finish on easier terrain to the anchors.
routes.Protection: 9 bolts. Don't fall while the pillar is behind you.
routes.Location: This route starts just to the left of a prominent 30 foot tall pillar, about 50 meters left of the Graduate.

routes.name: Second Agreement
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start just to the right of The Big F.  Ascend the crack that tends to the right side of the Big F's roof.  Continue up and right over seemingly blank terrain to the Fixe Anchors.

routes.name: Stuck in the Middle
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start as if doing the Big F. At the overhanging pillar, step left and ascend the offwidth crack to the ledge with a tree. Pro to #4. From this point you can ascend to the cliff top. Alternatively, you can traverse right over easy terrain to the Fixe anchors atop Second Agreement.

routes.name: Swan Song
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Some tricky moves up to a ledge below a small overhang. Pull the overhang and continue on good holds and through a crimpy crux to the top.

routes.name: Carnal Sin
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb decent rock to the first large ledge. Head up and left, pulling on a couple of overhanging blocks to reach the first crack, which can be protected with a BD #4; this crack varies in size but is generally BD #3 and #4. Pull the moves through the crack and get situated beneath the final overhanging and steep hand crack.The hand crack provides excellent jams in the BD #2 range. Pull the roof and fight the pump as the upper crack narrows to BD #1. Finish on the anchors (bolts with rap rings).The upper roof crack is an incredible 4-star hand crack.
routes.Location: Look for the roof with a hand crack to the left of the birch tree on the ledge. See the beta photo.

routes.name: High Society
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Gear at the bottom, bolts up top, anchor. Starts on easy terrain with plenty of pro and gets harder beginning at the slanted crack under the roof. Exit right to a small ledge and the first bolt. Continue up to the anchors on bolts. Bring an alpine draw for your last piece under the roof, and perhaps a somewhat longer sport draw to minimize rope drag at one bolt
routes.Location: At the right side of Morphine Wall.
routes.Protection: Standard rack; a 0.5 cam will protect the roof, along with an alpine (120cm) draw. Sport draws for the upper section, though a single longer one partway up will prevent the rope from running over a small edge.

routes.name: Long Reach
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: At the left end of Morphine Ledge Wall is  is chute.  This prominent line of weakness starts through broken terrain and tends in a generally left direction over broken terrain.  The lower section of the route can be a bit spooky; test the holds before using them.  However, half way up, the route gives way to solid terrain.  You will top out at a ledge just below the cliff top.  Belay here.  Rappelling from where you belayed your partner up is problematic, as it is a less than optimal wrap location.  To depart the route are two options: 1) ascend the obvious line of weakness to the cliff top; 2) down climb to the ledge one meter below where you belayed and rappel at the wrap station.

routes.name: Reunion
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: At Morphine are two prominent roofs.  Bisecting these two roofs is a book fold.  At the base is a yellow '39.'  This is your route.  Ascend the face.  Pull the crux move and continue up to the book fold and step left to the ledge with the large cedar tree.  From here you wrap.  An alternative is to continue up the roof through the crack, which is 5.10.  This is a very well protected route.  It is very much a Seneca style 5.7, so expect technical climbing and committing moves.

routes.name: Reunion Direct
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Begin on Reunion, climbing up loose rock to a large ledge. Make your way up the offwidth (protects well with a BD #4) between the two roofs of Sister Morphine and Carnal Sin. Trend up and climbers right beneath the first large roof (protects well with a BD #5), then traverse left under the roof to another large ledge. From here, head straight up to the steep hand crack at the top of the cliff.The route's crux is the overhanging hand crack at the top of the cliff, which is well protected by multiple BD #3s. Pull the small roof and climb the rest of the crack to the anchors (bolts with rap rings).Reunion Direct is rated 5.9 on rakkup, but I thought that attempting the crux roof moves onsight is at least 5.10-.A 60-meter rope barely reaches the ground from the anchors at the top.
routes.Location: Located on the Morphine Ledge.

routes.name: Sister Morphine
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A beautiful and inspiring route. The roof is intimidating but the difficult section is short. Scramble up pitch 1 (5.7) to the Morphine Ledge. Climb a finger crack to the roof with a fixed nut in the corner. A 10' hand crack in the roof gives way to a vertical off-width before a short finish to the anchor
routes.Location: The obvious giant roof with the hand crack
routes.Protection: Cams from .4-3", fixed nut in corner of roof at the top of the finger crack. Rings at the anchor

routes.name: Trajection
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb decent rock to the first large ledge beneath the flakes and the right of the birch tree. Ascend up and right on good gear, then to the left on the large flakes. Layback and jam the flakes trending left until you are in line with the Carnal Sin anchors. Traverse left until you are slightly above the Carnal Sin anchors. I thought the physical crux of the route was the last of the large flakes, while the mental crux was the leftward traverse after finishing the flakes.There are a couple of options to finish the route. Regardless of your finish, protect the traverse after the flakes for your follower.Downclimb a few moves and end on the Carnal Sin anchors. If you choose this option, note that your follower will be above the anchors and on lead at some point.Continue climbing up and left from the top of the Carnal Sin anchors and end on a tree slung with a couple of rap rings.
routes.Location: Look for the large flakes to the right of Carnal Sin (see beta photo).

routes.name: The Camel
routes.FA:: Helmut Microys, Michael Rosenberger, Francois Garneau (1963/1964)
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: In the middle of the Camel Area is a left facing series of features (flakes and small corner up a very solid line of rock. Climb around the small overhanging features. Ascend to a ledge. Climbing the slightly overhanging line to the wrap station (three bolts).Three bolts (glue in) plus rings.

routes.name: Double Lead
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: To the left of the prominent arch that constitutes Harlequin is a series of large and blocky right facing features. It looks downright ugly, but looks deceive. Climb through these left facing features, tending left. As you approach the cliff top, traverse right to the Fixe anchors. This is a good introductory trad route that offers good practice in rope management over relatively well protected terrain. Make sure you have a follower to clean, retrieving your last piece before the traverse to anchors is very difficult on rappel

routes.name: Grim Thin Flake
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A retro-bolted old-school trad route. The crux is right at the beginning, protected by two well placed bolts. Angle slight left to obvious flake. Follow to anchors.
routes.Location: About 10m right of Camel, where a thin, steep crack is protected with two bolts.

routes.name: Harlequin
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: In the Camel area there is a prominent buttress of which has two prominent roofs. The start of the route ascends the slab just left of the lower roof. Climb up and right towards the lower roof. Stay left of lower roof and aim for the left facing corner that leads up to the higher roof. Climb left around the higher roof. Traverse left under the cedar. Climb up onto the ledge where the wrap station resides. This is a technical, sustained 5.7.

routes.name: Jay
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Starts at the broken up section of wall 6m right of Camel. Climb this to the crack above which leads to the same ledge as Camel. To finish continue up and left (5.3) and build an anchor from the exposed rock, or take the Camel crack (5.7) to the Camel  anchors.

routes.name: Old Breed
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Originally climbed on gear, it was bolted in 2012.

routes.name: Positron
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This route resides on the north end of the Camel Wall. It is just left of J-Bean, which is marked by a yellow, '47.' At the start is a slight bulge. This connects to a very clean, left arching flake. This flake terminates at a roof. The original version of this route (as I was told) continued right of this roof, through easier climbing. However, a more fun (and 5.9) variation is to ascend the crack in the roof and continue up the face past two bolts to the fixed anchor. Excellent route!

routes.name: Reg's Route
routes.Shared By::
Randy Von Zee
            on Jun 16, 2018

                             · Updates
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on bolts left of double lead, but then crosses over it.

routes.name: Thunder Road
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Immediately to the left of Camel is a shallow, left facing flake (quite solid) whose crack gradually enlarges as it moves up the face. Ascend this flake system to a bulge (crack will continue up and right), which is to the left. Pull the bulge, which has no natural protection, but has a bolt. Atop the bulge a crack emerges and ascends up a shallow, right facing corner. This crack eats pro. Climb this to a resting stance. Gain the ledge to the wrap station. Note: someone denigrated this route by bolting it in its entirety. The route is truly a mixed route, with the only required bolt being the one at the bulge.
routes.Protection: Mixed.  Please be an honorable climber: use pro.

routes.name: Bei Tageslicht
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on flakes and move up over the crux buldge using a hidden hold to an overhang.
routes.Location: The bolted route left of Ein Kleines Nacht Klettern.

routes.name: Big Sky
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb flakes and overhangs to the top.

routes.name: Butterfingers
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Starts at marker #49. Ascend the varied terrain up to a crack that sits in a right facing corner. Continue up the incipient crack to the roof. Pull the roof. Fixed wrap station. An alternate 5.6 variation: same start. Ascend the arching left facing corner (is immediately to the right of the right facing corner. The arching crack and right facing corner converge 1/2 way up the route. Traverse left at the incipient crack. Gain the ledge to the left. Ascend to the 2nd ledge and traverse right to the wrap station. Both variations are quite good.

routes.name: Ein Kleines Nacht Klettern
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This is a short, obvious crack where The Big Gulp begins and often has plenty of climbers practicing on it.
routes.Location: Beside easy way down.
routes.Protection: Gear required. Anchors at the top.

routes.name: Four No More
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start at Palm Sunday but climb up and left of the shallow cave to reach some face climbing.
routes.Location: Paint #50

routes.name: The Head Mucky Mucky
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb up blocks to a big ledge then continue up the easy crack. Easy, blocky climbing.
routes.Location: Right beside the Penguin Hatch gully.

routes.name: Judy's on the Drug Squad
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Ascend the blocky face to a right facing flake that grows into a corner.  At the corner's terminus, ascend up and right through the bulge to easier climbing.
routes.Location: The route is at the right-most end of the Big Gulp area.  There is nearly always a party camped here, so you cannot miss it.
routes.Protection: This was once a trad route, but has recently been re-bolted with anchors.

routes.name: Madames Deconvience
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Clip the bolt under the roof and step left then head up. Use a long runner on the first bolt.

routes.name: Palm Sunday
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The route is located on a semi-independent formation to the right of Butterfingers. This formation is slightly uphill of all surrounding cliff. Ascend and move right to a shallow, left leaning ramp, which is located on the middle of the face. Continue up the steep terrain to the rap station.

routes.name: Peanut Butter Punch
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Unknown route between Bei Tageslicht and Trauma Belay.   A bit dirty with some loose rock and crux up high.

routes.name: The Penguin Hatch
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: An awkward move is needed to get into the crevice, which traverses behind the cliff to the exit up near "Wormhole".
routes.Location: About 150m climbers left of the "easy way down" is the entrance to a long crevice cave called Penguin Hatch.
routes.Protection: not worth placing protection either solo it or don't climb it

routes.name: Proton
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb solid flakes right of Butterfingers ending at the top of Butterfingers.
routes.Location: Start on Butterfingers

routes.name: Red Line Fever
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Brilliant sustained route. Climb the left edge before stepping right into the crack and harder climbing above.

routes.name: Sidewinder
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Shared start with Judy's. Head left after the third bolt.

routes.name: Trauma Belay
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Retro-bolted route going up blocks to short slab up top.
routes.Location: Left of Bei Tageslicht

routes.name: Wasp In Space
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Follow the bolts to the anchor above.
routes.Location: Left of Madames Deconvenience.

routes.name: Wormhole
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Chimney climb, squeeze under the stuck boulder.
routes.Location: Left of Red Line Fever.

routes.name: Yellow Streak
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Choose your own adventure up through the gully.
routes.Location: Start on a ledge just a little further up from Four No More.

routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start in the center. Move up straight through the middle of the boulder to finish on the horn.
routes.Location: In the middle on the Discovery Boulder.

routes.name: Blue Dye #69
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start on the arete and continue up through the blocky features and top out.
routes.Location: Around the corner from Red Dye #5 on the same boulder.

routes.name: Diablo
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start on the side of the boulder using the good holds right on the edge of the overhang. Move on the small feet and pockets out right onto the main face. Continue up and right on some good pockets and finish on the horn.
routes.Location: To the left of 1492 on the Discovery Boulder

routes.name: Discman
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the jug on left of the overhang, work up the edges and pockets to the right with a top out using the mega jug on the left edge.
routes.Location: Follow metal stairs to bizarro world and then follow the bouldering trail markers to the end where there is a large open area, the boulder is right in the middle.

routes.name: Discman v3
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the Jug to the left side of the overhang. Work up to the edges and pockets before topping out left centre using the left mega jug.
routes.Location: Enter bizzaro world using the black metal stairs and head down the bouldering trail following the Markers. at the end of the bouldering trail there is a clearing and the boulder is in the middle of the open

routes.name: Gettin' Juggy Wit It
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: climb up the flakes to the same finish as Sabishii
routes.Location: sabishii boulder

routes.name: I Am Mango!
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the pinch and small horn and work your way up the edges until top out.
routes.Location: Head left to the stone stairs from the entrance and turn left again at the 3 path fork into bizzarro world. head down the orange marked bouldering path and the boulder will be on the left at the end of the bouldering trail where the space opens up.

routes.name: Litchen It
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start on the arete and climb the arete to the top. Exit on the right side near the top and top out. Fun climbing on good holds.

routes.name: Nogoko
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on obvious good hand holds. Catch potentially massive swing and climb through better holds to top.Note: dangerous landing for move that can be low percentage. Try not to drop kick spotter.

routes.name: Pocket Protector
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the flake arete, one arete layer to the left of getting juggy wit it. downclimb recommended to avoid moss on your shoes
routes.Location: sabishii boulder

routes.name: Red Dye #5
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Stand start in the middle of the face. Reach up to good holds and stand up.

routes.name: Rungs
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: start on the left side and climb up chalky pockets and flats onto a mossy top-out

routes.name: Sibishi
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Low start on a crappy left pocket and right undercling. Some use the right hand awkward undercling (with poor feet) to move the left hand to the next crimp. I pull off the left hand pocket and go straight up with the right to match. Another hard deadpoint to the final left hand crimp just before the jugs awaits. Harder variations exist.
routes.Location: Somewhere near Bizarro world beyond the second set of stairs. Kind of a damp area, can stay wet for a while.
routes.Protection: two pads should be fine, maybe even one if you are feeling strong.

routes.name: Unknown
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start with the two side pulls. Step up to the unique pockets and continue up on the face to the left and top out.
routes.Location: Climbs the face to the right of Litchen It.

routes.name: Yesgoko
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The most common beta using the crack out left and a heel toe cam sets this bloc as a solid V4/6b
routes.Location: Kokoro Boulder

routes.name: Ababa
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the huge flakes up the middle of the boulder and top out.
routes.Location: Last boulder in Central, right side.

routes.name: The Arrow
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start under the small roof, climb up onto the face and top out.
routes.Location: Danzig, east side.

routes.name: Back to B.K.S.
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start on two good sidepulls and climb up the prow.
routes.Location: Lotus

routes.name: Band Saw
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start 3ft left of Log Jam with left hand on a sharp crimp at head height and right hand on the sidepull below the 2 finger pocket. Go up and left.
routes.Location: Saw Mill, the side facing Cal's

routes.name: Black Eagle
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start as The Phoenix and move out left to finish as The Crucifix.
routes.Location: Captain Hook, east side

routes.name: Blue Heron, Left
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the face left of the flake and climb straight up.
routes.Location: Blue Heron boulder

routes.name: Bolero
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the big ledge and move around the arete to top out. Bad landing.
routes.Location: 2nd problem, climber's right of stairs

routes.name: Cal's Problem
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the right leaning crack to an undercling flake, then up pockets to top out.
routes.Location: Cal's, on the side facing Saw Mill

routes.name: Captain Hook
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start with heel hooks and go left and up.
routes.Location: Captain Hook, northeast side.

routes.name: Celebrex
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the arête and move out under the steep section.
routes.Location: The "O", southeast arete.

routes.name: Contact
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start using the sloper on Life O'Rielly and a left hand crimp. Go up through a sloper and a small edge to a jug to the left.
routes.Location: Danzig, north

routes.name: Cream Cheese
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start in the hole and move up to a triangular flake, topping out to its left. The arêtes are out to keep it V4.
routes.Location: Bagel boulder, right of Danzig.

routes.name: The Crucifix
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start near the left side of the roof on two underclings. Using crimps and flat holds go out and right to a pocket at the lip and top out left of The Phoenix.
routes.Location: Captain Hook boulder, east side

routes.name: Dumb and Dumber
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start on a sloper on the arête at waist height. Traverse right along a series of flakes to the end of the boulder.
routes.Location: Cal's, on the side facing Saw Mill.

routes.name: Dynamo
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start sitting on a flat rock under the arête, using an undercling and a sidepull. Go up and right.
routes.Location: West of the Bagel boulder.

routes.name: The Dynosaur
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start in the middle of the face, move up and right to the blocky hold on the arête.
routes.Location: West of the Bagel boulder.

routes.name: Elevated Bloc (The Flesh Prince)
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start to the right of the crack on two underclings. Climb up and over the bulge to top out using big jugs.
routes.Location: Saw Mill, south side

routes.name: Frosted Flake
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Stand start at the small flake on the arete, traverse up left and across the massive ledge to the end and top out.

routes.name: Hook Direct
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start under the roof on the block, on side pulls, then move out to join Captain Hook.
routes.Location: Captain Hook boulder, northeast

routes.name: Josh's Highball
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start below Cal's Problem, traverse up and right to the horn near the arête then up.
routes.Location: Below Cal's

routes.name: Life O'Reilly
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Along with Super Sharp Shooter, this line is probably the best V6(+) you'll find anywhere in Ontario but be warned - the feet are polished to the point of nearly being useless. Start on some crimps about chest high and throw left hand straight up to a sloper using some horrible feet. Using one of a variety of equally bad foot options, pop up and right to a sloping crimp (crux) and control your swing as your foot will probably slip off. Now move left hand to a quarter inch bite-size crimp and get your feet up high - a final huge dyno gaurds the finish jugs. Four stars!
routes.Location: In the central area next to the V2 classic problem. Please see Dr. Topo or other guidebook:   topout.org/static/pdf/Niaga…
routes.Protection: A couple pads should be fine

routes.name: Log Jam
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on opposing sidepulls near the right corner of the face. Move up and right to finish around the corner on the slab.
routes.Location: Saw Mill, the side facing Cal's

routes.name: Lox
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the right arete at the mouth of the gap.

routes.name: Monkey Sweat Unlimited
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start low on the left side of the overhang using two small pockets. Move up through a sloper to gain the good horn
routes.Location: South of the Pear Boulder

routes.name: My Other Brother Herbie
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the handlebar shaped holds and traverse around the boulders.
routes.Location: South side of a boulder on your left as you approach Captain Hook from Saw Mill/Cal's

routes.name: No U Turn
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the vertical seam through the small v-notch and then right to top out.
routes.Location: Danzig, north side

routes.name: The Phoenix
routes.Shared By::
Randy Von Zee
            on Jul 22, 2018

                             · Updates
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start in the middle of the roof matched on a left facing rail. Move out and right to top out left of Captain Hook.
routes.Location: Captain Hook boulder, east side.

routes.name: Pinch a Loaf
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start just right of the wide crack with left hand on a large pinch and right hand on a crimp. Go straight up.
routes.Location: Left end of Saw Mill, on the path to Bizarro World.

routes.name: Pistol Pete
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start far back in the cave and climb out the roof and up.
routes.Location: Underhang, towards the Old Country.

routes.name: Plumber's Crack
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start on the ledge and climb the crack to a top out that can be awkward if it's sandy.
routes.Location: Big crack in middle of boulder.

routes.name: Pot Hole
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: start on the same jug-rail as No U-turn, climb up to the V-notch, then head left on slopers and crimps and finish on the same jug as Shocker.
routes.Location: No U-turn boulder

routes.name: Pot Mouth
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start on the rail at head height climb straight up.
routes.Location: Pear boulder

routes.name: Shocker
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start left of No U Turn using a triangular crimp and pocket. Move straight up through a sloper.
routes.Location: Danzig, left end of the north side.

routes.name: Slow Frowns in Brown Town
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the blocky holds left of the arête facing Electric French Fries and go up.
routes.Location: Captain Hook boulder

routes.name: Tales of Power
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start low, to the left of Cal's Problem. Move up and left using small pockets to a larger pocket on the arête. Top out on the slab.
routes.Location: Cal's, east side.

routes.name: Tantric Shocker
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the left arête using a pocket at waist height. Move up and then right to finish on Shocker. Variation V9 - finish on Felcher.
routes.Location: Danzig boulder, northeast arete.

routes.name: Think Positive
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start as Shocker and then move right to the sloper on, and finish as, No U Turn.
routes.Location: Danzig, north side.

routes.name: Achillis' Heel
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start on large holds under the right hand side of the roof. Move straight out through the overhang and top out.
routes.Location: Balancing rock, on the side facing Lactic

routes.name: Action Arete
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on small pockets left of the arête. Move up to the left side pull and small pocket and then move right to the ledge. Large pockets to the left and right are out.
routes.Location: Left of the side of Caboose facing Balancing Rock.

routes.name: All Aboard
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start near the right arête on small pockets and traverse the face without using the jugs on top.
routes.Location: Engine boulder

routes.name: Awe
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on low holds, move to the ledge and then to the pocket above.
routes.Location: Riddler, north side

routes.name: Battle Royale
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the arete, traverse left and finish up Harold.

routes.name: Bottlerocket
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the sharp plate. Move up on slopers to a good pocket.
routes.Location: Balancing Rock

routes.name: Brave New World
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start on two sidepulls left of Death Trap’s finish horn. Go up to a sloper and then turn the lip to a big horn near the tree.
routes.Location: Lactic Acid east side.

routes.name: Conundrum
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the far right side and traverse left to the corner.
routes.Location: Riddler

routes.name: Copycat
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start in the middle of the face, move up to the “doorknob” and climb up through pockets.
routes.Location: Lactic Acid west

routes.name: Cyanide
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start and climb up the overhanging jugs to the top.

routes.name: Dear Balancing Rock
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start just right of Paris’ Weakness on a small slot-pocket. Move up and left around the corner.
routes.Location: Balancing Rock, northeast

routes.name: Death Trap
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start near the cliff edge on a slot-pocket and block. Move up and right to an undercling and crimp. Trend right until you can top out at the horn on the lip.
routes.Location: Lactic acid, on the east side near the cliff edge.

routes.name: Diamond in the Rough
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start in the middle of the face and traverse left around the lip of the roof. Finish on large jugs after the second corner.
routes.Location: Balancing Rock, east side.

routes.name: Doors of Perception
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Right exit of Brave New World
routes.Location: Jump straight to jug from left hand pinch and right hand side pull

routes.name: Dung Beetle
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the jug on the corner. Traverse left along the lip all the way to Arete and top out.
routes.Location: On smaller rock between Fly Wall III and Balancing Rock. Start on the side facing Balancing Rock.

routes.name: God Must Be a Climber
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the obvious flake, traverse left on slopers to the corner and top out.
routes.Location: Heaven, east side on a flake

routes.name: Happiness
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Another juggy route with a little more movement than its neighbour (Cyanide).

routes.name: Harold
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Take the most obvious line up the middle of the boulder and to out.

routes.name: Hector Toehook
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start as Achilles' Heel, but move right at the second jug to slopers and then a triangular pinch.
routes.Location: Balancing Rock

routes.name: Horshack's Last Dance
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start at the sidepull and two finger pocket, staying right of the pothole. Climb up small pockets to the top, making use of the giant dish.
routes.Location: Toto, west

routes.name: Hueco
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: climb the face through several huecos
routes.Location: around the right corner of knee Bar

routes.name: Hueco
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the high, large heucos and climb the face.
routes.Location: Kneebar boulder, near the walk to Waterworld.

routes.name: The Hunter
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start under the right arête on a diagonal rail, then move up the right hand face tothe large ledge.
routes.Location: Lactic Acid north and west sides

routes.name: Husband and Lies
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the left side corner of the boulder and come up and traverse to the right, finishing on the same finish hold as Wicked Witch of the West
routes.Location: Toto, south side

routes.name: Immaculate
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the rail at chest height, move up to pockets with your left hand and use a crimp on your right. Make a bit of a throw to the top and mantel out.
routes.Location: Immaculate boulder

routes.name: Immaculate Contraception
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start as Immaculate. From the pockets, move right to the arête and climb up.
routes.Location: Immaculate boulder

routes.name: Invisible Woman
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb up good crimps and edges left of the arete to top out.

routes.name: Jim's Arete
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Across from Knee Bar is a small boulder. sit start on the arete and top out.
routes.Location: Across from Knee Bar

routes.name: Knee Scum
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: climb the jug column up the right arete of knee bar
routes.Location: right arete of Knee Bar

routes.name: Loose Caboose
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the arête using a pinch and pocket and traverse the entire face.
routes.Location: Start on the right side of Caboose and head left.

routes.name: Monkeyfist
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: With back to the river, sit start, climb up the face
routes.Location: back to the river

routes.name: Open Forum
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start with right hand on an undercling and left on a pinch. Heelhook with left and slap up to a slopey dish. Throw up again to a big pocket.
routes.Location: Toothpick

routes.name: Pocket Traverse
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on pockets near the right corner. Traverse left on pockets up to the vertical crack and jug, and top out.
routes.Location: Balancing Rock

routes.name: Previously Tapped
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start just left of Girls of Cheektowaga with a left-handed gaston. Move up to a pocket and top out.
routes.Location: Balancing Rock, north

routes.name: Pump Traverse
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on two left facing sidepulls. Move up and left to finish on the big horn.
routes.Location: Lactic Acid North

routes.name: Quantum Liasons
routes.Shared By::
Randy Von Zee
            on Jul 19, 2018

                             · Updates
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start right of Copycat with right hand in a hidden pocket and left hand on block. Climb the face.
routes.Location: Lactic Acid west

routes.name: Right (Toronto)
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the large block (careful! it moves) and climb up on good pockets.
routes.Location: Toronto, west side

routes.name: Right of Standard
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the obvious line in the center of the face, moving up crimps, sidepulls, and a pinch. Don't fall.
routes.Location: Fly Wall III

routes.name: Root Canal
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start on underclings, move up to a sidepull and to a pocket out right.
routes.Location: Right end of toothpick boulder, near where another rock is adjacent to it.

routes.name: Royale with Cheese
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start left of Achilles Heel and move out diagonally through edges, underclings, and pinches to the lip. Finish on obvious jugs.
routes.Location: Balancing Rock, on the side towards Lactic Acid / the cliff.

routes.name: Rubberband
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the ledge and sloper pocket and traverse right past the large flake to the ledge above the start of Root Canal
routes.Location: Left end of Toothpick's problems, near where Fly Wall III is behind you.

routes.name: Sexual Inadequacy
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start under the right arête on a diagonal rail, then head straight up the arete.
routes.Location: Lactic Acid north side

routes.name: Sharknado
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the left arete and face of the boulder to top out.

routes.name: Snake Eyes
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start under the right arête on a diagonal rail. Move left through a large pocket, to the slopers and then up the arête.
routes.Location: Lactic Acid North side

routes.name: Somthin' For Nothin'
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start in the middle of the overhang on a crystalized pocket. Traverse left on good edges and move to a jug up and left
routes.Location: Lactic Acid North

routes.name: Stretch Armstrong's Summer Vacation
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on edges just left of Winter Retreat and climb straight up on pockets. Crux may be moving off of the pockets on the main face.
routes.Location: Toothpick

routes.name: Thunder and Lightening
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start on pockets on the right side of the face. Move up to slopers and then right to an edge.

routes.name: Tornado
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the large undercling and climb straight up.
routes.Location: Toto, south side

routes.name: Tractorbeam Traverse
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the far left and traverse the lip around the corner to the slot and two finger pocket and top out.
routes.Location: Saucer, traverse across the north side

routes.name: The V Traverse
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Traverse the line of pockets from left to right around the corner and onto the face, then up to the top ledge.
routes.Location: V Boulder, near the upper part of Romper Room

routes.name: Variance
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start on a high pocket and the left arete, wrestle your way to the top.
routes.Location: The V Boulder

routes.name: Voldemort
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes: White Monde, we redefine interior spaces into captivating realms of beauty and functionality. As one of the premier interior fit-out companies in Dubai, we specialize in transforming concepts into reality, blending creativity with meticulous craftsmanship to craft spaces that inspire
routes.Description: Start on the ledge, climb through the large side pull rail, up to a big pocket, and top out.
routes.Location: The V Boulder

routes.name: Whitewall
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: climb the face using the slanting rail and top out
routes.Location: around the left corner of knee bar

routes.name: Wicked Witch of the West
routes.Shared By::
Randy Von Zee
            on Jul 19, 2018

                             · Updates
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start as Tornado on two underclings and move left and up the clean face to the sloper pocket to finish.
routes.Location: Toto, south side

routes.name: Winter Retreat
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start just left of Root Canal with left on a spike and right on a sidepull. Move left to the large flake and then to the top.
routes.Location: Toothpick

routes.name: The Witchita Express
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start in the middle of the face on sidepulls. Move up to a pinch and top out on jugs.
routes.Location: Engine Boulder, the side facing Dining Car.

routes.name: Fubar
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: start on the left side of the milestones boulder. Climb up to under the roof and traverse right along the line of weakness. Officially ends where Snafu V4 ends but you can keep going until it doesn't feel V2 anymore.

routes.name: Milestones
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: sit start down low on obvious chalky jugs. Fire up to the knobby left hold and crimp and slap your way up the slopers or so to the peak of the arete. Round the arete and downclimb

routes.name: Pawn Shop
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: sit start with hands spread on left and right 45degree angled crimps. Move up to the sloper-jugs and finish the same as Milestone

routes.name: Slab du la Crimp
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: establish on the slabby face. I recommend starting on the lowest crimps you can for maximum value. Tic-tac your way up to the lip and top out and downclimb behind. Quite easy for the grade.
routes.Location: standalone boulder around to the left and 5meters behind the Milestones boulder

routes.name: Stylin'
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: sit start on rightmost and lowermost flakes on the milestone boulder. pull straight vertically up through some good flat edges to the arete and top out. I recommend keeping the actual arete off until the last few moves for maximum V2 fun, but there's plenty of chalk on the right arete the whole way up which makes it feel easier

routes.name: 5K Runningman
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start as Runningman. Instead of going through the first hole, go through the hole on the right side. Come back down the first hole and finish with matching on the right pocket.
routes.Location: Georger

routes.name: Adam's Virginity
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start under the roof with right hand on a rail on the right side of the prow and left under the roof. Turn the lip and top out.
routes.Location: Superfly

routes.name: Afterthought
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start sitting in the back of the cave. Move out to the lip and then up and right to top out.
routes.Location: Cleanup boulder, left-most of the side opposite the trail to central.

routes.name: Afterthought
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Stand start on the shelf jug, then move straight up and trend left at the flake to top out.

routes.name: Arete Love
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start. Grab incuts and smear on a polished notch. Climb the arête and step off on the low boulder.
routes.Location: Georger, southwest

routes.name: The Bad Kind Of Cheese
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Stand start and climb through pockets and jugs to top out using the arete and face.

routes.name: Blow Chicks Clothes Off
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on two sidepulls at chest height. Move up to a jug and block and then top out on jugs. (Sit start is V4).
routes.Location: Disalvo, left end of north side.

routes.name: Cool
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start low on a pocket and edge. Move up on edges staying left of the arête.
routes.Location: Right across from Toad.

routes.name: Courage, My Love
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes: 33win – Cổng Game Đẳng Cấp Với Những Cơ Hội Thắng Lớn mang đến cho người chơi những trải nghiệm cá cược  Website:  authenticafghanistan.com/
routes.Description: Climb the left side of the Fortune Favours the Brave boulder.
routes.Location: Left of Fortune Favours the Brave.

routes.name: Crack Fiend
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the obvious ledge, move to the dihedral crack and top out.

routes.name: Darlene
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start on the right facing rail and go up to a good edge and the left to finish on Pica.
routes.Location: Georger

routes.name: Delicatessen
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start in the cave between the two boulders on two sidepulls. Traverse right to the large undercling.
routes.Location: Georger

routes.name: Energy Crisis
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Link Delicatessen and Pica.
routes.Location: Georger

routes.name: Epiphany
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start underneath the massive roof on a large chalky jug. Climb straight out the roof on edges, pockets and pinches. Turn the lip on good holds and top out.
routes.Location: A boulder out to the right from the main path through Upper Old Country towards Central.

routes.name: Exemplify
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start on the left arete, move up to a good sidepull and then to the peak.
routes.Location: X, Left/south side.

routes.name: Exlax
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start in a dihedral. Climb up through the open book.
routes.Location: X

routes.name: Eyes Lay Waste
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the left end of the overhang using a sidepull on the face and the rounded lip. Climb up and right to the mail slot at the peak. Top out right or downclimb the tree.
routes.Location: Cleanup

routes.name: The Flasher
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start on a pocket and crimp and throw to the to the sloping edge.

routes.name: Floryshe
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on a curved ledge at chest height. Go up through sidepulls to a triangular hold and move left to the top.
routes.Location: X

routes.name: Floryshe Left
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start as Floryshe, but at the triangular hold near the top go far left to the finishing jugs of Love of My Life.
routes.Location: X

routes.name: Foot Fetish
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb up the right hand side of the face, using sidepulls and crimps to top out. The hardest part should be getting the last hold before the lip.
routes.Location: Superfly wall, right/north end.

routes.name: Fortune Favours The Brave
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb large edges up the middle of the boulder.
routes.Location: Southeast of the flywall, on a tall boulder in a narrow gully.

routes.name: Fred the dogg
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: SDS matching on the crimp. Move left using the side pull then follow "Walk the Dog" to the finishing jug.
routes.Location: Right of windy city

routes.name: Georger, Right Arete
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start on the undercling and climb up small pockets and edges staying off the arête.
routes.Location: Georger

routes.name: Girl Scout
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start in the cave between the two boulders on a juggy pinch. Move right through a sloping pocket to a good pocket on the right.

routes.name: Gulag (Fart)
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start just right of the arete on a crimp and sidepull. Move up to a pocket and sloper and insecure top out.
routes.Location: X

routes.name: Hey LG!
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the jug at the end of the overhang and go up.
routes.Location: Spider's Web boulder

routes.name: Highstep Arete
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start on underclings on the blunt arête. Move up to a jug on the right and pockets on the arête.
routes.Location: Spider's Web, south side

routes.name: Hoisin
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Stand start right of the overhanging bulge. Follow the crack over the bulge and climb up the slabby face.

routes.name: Jason 5
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start with right on a two finger pocket just left of the flake and left on a low pocket. Move up staying left of the flake.
routes.Location: Toad

routes.name: Juicer
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Choose from lots of holds and traverse the slightly overhanging face or climb to the top.

routes.name: K8
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start 1 m left of K9 Track Suit with left hand on a large side pull and right on a low flake. Move up on crimps and sloper to top out just left of K9

routes.name: K9 Track Suit
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start with a left hand crimp and an undercling. Follow slopers over the bulge to gain the slab.

routes.name: Keep it on the Down Low
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start in cave between the two boulders with right hand on sloper and left on low sloping edge. Move up and right using positive crimps and top out on the slab.

routes.name: Logger Arete
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start as Super Sharp Shooter. Move up to the good edge and then go left to the arête. A deep mono may help you gain the top.

routes.name: Love of my Life (Cornelia)
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start as Florsyshe. At the sidepulls traverse right into the upper section of Mangler.
routes.Location: X

routes.name: Mangler
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start matched below the peak of the inverted V. Climb up on crimps to the peak of the V and then up the flake system.
routes.Location: X boulder

routes.name: Megaman
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start with both hands on the undercling and reach for the slot with your right hand. The problem finishes at the jug three-quarters up the boulder.
routes.Location: Between Arete love and ripple direct

routes.name: Mirin
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Stand start as Dashi on a slanting ledge and follow the flaky system up to a highball top out.

routes.name: The Mural Traverse
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the far right side and traverse low on small holds to the junction of the two boulders.

routes.name: My Upside Down Milkshake
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start just outside the cave on a large pocket. Move up left to the pothole and top out through the hole.
routes.Location: Georger, on the side towards Upper Old Country

routes.name: Nest Egg
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the pothole, traverse left to the arête and then climb up.
routes.Location: Forgotten boulder, north side

routes.name: No Time for Sittin' Around
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the black flake and climb straight up on pockets and sidepulls.
routes.Location: X Boulder, right/north end.

routes.name: One Inch Punch
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start on the lower right side of the inverted V. Move left up the feature to its peak and then finish on No Time For Sitting Around.
routes.Location: X boulder

routes.name: Pete's Pinch
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start near the right arête on a micro-edge and pocket. Climb through the side-pull and two underclings to the top.

routes.name: Pica
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start matched on a large undercling on the right side of the cave (end of Delicatessen). Traverse right around the corner and follow seams to the top. Move right around the corner, follow the seam to the top.
routes.Location: Georger

routes.name: Pocket Rocket
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start on low pockets right of the flake. Move up through a slopey pocket to the top.
routes.Location: Toad, right

routes.name: Procreation
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start with left hand in the pocket in the pothole and right hand on the lip. Heel hook the lip and go up for the large block. Finish on the jug at the peak and step off on the lower boulder.
routes.Location: Georger

routes.name: Rail Slide
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb pockets to the long vertical slots on top.

routes.name: Ripple
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the right arete on the “rippled pocket”. Follow the lip and edges to the top.
routes.Location: Georger

routes.name: Ripple direct
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start with your left hand in the narrow undercling and right hand on the ramp.
routes.Location: Direct start of ripple.

routes.name: Rule of Thumb
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start as Under Direct Rule and climb up and right using crimps and the corner. Hit the lip and top out to the right.
routes.Location: Cleanup boulder, right end of the side opposite the trail to central.

routes.name: Running Man
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start left of the pothole on the juggy rail. Move up and right to the pothole and top out through the hole.
routes.Location: Georger

routes.name: Shadow Boxing
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the flat eight-finger edge four feet right of gulag's start. A bunch of left-facing sidepulls and flakes descend from the peak of the boulder. From the edge, do a long move to get a tiny left-facing crimp sidepull (its sharp!), and head up to the sidepull flakes. Keep workng up and slightly left, resisting the urge to jump right into an adjacent problem. Finish over the lip on big holds.
routes.Location: X

routes.name: Spectacles
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start as Floryshe, climb further up and right to get the namesake hold, and end on the finishing jugs of Threesome.
routes.Location: X

routes.name: Sprain
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start left of Y.A.P.P. on a low jug. Climb the face trending slightly right.
routes.Location: Georger 3

routes.name: Subway
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the ledge, then work to another good ledge and top out.

routes.name: Super Sharp Shooter
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This is the premiere uber-classic line at Niagara Glen. Guidebook says V6 but beware, this problem routinely gives even strong climbers fits and the feet are very polished (took me a good 30+ attempts which is quite a bit for me at that grade). Start on on an undercling and move into some thin crimps at about chest height. After this, a long move to a sloping two finger pocket marks the crux which must be executed precisely and with great determination (hence the name). I've given it a V6+ grade given my experience on other problems of that grade. Feel free to disagree....
routes.Location: See one of numerous topos available online such as:   topout.org/static/pdf/Niaga…

routes.name: Superglue
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start on the large pocket on the corner and climb up.
routes.Location: Toad

routes.name: Take Your Pick
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start as Epiphany and climb left out under the roof on a good sidepull. Hit the lip, traverse right and then top out.
routes.Location: A boulder out to the right from the main path through Upper Old Country towards Central.

routes.name: Thelemic

routes.name: Threesome

routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Stand start at the rail in the middle of the face, climb directly up and top out.

routes.name: Cocina
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start with left hand on a high big sidepull edge and right hand of the lowest opposing sidepull. Climb up.
routes.Location: Birdland

routes.name: Dognuts
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start on the left side and climb up a seam on the face, then top out.
routes.Location: Graffiti

routes.name: Dust Bunnies
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Stand start on the right side of the face and climb up to top out.

routes.name: Elephant Talk
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start two feet left of the right arête and climb up small edges the whole way up.
routes.Location: Noah's Ark

routes.name: Floating Seagulls
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the two handed jug. Move left to the arête, traverse onto the face and gain the big jug to the topout.
routes.Location: Right of Comrade/Birdland routes, on the tip.

routes.name: Funnel Cake
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Stand start on the juggy ledge on the face, climb straight up and top it out.

routes.name: Gary Greenfeet
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start under the little overhang with left on large pocket and right on crimp. Work your way through crimps and pockets.
routes.Location: Fantastic Four, right/closest to Comrade

routes.name: General Principles
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the left side and traverse the main face staying low to finish on the big sidepull around the corner
routes.Location: Noah's Ark, left end of east side.

routes.name: Harry Highstep
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start left of Percy Pete with left on pinchy block and right on sidepull. Go straight up.
routes.Location: Fantastic four

routes.name: Hat With no Tassle
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start down low on the big pockets, pull up to a small edge and go for jug above.
routes.Location: Rifle

routes.name: The Icey Heel
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start towards the left side and traverse the lip to top out on the far right.
routes.Location: Slipshot, east/trail side

routes.name: Impressionist
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start on the good crimps and climb through the good holds.
routes.Location: Comrade

routes.name: Instant Espresso
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the medium crimps all the way to the right of the boulder.
routes.Location: Rifle, right

routes.name: Kareishu
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Stand start in the middle of the face and climb direct to the peak.

routes.name: Luge
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start as Waterslide and climb out the right side of the roof.
routes.Location: Dihedral to the left of the stairs on the way to Central area.

routes.name: The Magic Bullet
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start near the middle of the face on a low sidepull and pocket. Move up right to a pocket and then to the lip.
routes.Location: Controversy, west side.

routes.name: Mandy Middlefinger
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start on an undercling pocket and a sidepull pocket, go up to the mono and up left.
routes.Location: Fantastic Four, left-most / furthest south.

routes.name: The Minimalist
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start sitting under the clean blank bulge. Start on a left hand undercling and right sidepull. Move up to the slot and top out on good jugs.
