routes.Location: Comrade

routes.name: Moth Balls
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Stand start on the left arete, climb the arete to top out.

routes.name: Motile
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the right slabby face with the vertical slashes to the top.

routes.name: Mr Gnu's Gotta Problem
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start right of the arête and move through the pinch and small edges to the top.
routes.Location: Noah's Ark

routes.name: Percy Pete
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start, then climb the right side of the face.
routes.Location: Fantastic Four, right end of southeast side.

routes.name: Poo-Tips
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Starts low on the right under the roof, moving left and up onto a bad sloper. Continue left and up. Topout is mossy.
routes.Location: Entering Wonderland from Central, to the left as you descend down the steps. Starts under the short (~8 ft.) protruding ledge

routes.name: Quickie
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start on the low flake and move left to a pocket then right to the lip.
routes.Location: Controversy, left end of west side.

routes.name: Revival
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the large sidepull rail below the split on the boulder face, then join to top out.

routes.name: Simply Stupid
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start. Move though the good edges and block to the large sloping pocket.
routes.Location: Rifle, left

routes.name: Tastes Like Chocolate
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start just left of the large crack and climb up and left.
routes.Location: Comrade, right end of the side that does not face Slipshot.

routes.name: U.14
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the high pockets on the prow and climb up.
routes.Location: Controversy, south side.

routes.name: V2 by 2
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start in the middle of the face and climb up through the undercling.
routes.Location: Noah's Ark

routes.name: Vigilante Justice
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start in the overhang on a fat pinch and bad crimp. Climb up using slopers to topout on the clean jugs.
routes.Location: Slipshot, west side

routes.name: Walking Spanish Down the Glen
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start, squeeze the blocky sidepulls and climb up the tall arête. The classic V0 in the Glen.
routes.Location: Hallway

routes.name: Waterslide
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start at the back in the dihedral and climb out the left side of the roof.
routes.Location: A dihedral left of the stairs heading towards Central.

routes.name: Who Needs Feet
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start on a jug on the left side of the boulder. Climb along the lip on good jugs to topout around the corner.
routes.Location: Comrade, left end of the side that does not face Slipshot.

routes.name: 99 Bottles of Beer
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Shares a start with 'Hangover Overhang' and takes the line of bolts up and left over a roof

routes.name: American Lightweights
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Large ledge before reaching first bolt, then overhang. Anchor is basically after topping out. Lots of rope friction on rock if lowering from anchor
routes.Location: First sport route if accessed when forking left from parking lot. Next to picnic table.
routes.Protection: First bolt is above an easy ledge

routes.name: Beer Bottle Rocket
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb up the face of the vague buttress to anchors.
routes.Location: Descend to the cliff's base, then proceed left (climber's left) to a wide area marked by a distinct Sphinx-shaped rock formation above.

routes.name: Bottle Glass Pinnacle
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start below a large layback crack on the outside of the pinnacle. Climb the crack and up left to the top.
routes.Protection: Good options for gear placement. Rappel down from 99 Bottles of Beer (5.10b) to the immediate right.

routes.name: Captain Caveman
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Juggy and overhanging start moves into a short technical move above the cave.
routes.Location: Located on the left edge of two double stacked micro caves. Look for the first bolt on the top left of the lower larger cave.
routes.Protection: 5 bolts to anchors with rappel rings.

routes.name: The Cardinal Rule
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This route is positioned on the wall between the first two pinnacles of the crag. Climb vertical terrain navigating a crack to reach a ledge. After the ledge more vertical terrain gets you to the anchors.

routes.name: Caviar Heaves
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The more prominent crack system up the vague buttress.
routes.Location: Start just left of Champagne Hangover. Follow the right trending crack.

routes.name: Champagne Hangover
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Left most crack on buttress

routes.name: Dans Magical Night in Puja
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Right most rout in the area. Similar start to "I'd rather be at whites bluff".
routes.Location: Right most rout in the area. Similar start to "I'd rather be at whites bluff".
routes.Protection: 5 bolts to anchors with rappel rings

routes.name: Dial 911
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: An apt name for a climb with potential for big air onto lower angle terrain below.
routes.Location: There is a blank headwall left of the Sphinx Crack, scrabble up to the face and climb it.
routes.Protection: Very little places for gear, mostly BD cam size: .75, 1, 2. Place lots of gear before the face and try not to blow it!

routes.name: Don't Drink and Dive
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Apparently this climb was easier until a piece broke off.
routes.Location: Immediately left of Steve's Route.
routes.Protection: 5 (?) bolts to glue-in bolt anchor over the edge without rappel rings. Bring double length sling or alpine draws to set up a top rope anchor.

routes.name: Falling Stars
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Finger sized crack that trend up to the right on a slight overhang. Clear the mantle and continue onto a second face climb further back to end.
routes.Location: Belay should be located right next to a small tree before walking around the arete.

routes.name: Fat Crack Overhang
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Follow the crack to the roof before climbing through the roof using a fat roof crack. Continue until you reach anchors of 99 Bottles of Beer.
routes.Location: Right of Hangout Overhang.
routes.Protection: Great protection, I suggest placing a size 3 or 4 cam before completing the crux of climbing through the roof.

routes.name: Front of the Pinnacle Junior
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the pinnacle right of Furry Critters (shown in photo), step across to the main wall and continue to climb out. Either build a trad anchor or use the anchor closest.
routes.Location: Right of Furry Critters.

routes.name: Furry Critters
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the face to the crack and up ledges to the top.
routes.Location: Start about one meter left of the pinnacle.

routes.name: Furry Critters Direct
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Low crux, easy climbing after 2nd bolt.

routes.name: Gym Climbers
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start climbing below the large dead cedar in the photo. Top out just right of the dead cedar, anchor just over the lip.
routes.Location: Under large dead cedar.
routes.Protection: 2 glue in anchor points at top.

routes.name: Hangover Overhang
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This route sits at the edge of a pinnacle and follows up an arete to a striking overhung ledge where the route ends. Unlike many escarpment "5.9s" this rating on this route is fair. The crux of this route is pulling onto the face just below the anchors. With both a good rest before the crux move, and the bolt line directing your falls out into space, this is a great lead for new climbers.    The rock on this climb can stay wet longer than others in the area which is of particular concern as this can make the final moves extremely insecure.   As a final note, the draw line on this route makes cleaning on the way down difficult (particularly if you rappel). It is best to have a second clean on their way up.
routes.Location: This route is roughly 50m from the down climb to the climber`s left. It sits on the edge of the pinnacle and can easily be identified with the striking overhung finish.    Lower or rap off the anchors to get down.
routes.Protection: This route is tightly bolted all the way up. There are 7 bolts plus the anchors (if memory serves) so you will need 9 draws. Most climbers should be able to reach the first bolt from the ground.

routes.name: Hole Funness
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Short left trending bolted line. Slight traverse left then follows a notch upward to the finish.
routes.Location: Located 12 feet left of Furry Critters.

routes.name: I'd Rather be at White Bluff
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A more tame version of the climb to the left.
routes.Location: Starts just to the right of the small cave, with the first bolt located at the top and right of the cave.
routes.Protection: 5 bolts to anchors with rappel rings.

routes.name: Intrusive Thoughts
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Starts to the right of The Cardinal Rule (5.8). Climb up into a cave to set the first draw, then work your way up the sidepull/layback crack line to a ledge on the left. Take some breaths to calm the nerves before making some technical moves to get the second bolt. The climb goes straight up until veering to the left to finish on some rustier anchors to the right of The Cardinal Rule's anchors.

routes.name: Korn-erred
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Both Korn-erred and Nice Bulge share nearly the same start with Korn-erred being the left option that follows a more prominent crack system with a more overhung start.

routes.name: Launching Pad
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Easy with no rope drag (provided you bring at least 2 double-length draws) and excellent top-out: great for newbies
routes.Location: Left of American Lightweights

routes.name: Nice Bulge
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Both Korn-erred and Nice Bulge share nearly the same start with Nice Bulge being the right option that features mainly budges and ledges to climb.

routes.name: Ray's Big Butt
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Mellow but interesting climbing.
routes.Location: Left most route at Bottle Glass Crag, just left of the double stack of micro caves. Starts next to sign indicating where climbing is permitted.
routes.Protection: 5 bolts to anchors with rap rings.

routes.name: The Sound of Breaking Glass
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb up the corner and step left to the top.
routes.Location: 5m left of the first pinnacle.

routes.name: Sphinx Crack
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the crack through the wide section at the top. While you're at it, you may as well surmount the Sphinx.
routes.Location: On the left side of the buttress is a crack leading to a Sphinx shaped block overhanging the top of the cliff.

routes.name: Steve's Route
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Previously known as 'Crawling on Glass'
routes.Location: Immediately right of "Don't Drink and Drive"
routes.Protection: Anchors with rappel rings at the top

routes.name: Unnamed (Oct 25)
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Similar start as Furry Critters, but stay on the left (darker) section of the wall above the two big blocks. Climb finishes towards the left side of the headwall. Most heavy loose holds were cleaned but use caution.

routes.name: Unnamed (Oct 25)
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start the climb at the left-facing flakes at the base. Stay left of the roof.Some loose rocks were removed when establishing the climb.
routes.Location: On the main cliff wall behind the Second Pinnacle; climber's left of Glass Carpet. This route climbs just right of the tree near the top of the wall (pictured).

routes.name: Wally
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Bolted sport route in Bottle glass. High first clip.

routes.name: Angel Drawers
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start in the corner climber's right of Tapestry then move right at the roof and back left to reach the overhanging crack.
routes.Location: 3m right of Tapestry

routes.name: Autumn Finale
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)

routes.name: Baby Face
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb up the difficult face with very little protection.
routes.Location: Climbers left of "Crank if You Love Jesus"

routes.name: Boa Constrictor
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A wide crack / chimney (depending on the dimensions of the climber) that goes 2/3 of the way up the cliff. It is sitting in a corner allowing for lots of stemming if you want to stay out of the crack, or right side in if you want to try your hand at off-width movements. You can climb the entire route outside of the crack, but where is the fun in that?     This route is quite popular, so get there early in the day if you don't want to line-up.
routes.Location: It is around the first "corner" after you head to climber's left from the down climb. It is quite distinctive as this is the only wide crack in this area.
routes.Protection: The guide book says there is good gear, but if you have an escarpment rack the chances are you will need to hunt around for smaller placements further in the crack or on the surrounding faces.   There are glue-in anchors at the top of the cliff, but they are about 1.5m from the edge so bring a long cordlette or some double length slings.

routes.name: Bob's Your Uncle
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Loose face through tree branches
routes.Location: Start at 145 m

routes.name: British
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Large layback crack creating low crux. Easy after completing the crack section, nice climbing with great gear.
routes.Location: Right of Fear of Flight and left of Piece of Duff (middle of the 3 cracks).

routes.name: Calhoun
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!

routes.name: Cedar
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!

routes.name: Chopped
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Bolted in the winter of 2010-11 between the Prow and Georger Pecans, this line was subsequently chopped the spring per the guidebook. It is still feasible to do this line on top rope and it climbs the arete close to the corner and left of the Prow. Solid holds with occasionally good feet
routes.Location: In the Endgame area; around the corner from the off with chimney that is Georger Pecans.
routes.Protection: Top rope only: no pro, unless someone decides to rebolt this line.

routes.name: Durley's Return
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!

routes.name: Fear of Flying
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Through a roof, thin crack to easy groove
routes.Location: Start at 40 m

routes.name: Flying Kiwi
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!

routes.name: Flying Squirrel
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!

routes.name: The Go Beside
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb up the the bolt immediately left of Operation Evaporation. Continue past 3 more bolts, staying generally on the arete. Move onto Operation Evaporation on the large ledge and continue easily to the top.
routes.Location: Left of Operation Evaporation

routes.name: Green Thumb
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!

routes.name: Hydroseal
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Thin corners and overhangs, up and right to easier climbing
routes.Location: Start at 135 m

routes.name: Hyper Extension
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on pocketed face left of Thin Series, height dependent
routes.Location: Start at 15 m

routes.name: Jetsam
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!

routes.name: Knight's Move
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Up and left past a bolt to a left facing corner, follow corner to top
routes.Location: Start at 110 m

routes.name: La Guapa
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb cracks to the ledge and traverse left to access the obvious offwidth above (crux).
routes.Location: Shares start with Second Time Lucky and easily identified by the obvious offwidth.

routes.name: Nervous System
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Thin cracks and corners, avoiding Skywalker
routes.Location: Start at 112m

routes.name: Orange Wall
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Starting on a detached hold, climb up and left to a high first bolt. From here move straight up the face past another bolt and a pumpy bulge to easier ground, where the climbing becomes more meandering. Just before the top is a pin-protected move; the pin can be backed up with good gear.
routes.Location: About 15 feet left of Boa Constrictor.
routes.Protection: Good gear is available for the hard parts, after the sketchy climb to the first bolt. Small nuts and TCU's are important. Standard rack to 1". Bolted toprope anchor.

routes.name: Orange Wall Direct
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A direct start to Orange Wall. Boulder up to the aging piton, then join Orange Wall and continue to the top.
routes.Location: Immediately left of Orange Wall

routes.name: Ramble
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!

routes.name: Reject Direct
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Shallow groove over bulge to finger crack
routes.Location: Start 3 m right of Boa Constrictor

routes.name: Reservoir
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!

routes.name: Sandman
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Follow crack to a small roof
routes.Location: Start at 240m just right of Orc

routes.name: Second Time Lucky
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb up the crack left to the ledge. The route goes up and left from here.
routes.Location: Shares start with La Guapa which goes right from the ledge and is easily identified by the large offwidth crux above.

routes.name: Skywalker
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Straight to a large solution pocket, thin crack past a pin - no good pro until the crack
routes.Location: Start at 111 m

routes.name: Spring Finale
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Crack system to the right of autumn finale that climbs through a small bush into a roof and up the arete. Line stays on the opposite side of autumn finale. Decent pro available.  This route is not in the guidebook but has definitely been climbed before. I'm adding the route and naming it because I climbed it today but I'm not the first ascensionist. If the first ascensionist sees this, please feel free to update.
routes.Location: Right sided crack on the right side of Autumn finale.
routes.Protection: Gear. Bolts anchors on top of cliff.

routes.name: Spurbur
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!

routes.name: Staircase
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Check guide book for details. Potentially an old descent to access the crag. May be soloed up but offers decent gear placements. No bolted anchors at the top, large block about 10-15 feet away from the top may offer some anchor placements. Somewhat overgrown and dirty, not recommended unless looking for an easy/first trad lead.
routes.Location: The start of the route is essentially the high point on the trail, short walk climber's right of Thin Series. Look for the large flake at the base, that points climber's right (see photo).
routes.Protection: Decent placement, no bolted anchors (block about 10-15 feet from top with some anchor placements)

routes.name: Sunshine
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Up a crack, passing roof on the right, then left to easy groove.
routes.Location: Start at 138m

routes.name: Sunsplash
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: face to blocks, move left, then angle right
routes.Location: between End Game and Flying Kiwi

routes.name: Super Dave's Last Stunt
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Thin right-facing flakes to a roof, up and right
routes.Location: Start at 222 m

routes.name: Tapestry
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Classic 5.9

routes.name: Thin Series
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!

routes.name: Unknown
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!

routes.name: Veg
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: 2 bolt anchor that's accessible from the top.Climb the obvious line of cracks and ledges to the right of Boa Constrictor. Anchor is far back from the edge.Protection: Standard Rack to 4". Bring a lot of slings.

routes.name: Which Way Now
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: climb to ledge, traverse right, face and crack through a notch
routes.Location: Start at number 0

routes.name: Yoyo
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!

routes.name: The 5.3 Right of Median
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb a big flake right of the Median ledge that ends at anchors on a ledge.
routes.Location: Anchors are located on the ledge just right of the tree you come to after getting off the flake and onto the ledge. The climb is located right of Median, a very obvious large flake.

routes.name: Abendrot
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This route follows a finger / hand crack up the face with many good flakes and block to stand on along the way. This crack is extremely polished making jamming difficult, and the feet are extremely slippery for such a low grade. After heavy rain this route may be covered in mud and dried dirt adding to the insecurity of the holds. It is still a decent easy lead due to the size of the crack and secure gear placement stances.
routes.Location: This route is approximately 100m to the climbers left of the stairs. The biggest landmark to locate this route is tree found about half-way up the face just to the right of the route.
routes.Protection: This line is lead on trad gear. A standard escarpment rack will work here. The crack is quite irregular making cam placement tough, but there are enough restrictions to make passives easy to place.   There are glue-ins at the top of the cliff, they are about 1m back from the edge so be sure to take a long cordlette or some slings to extend your anchor down if you wish to top-rope this route.

routes.name: Army Days
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Up to a bolt, then left to a mantle, straight up and undercling the roof
routes.Location: Start at No.8A

routes.name: Bird's Nest Soup
routes.FA:: Jim Mark, Roger Parsons, Helmut Microys
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb up to the "bird's nest" (the large pocket below the overhang). Make the reachy move up left and then follow corners to the top.
routes.Location: Start 5m left of "Final Finale Direct".

routes.name: The Blocks
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb up blocks to the ledge and exit through the gap.
routes.Location: Route does not topout. Exit through the gap from the ledge.

routes.name: Chalk
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb straight up to the first mantle ledge on "Chalky's Balance Climb". Second half of the route follows the finish of "Chalky's Balance Climb"
routes.Location: A couple meters climbers left of "Crack One".
routes.Protection: nuts, small cams, bolts on "Chalky's Balance Climb"

routes.name: Crack One
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the crack past a flake and small roof to the top.
routes.Location: Start at No. 8C

routes.name: Crack Three
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb crack 3m right of The Plaque to the top. Avoid Finale.
routes.Location: Climber's left from the stairs. Near The Plaque.

routes.name: Crack Two
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Follow the layback crack through a small roof to the top.
routes.Location: Start at the plaque.

routes.name: Crack Two and a Half
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb straight up the face between the two cracks.
routes.Location: In between "Crack Two" and "Crack Three".

routes.name: Deadline
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb up to the bolt, pockets on the left to keep at 5.8 (or crack on the right for 5.10)
routes.Location: Start at No.8

routes.name: Discovery
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Easy. Aim for the trees. Bring a helmet as occasionally kids will throw sticks and rocks from path above
routes.Location: Start left of Sinusitis
routes.Protection: Anchors at top, requiring long runners

routes.name: Final Finale
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb up over a small roof, move up and right across a ledge. Continue up and right to a small pillar above a large roof and then head back left and up to the top.
routes.Location: Start at No. 10

routes.name: Final Finale Direct
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Through open book (climb the face for 5.8) to the roof, move left to Final Finale belay, then back right and up past the cedar
routes.Location: Start 5m right of No.10A

routes.name: Finale
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the groove until it is possible to step right. Follow ledges and cracks to the top.
routes.Location: Start at No. 9

routes.name: Holy Trinity
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Follow the corner until blocked by a roof, traverse right past a suspect black flake. Climb the corner and traverse right again then take the gully to the top.
routes.Location: Start at No. 13

routes.name: Jiminy Cricket
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the large right-facing flake. Continue straight up past a bolt near the top.
routes.Location: Start at No. 4C

routes.name: Median
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the corner below two belay bolts on the ledge. From the ledge, climb a small roof by a large tree, follow the gully to the top.
routes.Location: Starts right of Sinusitis.

routes.name: The Pinnacle
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb up to the ledge and follow cracks in the middle of the face to the top.
routes.Location: Start at No. 22A
routes.Protection: Gear required. Rappel station at the top of the pinnacle.

routes.name: Sinusitis
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Follow the cracks to the large corner, head up the corner and then traverse left (crux). Wrestle through cedars to get to the top.Can be climbed as 1x long pitch or 2x. Midway anchors are offset on the ledge below the roof.
routes.Location: 3m left of Median.

routes.name: SN2
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climbs the pinnacle. Follow thin cracks up then head slight right to the top.
routes.Location: Start at No. 22C
routes.Protection: Gear required. Top of pinnacle has rappel station.

routes.name: Steve's Arete
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the thin crack to a ledge. Traverse up and right, following cracks and mantles until near the arete. Head up.
routes.Location: Start at No. 6

routes.name: Straight Up
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the small dihedral to a triangular roof, step left onto a ledge then step right and head straight up through the roof.
routes.Location: Start at No. 1

routes.name: Wet Dreams
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climbs the pinnacle. Climb up to the ledge and follow the arete to the top.
routes.Location: Start at No. 22A.
routes.Protection: Gear required. Rappel station at the top of the pinnacle.

routes.name: Abendrot
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: fun climbing for the grade, a little run out in the middle but could be better protected with large cams to place in very large crack/flake
routes.Location: Pinocchio Area
routes.Protection: trad route, bolts at top to build anchor, but spaced apart

routes.name: Ape Factor 6
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Nothing special. Do some climbing between "Fallen Trees" and "Hot Stuff". Some reach moves I guess.
routes.Location: Showpiece area

routes.name: B.S.
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Two cruxes separated by good ledges. May look more intimidating than it is, the holds are there once you commit!

routes.name: Be a Hoot, Don't Noise Pollute
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Awesome climbing with a few reachy moves on good holds (crux).Stick clip to 2nd bolt or extend first bolt to minimize rope drag, also might be good to extend the 3rd draw (at the first ledge).

routes.name: Be a Mentor
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Furthest left (facing cliff) bolted route in Rattlesnake.

routes.name: Chalky's Balance Climb
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Fun climb except for the almost non-existent protection. Climb the Crete on the left side of the wall up to the overhang.
routes.Location: Plaque wall, climbers left of "Chalk"

routes.name: The Chemist Returns
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb to bolt, then up the crack on the right
routes.Location: 2.5 meters left of McMaster Special, opposite of pinnacle.
routes.Protection: All RSP is bolted for Top Rope

routes.name: Deadline Crack
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the difficult thin crack up the middle of the recessed wall with a single bolt for pro.
routes.Location: Recessed wall

routes.name: Discovery
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!

routes.name: Discovery 2nd Pitch
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: From the ledge at the top of (bolted) Discovery, continue up corner right to Median Ledge. From the ledge, continue up the corner right to the Median gully, cross over and climb up the steep wall above passing another old bolt to the top.
routes.Location: Climber's left from the stairs, past pinnacle and Split Ledge, before stream.

routes.name: Easy Chimney
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Can be used as a descent as well but very polished. Name speaks for its self.
routes.Location: Plaque wall, hard to miss look for the chimney.
routes.Protection: gear but honestly its to easy and doesn't need gear

routes.name: Fallen Trees
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start at a right facing flake just left of "Pinocchio". Climb up to the bulge, step left onto the ledge and up the groove on the right. A message from the guidebook "As you try and make it feel 5.5, consider that it was originally graded. 5.3!".
routes.Location: Showpiece area

routes.name: The Father
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Boulder up the thin crack to the right of a large pocket. Traverse left to the high first bolt, then right to a second higher up. Exit up the groove.
routes.Location: Right of the spring

routes.name: Find a Mentor
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Fun easy route
routes.Location: All the way at the end - climber's left - of the crag, just a few meters left of B.S.

routes.name: The Fire Escape
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb corner up to ledge, scramble up and left to belay below a right-facing corner. Climb corner to roof and move right to escape.
routes.Location: Climber's left of stairs, immediately after pinnacle.

routes.name: H2S
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start a meter or so left of McMaster. Climb through the roof.   Trad- don't trust the pins
routes.Location: Just climbers left of stairs, before pinnacle.
routes.Protection: All RSP is bolted for top rope.

routes.name: Hardman Traverse
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Traverse the undercut wall between "Holy Trinity" and "Crepidation". Climb from left to right.

routes.name: Holy Cow
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb a nice crack under the roof to the first bolt, which you will definitely want with the sketchiness to come. Climb up to a few feet below the main roof to find a nice horizontal Juggy flake, holds are great but the flake feels sketchy. Climb right past the roof to another set of fairly jugey holds up a crack and traverse back left over the main roof. Topping out right of the small cedar tree where there is one more bolt.A handful of rusty pitons dot this route at the crux’s, clip at own risk!!!
routes.Protection: Ancora at top and two bolted rap rings for a quick repel

routes.name: Hot Stuff
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb "Jimmy Cricket" to the ledge system, follow the ledges diagonally right to finish near "Abendrot". Place lots of gear on the traverse just incase your second falls.
routes.Location: Showpiece area

routes.name: Loblwas
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Can be tricky for the grade.

routes.name: Lutz'z Route
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The climb starts as thin face climbing on polished limestone. Hard crux after 2nd bolt.

routes.name: McMaster Special
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start at pedestal, hit the roof, call it a day, pretty simple.
routes.Protection: All RSP is bolted for top rope.

routes.name: Median
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: First pitch is 5.0, second is 5.3. Short, easy route, great for beginners with good stances for placing gear and bolts between pitches and at top for setting up a belay.
routes.Location: Climber's left of stairs, past the pinnacle and the Split Ledge - below next ledge system with a low-angle broken up area below. If you get to another ledge system with a spring at the base, you've gone too far.

routes.name: The Mother
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Two pitch climb, with the first pitch fully bolted.
routes.Location: Similar start to the father before trending left under a small roof.

routes.name: Orcstone
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Shares start with Straight Up and actually goes straight up.
routes.Location: Shares start with Straight Up

routes.name: The Overhang
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb either of two facing corners to large ledge. From ledge, climb lightning bolt wide crack and move right to top out near tree.
routes.Location: Couple metres left of The Blocks

routes.name: PD
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Fun climbing to an anchor above and just left of a large block (climb left of the roof)
routes.Location: Just down the wall and around the corner from Pinnacle wall.

routes.name: Pinocchio
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!

routes.name: Poor Tree
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start a couple metres left of "Jimmy Cricket", climb the face trending left to the crack at the top (right of the cedar). No gear until the top.
routes.Location: Showpiece area

routes.name: Raccoon Army
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!

routes.name: Rogue's Gallery
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The obvious roof crack left of BS

routes.name: Showpiece
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Scramble up blocks just left of the arete and climb the thin crack up right to a right-facing flake.  From the small ledge, climb the wide crack and over the final small roof to victory.  Very polished.  A broken hold has made this route significantly harder than the original 5.9 grade.
routes.Location: Descend the stairs and hike climbers' left.  Pass the spring and the plaque, arriving at an area with a wall recessed back and flanked by two chimneys.  Showpiece is more-or-less the arete on the left side of the recessed area.  The remainder of the climbs are further left after the base trail descends.

routes.name: Sinusitis
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb this "two pitch" climb as a single! The upper section under the roof features a fun crack and dihedral before stemming out left to mantel. Good gear on the second pitch.
routes.Location: Route starts on the chossy staircase, just left of the median that heads into an obvious dihedral under a roof.
routes.Protection: Bolted anchors on the topside of the cliff.

routes.name: So Good
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Very fun and enjoyable. Crux section between two ledges about halfway up.

routes.name: Son of Bogus
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the face left of the arete straight up to finish on "Showpiece" wide crack while avoiding the initial cracks on "Showpiece".
routes.Location: Showpiece area

routes.name: Stickety Clip, Barba Trick
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Easy line that climbs up and to the left on Pinnacle wall
routes.Location: Farthest of the three bolted lines on pinnacle wall

routes.name: Thank Mr. Goose
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Nice climbing up to an anchor immediately below and to the right a large block.
routes.Location: Immediately right of PD

routes.name: Treed Lightly
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: First set of anchors are deep on a ledge about 40% up the cliff. A few loose holds (one on the lower left at the beginning of the climb and one on the higher left close to the first ledge). Limestone.

routes.name: Treed Lightly (Extension)
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Can be done from ground up with about 16 draws, just extend or skip the midway anchors. Crux sequence is high enough from the ledge to avoid a bad fall.

routes.name: Trepidation
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Interesting climb up the Crete left of "Creepy Crawly".
routes.Location: Showpiece area

routes.name: Two Pieces
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The name is accurate! There's about two pieces above a bolt which protects the crux, start at the same spot of "Showpiece" but pull left through the roof.
routes.Location: Showpiece area
routes.Protection: bolt at crux the about two pieces above that.

routes.name: Two Pieces Direct
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb up "Two Pieces" to the ledge. Go up the left facing corner and continue straight up the wall to the top.
routes.Location: Showpiece area
routes.Protection: bolt at the crux then very little good gear

routes.name: Yard Sale Season
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Blocky and polished, like most of the climbs in the area. Overhanging arete that features many blocky segments.
routes.Location: Right most sportline in the Pinnacle Area (closest to the stairs).

routes.name: After You
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start at the bottom of Noob Cocoon, veer right after the ledge, working diagonally up to a cedar. Belay here (do not sling cedars!), then make the short trip to the top, either through the roof crack or up the face.
routes.Location: Right edge of Cave Ledge, shares start with Noob Cocoon.
routes.Protection: 1 or 2 bolts at start (from Noob Cocoon sport route), then good gear to anchors at top.

routes.name: Amphitheatre
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Can set a top rope by going left before reaching the stairs and going over the wall near the lookout area. There are 3 bolts at the top to create the anchor.Excellent climbing with a few tough sections which ease off into a 5.7 for the last third of the climb.

routes.name: Animal Route
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start just right of "Voyageur at a corner. Climb up throught the jungle to the top.
routes.Location: Nelson's Escape Hatch Area
routes.Protection: I didn't place any gear and if you decide to climb this route just free solo. Its not worth a climb let alone gear

routes.name: The Beer Party
routes.FA:: Urs Kallen, Jim Ferguson, Helmut Microys (1965)
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The Beer Party climbs the large corner between the two sport routes, Rocket Man and Exposure Plus. Start left of the corner and climb the path of least resistance over some loose rock to a ledge beneath the first bulge (crux). This somewhat awkward bulge has two pitons and some decent jams.Continue up the corner to the top.
routes.Location: The Beer Party climbs the large corner between the two sport routes, Rocket Man and Exposure Plus. See beta photo.
routes.Protection: Gear to 2 inches. Two glue-ins (no chains) and a large tree are located on top to bring up a second. Alternatively, traverse hard right near the end of the route and end on the anchors for Exposure Plus.

routes.name: Bloody Mess
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Short and dead easy. However, this side of the wall gets less traffic so great for newbies. Bring long runners and watch out for rope getting caught in crack near top-out ledge.
routes.Location: Look for miniature cave/hole at base of cliff and climb straight up.
routes.Protection: Anchors at top, quite far from ledge so long runners are required

routes.name: Cobwebs
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Follows the left-facing corner. Polished.
routes.Location: Start at No. 33

routes.name: Crystal Palace
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Easy blocky start leads into an overhanging traverse right. Continue up through small dihedrals above.
routes.Location: Left most bolted line on Amphitheatre Ledge area. Two other sport lines should be present directly to the right

routes.name: Deception
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the bulge between the starts of "Pedro" and "Orangeman". Continue straight up to the top, staying left of the crack.
routes.Location: Cobwebs Area

routes.name: Excalibur
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Bouldery start leads to nice climbing up top

routes.name: Exposure Plus
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Fun climbing with one tricky section. Don't get sidetracked!
routes.Protection: Bolts and anchors.  11 draws (not incl. your anchor)

routes.name: Freedom
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Chimney/crack climb that's good for beginner lead.

routes.name: High Traverse / Limbo Plus
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Long and exposed, with a steep exit.

routes.name: Ice Castle Plus
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Left variation of "More Than Three is a Crowd", basically bypassing the 5.9-5.10a climbing.
routes.Location: Middle bolted sport route in the Amphitheatre Ledge area. After the first two bolts it merges with "More Than Three is a Crowd".

routes.name: Jolly Rectum
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Face climbing up to a small roof at 15m, go around left, and then back right to exit via the roof crack.
routes.Location: Last route (from climber's left to right) in Cobwebs area.

routes.name: More Than Three is a Crowd
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Low crux at the blocky start.

routes.name: Nelson's Escape Hatch
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Blocky steps and easy low-angle chimney leads to the top of the cliff. Sometimes used as an alternate to the nearby stairs, though this is not recommended.
routes.Location: Descend stairs and hike climber's right, following ledge until it ends. Look for blocky "steps" and chimney - you're there.

routes.name: Noob Cocoon
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Location: Right bolted route at Cave Ledge area

routes.name: Orangeman
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Right of "Pedro". Climb the right facing corner with orange lichen, then the crack on the left to the top.
routes.Location: Cobwebs Area

routes.name: Over the Hill
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Follow the obvious groove past trees.
routes.Location: Start at No. 41

routes.name: Pedro
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start just right of "Solo Direct", climb straight up and over the bulge at the top.
routes.Location: Cobwebs Area

routes.name: Poop Pouch for your Pooch
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!
routes.Location: Starts at cave on ledge

routes.name: Port
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start below the dead ceder midway up. Climb up keeping left of the dead tree.
routes.Location: Far east cliff

routes.name: Rocket Man
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Starts off as a warmup to the ledge and then two distinct cruxes separated by a good rest. A couple powerful reachy moves in both cruxes along with a few crimpy intermediates. Very fun after figuring out a beta that works for you.
routes.Protection: Bolts and anchorsMight be good to extend the 3rd and 5th draws to avoid your rope snagging against a block at the first crux.

routes.name: Roger's Route
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start 2m right of surprise. Climb straight up and out the overhanging corner at the top.
routes.Location: Far east wall

routes.name: Solo
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start a few meters right of "Frenchman" at some thin cracks and corners. Climb up the steepening wall and move right to exit more easily. The bottom is very poorly protected bring your small wires.
routes.Location: Cobwebs Area
routes.Protection: Very poor pro - bring your micro nuts

routes.name: Solo Direct
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start a couple meters right of "Solo", and climb straight up to exit the same.
routes.Location: Cobwebs Area

routes.name: The Spider
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Follows a classic line with several old pitons still visible.
routes.Location: Located just left of Cobwebs crack. Easily identifiable as there are two sport lines next to one another, the one on the right is The Spider. It features an obvious undercling below the first bolt. Might be seeping in early season near the anchors.

routes.name: Starboard
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb up as for "Port", but stay right of the dead tree. This route was retrobolted as "Trash Pandamonium".
routes.Location: Far east cliff

routes.name: Surprise
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the face to the right-facing corner at the top. Ascend the corner.
routes.Location: Start at No. 38

routes.name: Trash Pandamonium
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climbs the same route as "Starboard" just you can climb it as a sport climb. Retrobolted from "Sarboard"

routes.name: Tune Your Head Not Your Crag
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Interesting climbing for the grade

routes.name: Voyageur
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Loose corner full of choss and dirt and weeds and freakin' trees.
routes.Location: Hike climber's right after descending stairs. Route is right after More Than Three is a Crowd and Garden Path.

routes.name: Wanderweg
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Wander up the wall right of "Trash Pandamonium" trending to the right. Exit on "Over the Hill".
routes.Location: Far east cliff

routes.name: Where's Your Bucket
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Retro bolted route on left most side of the east end area.

routes.name: Aquawoman
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the right sidepull and left crimp work up to the base of the roof and straight up the sloopy moves left of the arret very fun climb definetely a lil heady!
routes.Location: This climb is the third from the left

routes.name: Flip flops
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Low start on the left side of the the boulder large moves on awesome holds. Depending on water level this climb may be out. Very enjoyable climbing and a good warm up to the other routes

routes.name: Gentle giant
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Stan start on crimps in the middle of the boulder. Refer to picture. Follow the seam up to the horizontal ledge and trend right. Wrap around the right arette. Grab the large orb hold and top out.

routes.name: Wish you were here
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Starts on W shapped crimp in the middle of the boulder work up the slab to the roof then left towards the arret make a big move to pull past the roof onto some small crimps the arret and everything past it is out, there video beta on youtube of the crux
routes.Location: This is the second route from the left of the boulder (2)

routes.name: 3 Fingers Deep
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start under the right side of the steep roof on a rail and a pocket, and then climb straight up.

routes.name: The Kids Are Alright
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start under the middle of the roof and climb out on huge holds, trend slightly left on the face to top out.

routes.name: Nine Hours Later
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: SDS as per Kids, but head left while under the roof. Use toe hooks and jams to get to a pocket on a under the roof and pull the lip to the face and top out.

routes.name: Cedar Arete
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: SDS as low as possible on sidepulls and compress upwards and slightly left to follow the arete. Make sustained compression moves that end with a big throw to a large jug a few feet below the top. Match on the jug and drop off, or if you prefer top out and enjoy the view. Either way treat the small cedar tree growing out of the jug with care. The wingspan of a condor will help greatly on this problem.   Also beware of the jumbled shale piled at the base of this problem. Some nasty, uneven edges which require careful padding.
routes.Location: Starting holds are found at the base of the obvious arete on the Cedar Arete Boulder, which is found just behind the Crab.
routes.Protection: A couple pads to protect the sharp, uneven landing. Watch out for butt (tailbone) dabs on the first few moves.

routes.name: Hill's Thistle
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: SDS on low holds just to the right of the Cedar Arete problem. Instead of moving up and left, use the long, sloping arete to go directly up. A big move right off the start gains a decent section of the sloped arete from which you can move to a crimp for the left hand after locking in some heel or toe hooks. Once you move off this crimp the difficulty eases for the top out.
routes.Location: Found at the base of the over hanging arete on the Cedar Arete boulder, which is just behind the Crab.
routes.Protection: pad for the first big toss

routes.name: Cliffbrake
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: SDS Start with a toe hook where the boulder meets perpendicular V5 boulder, and hands in lower pockets, work your way up and get an adjusted high right heel on a semi-solid lip, go for high crimp, move left hand up to higher pockets and top out.
routes.Protection: Make sure to use a large pad under the bulk of the problem, and a smaller pad for the SDS

routes.name: The Crab
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: SDS under the roof on crimps and move right to slopers and horrible sloping crimps as you compress up the prow. Careful foot placement will make this somewhat easier. Squeezing for all you are worth make a big move to the huge bucket jug at the apex of the roof and finish as per Queensnake with a mantel.   The hardest classic at HWLD and a really excellent problem.   Unfortunately, The Crab boulder shifted over the 2015-2016 Winter and this problem looks to be no more.
routes.Location: Under the roof of the Crab Boulder
routes.Protection: pad or two under the roof and under the apex

routes.name: Grebe
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Stand start on high crimps, pull on with high feet and move up the short slab to top out. Pulling onto the problem is the crux and will feel significantly easier if you are tall/have strong fingers.   Traversing in from the right on slopers is "Moonwart" V4.
routes.Location: Located on the lake facing side of the Crab Boulder. Start on the obvious chalked crimps in the middle of the slab.

routes.name: Queensnake
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rising traverse along the SE side of The Crab boulder. SDS on the left and follow jugs and slopers at the lip of the boulder until the apex of the overhang at the right end. From a huge jug move up to pockets on the blunt arete and mantel. The crux comes at the end, especially if you get pumped traversing across the lip.   The closest thing to a warm-up in the area, completing the traverse to the jug gets the blood flowing even without the topout mantel.   Unfortunately, The Crab boulder shifted over the 2015-2016 Winter and this problem looks to be no more. The jugs at the lip of the boulder that were previously used to traverse are now less than a foot off the rock shelf, and pretty much underwater when the water level is high.
routes.Location: Located on the Crab Boulder, Shelf Beach
routes.Protection: pad under the apex of the roof for the topout mantel

routes.name: Chrome Blackbird
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The right-hand SDS to the classic Rusty Blackbird, this problem adds two difficult moves before joining the direct line up the face.    SDS with an undercling for the left and a sloping, triangular edge for the right. Make two strenuous moves to get established on the triangular edges used for Rusty and continue to the top out.   While this line may or may not include a "true" V7 move, the right sit eliminates the "easier" start used in Rusty Blackbird and adds difficulty to the crux and top out of that line. Somewhat contrived,  but adds some more mileage to a great line and provides some excellent, hard climbing.
routes.Location: Starts slightly right of Rusty Blackbird on the Crow Boulder. The undercling is somewhat hidden but the triangular edge to the right should be obvious.
routes.Protection: pad as per Rusty Blackbird, remember to protect the block found at the start of this problem during the crux and top out.

routes.name: Koka-Kola Klassic
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A possible warm-up for working the other lines found here, this problem starts on the left end of the Crow Boulder on a large jug. SDS and move left and up through more jugs and a diagonal crack in order to reach the top. Take care with the top out using the crack and moving slightly right to find feet.    This problem is also worth doing to check out the top out of Rusty Blackbird, which can be cruxy.
routes.Location: Left side (closest to the woods) of the Crow Boulder. The starting jug is just left of the starting holds for Rusty Blackbird.

routes.name: Rusty Blackbird
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: SDS with a large edge for the left hand and a hidden pocket/jug for the right. Make a big move right off the bat to stick the unique triangular, sloping edges in the centre of the wall. From here figure out a way to secure a higher good edge just below the lip and then move into the sloping, difficult top out.   This is an area classic with powerful moves through the face and a tenuous top out which requires a complete change of gears. Shorter individuals will have a harder time sticking the upper ledge as places for your feet disappear quickly, but keep at it and try a few of the less obvious footholds. Excellent climbing on good rock.
routes.Location: Smack in the centre of the Crow Boulder. One of the most-chalked problems here so picking it out shouldn't be too difficult.
routes.Protection: pad or two, watch the block sitting just to the right of the problem as a fall from the crux or the top out tends to send climbers heading towards it.

routes.name: The Warbler
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: SDS matched in a long vertical hueco and follow the line of sloping pockets and huecos directly up. For full value try to avoid using holds to either side of the interesting sloping features. This line is okay but awkward and short.
routes.Location: Found on the lake-facing side of the Crow Boulder, just to the right of Chrome Blackbird. The vertical line of sloping huecos is very obvious.

routes.name: Knife
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb up the arete and top out, pretty simple, lean left with body weight.
routes.Location: the start is by two small pockets

routes.name: Wildebeast
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Incredibly hard first compression move to a right bump, move body over right lip and back, finishing over the rounded face.

routes.name: Albatross
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: SDS, LH rail, RH crack, climb to the left and top out. Bring a spotter for the possibly big swing out.

routes.name: Fatman
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb only on the arete on the right side, the ledge/flake on the left is out.

routes.name: Fatman left
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: SDS in the middle with the bottom of the left flake and a crimp on the right, climb up the middle of the boulder.

routes.name: Flip It To An Undercling
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: SDS. Good edges and a cool ring hold/pocket lead up the face.

routes.name: Bruce Trail
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climbs around the good pockets on the cliff-facing side of the Roger '99 Boulder. Good landing; fun.

routes.name: Rabbit in the Moon
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: SDS right and low on a softball sized pod and move left across pockets and edges to topout on Tyson

routes.name: Tyson
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: SDS. Powerful moves on the left angled arete and wall

routes.name: Emerald Allsorts
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A real gem. Boat start. From the obvious jug at the bottom of the shield make some tricky long moves to gain the flatty jugs below the roof, turn this on good holds to gain the hanging groove. NB you can step right at the ledge for any of these routes to do 10 feet of about 5.6 to the very top.
routes.Location: Boat start.

routes.name: Jellification Nation
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Nice moves up the little arete lead to a rockover, before stepping left to gain the hanging groove. The height is no where near 60 ft, as per the 5.7 description, it's 35 ft max. Also, the climbing is pretty safe, and you don't need to jump in to get down
routes.Location: Same start as the existing 5.7 - you can also access this by climbing in from the R.

routes.name: Lugia's Aeroblast
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This route begins on a ledge right near the water. This is where you get off your boats and begin climbing. You will first traverse to the far left of the face. Then you will proceed upwards. At around 15 feet you get to a nice ledge where you can plan the rest of the climb here, you can take a left and climb a dihedral, I did not climb this one. I went straight up and climbed the small crack that is directly under a tree. There are some loose rocks to the right of the overhanging tree. This rock has been climbed before so there are sure to be routes that others have done.
routes.Location: Lake of Bays, Ontario, Canada. The start is a ledge you can just step right off of the boat and chalk up. The only safe way to descend is jumping of the ledge by the tree. You COULD keep going to top out after the ledge near the tree. But this is very dangerous for if you fall you will hit the ledge. The most obvious landmark is the large dihedral and a overhanging tree. These are commonly called by locals the 'dorset rocks' and the 'dorset jumping rocks' as it is a very popular destination to jump from.
routes.Protection: NO PROTECTION. This is DWS, falling will result in you scraping down the rock unless you manage to push off.

routes.name: American Bucks
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Namesake of the wall. Rappel to the hanging belay in a corner and move right to the arete and up on good holds.

routes.name: Ascent to Olympus
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rappel to the same hanging belay as American Bucks. Climb the crack/corner on the left to a face with bolts.

routes.name: Curses from the Underworld
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This route is accessed by rappelling at a gulley at the left side of this crag to a ledge. This is an offwidth crack requiring large gear.

routes.name: Dandy
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rappel down just east (climber's left) of Kiss of the Spider Woman to the hanging belay.  There is a "D" painted in blue at the top of the cliff to locate this climb. Take the mostly vertical face up from the hanging belay.

routes.name: Global Warming
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Starting on the same ledge as Curses from the Underworld, this route moves right and finishes near the left side of Dandy.

routes.name: Kiss of the Spider Woman
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The other namesake and classic line on this wall. Rappel down to the hanging belay, located by the letters "KS" painted at the top of the cliff.

routes.name: White Fang
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This is the furthest west (climber's right) route on this wall. Rappel down to the hanging belay and climb the thin face to the top.

routes.name: Bananarama
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This two pitch aid line consists of thin gear and hooks and starts at a hanging belay under the B-52 overhang.
routes.Protection: Hanging belay, thin gear and hooks

routes.name: Forever Expired
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This is the obvious route that goes up under the B-52 overhang. Long and varied climbing leads to the top of the cliff. This route was done by Sonnie Trotter in 2004 and is the hardest route in Ontario.

routes.name: Banshee
routes.FA:: Reg Smart, David Smart, Quinton Bennet
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The namesake of the cliff for a reason, this great trad line takes a thin crack to some face climbing, finally finishing with a hand crack to the top of the cliff.  There is a painted "B" at the top of the cliff to mark the top of this climb.
routes.Protection: Fingers and hand sized gear. There may be a pin and/or a bolt after the finger crack at the start of the face climbing.

routes.name: Light 'n' Easy
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb to a ledge, clipping two bolts along the way; take the obvious crack from the ledge to the top of the cliff plugging gear along the way.

routes.name: My Left Foot
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb a corner up to a roof and then to the top.

routes.name: Nash the Slash
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Brutal offwidth!

routes.name: Unnamed
routes.FA:: Reg Smart, Dave Suda, R. Stracken
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rappel to the hanging belay and climb the face to the top.

routes.name: Crescent Moon
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The other namesake of this wall climbs, you guessed it, the crescent-like feature that is bookended by face climbing at the beginning and end of the climb.
routes.Protection: Mixed - some medium gear for the arching crack and draws for the bolts on the face.

routes.name: Crucible
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start from the ground near a very large tree and move into a corner where you stem to the top.

routes.name: Everything is Illuinated
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start the same as Not Another Mantle but go even further left, eventually moving through bulges to a high crux.

routes.name: Flying Squirrel
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Hard movement on great rock, excellent line.

routes.name: Ghost and the Darkness
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Another tough line on this wall, this thin route takes on some dark rock to the top.

routes.name: Lord of the Flies
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: One of the namesakes of the cliff, this line starts just behind a large dead tree and climbs up the streaked face.

routes.name: Not Another Mantle
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start up the large tree mentioned in the description for Crucible and take the bolt line that heads up and left (gold hangers).

routes.name: Open Project
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Open project.

routes.name: Pow Wow Highway
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Linkup - Start the same as for Everything is Illuminated and move far left to meet up with The Victim at its second bolt and take it to the top.

routes.name: Project
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Also a hanging belay, this project is expected to be in the 12+ range. Rappel from the top, east (climber's left) of The Victim.

routes.name: Unnamed
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb up starting on the arete just right of an offwidth.

routes.name: Unnamed2
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Another route without a name on this cliff. *This route does not currently have an ending.*

routes.name: The Victim
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The only hanging belay on the wall but an excellent line. Rappel from the top of the cliff at a jut-out to the hanging belay and move up on long moves.

routes.name: Big Ben
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rappel to the hanging belay and climb the face to the top.

routes.name: Cave Route
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rappel to the hanging belay and clip bolts and fixed threads to the "cave" then move out to the top.

routes.name: Change of Season
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The only trad route on the wall, climb the crack climber's left of Big Ben to steeper climbing near the finish.

routes.name: Cumulus
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The namesake of the crag, this excellent line contains face climbing to an overhang with better holds. Rappel from the top at a blue painted "C" at the cliff top that marks the top of this climb.

routes.name: Distant Thunder
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rappel just east (hiker's right) of Cumulus to climb the face that ends with a roof near the top.

routes.name: Pirates
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rappel from trees at the west (hiker's left, climber's right) end of the lookout at the cliff top.

routes.name: Shakedown
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rappel down just west (hiker's left) of Cumulus and climb up to an awkward roof then to the top.

routes.name: The Wax Bomb
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rappel just east (hiker's right) of Pirates to the hanging belay; climb past the thin start to find easier climbing to the top.

routes.name: American Dream
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This route starts 50 feet east (climber's left) of the Latvian Ledge area and contains several boulder problems up a varied face.

routes.name: Azrael
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rappel just past Latvian Ledge to a one bolt belay or do the direct start which is Gargamel & Azrael. Climb the crack to the top.

routes.name: Bandit
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rappel to a hanging belay on the white face and climb a brutal thin face.

routes.name: Bareback
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Dynamic climbing through several boulder problems marks this good climb that goes up just left of The Fin.

routes.name: Blue Bottle Chronic
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rappel from the top of the cliff approximately 250 feet past (east of) the Latvian Ledge area to the hanging belay and climb the face to a roof.

routes.name: Bottlerocket
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
