routes.Description: Rappel to the hanging belay and climb the face to a small roof.

routes.name: Brokeback Mountaineer
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start as for Bareback but move left after the first bolt. Climb the right side of the roof and move through the interesting finish.

routes.name: The Fin
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The namesake of this cliff and a popular line moves through an obvious black fin feature up through interesting moves near the top.

routes.name: Gargamel & Azrael
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This line is the direct start to the trad line Azrael and adds some difficult movement at the beginning on a bolted steep face. Bring small to medium gear for the Azrael crack finish.

routes.name: Glass Bottom Boat
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb over fragile rock to a bolt and continue on a crack to a roof.

routes.name: The Golden Hour
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start just to the left of Brokeback Mountaineer at the far left side of the choss ledge. Easier climber brings you to a no-hands rest where you can ponder the harder arete to come.

routes.name: Project
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Project

routes.name: The Scenic Route
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start as for Brokeback Mountaineer but take the roof straight on and move left through a wandering route to the top.

routes.name: To Catch a Thief
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rappel from the same point as Bandit but use the hanging belay just to the east (climber's left). Another thin line.

routes.name: Death by Spanking
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The next lookout east (off of blue trail) of For the Gallery; rappel to a ledge. Climb the slick slab.

routes.name: Fish in a Barrel
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This line climbs just left of Turbine off of the same ledge. The rock may be of poor quality.

routes.name: For the Gallery
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Hanging belay. Rappel next to the lookout near the blue trail. It is also possible to climb from the base to a loose ledge to the hanging belay. Edit: This route now starts from the ground in a dihedral feature. It moves left out of the roof and a tough mantle gets you into the meat of the route as it was climbed from the original hanging belay.

routes.name: Grey Matter
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Located just east (climber's left) of Lioness. This steep route starts from the ground and rises to a grey vertical face.

routes.name: Headspace
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Hanging belay. The route that the wall is named after. This route is just west of where the approach trail meets the cliff edge. Rappel down the black face to the hanging belay anchors.

routes.name: Jack in the Pulpit
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Located just east (climber's left) of Headspace. Rappel to a ledge and climb a crack system to a feature known as the pulpit.

routes.name: Lioness
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Starts from a ledge just east (climber's left) of Jack in the Pulpit.

routes.name: Long Weekend Ledge
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This line climbs out the left side of the ledge that that Fish in a Barrel and Turbine start on.

routes.name: The Mentor
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This route climbs from the ground with a tough boulder problem followed by more hard climbing. The route eventually merges right into Soul to Soul.

routes.name: Project
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Project - difficulty unknown

routes.name: Pushin' Up Daddies
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This route climbs from the ground near Soul to Soul, up a loose corner to more solid rock up higher.

routes.name: Scooped
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This is a mixed line that is located climber's left of Pushin' Up Daddies.

routes.name: Soul to Soul
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Hanging belay. Rappel to the hanging belay about 100 feet east (climber's left) of where the approach trail meets the cliff. Tough movement right at the start.

routes.name: Tooth Brush Was Not Enough
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This is the furthest route to the east (climber's left) of the Headspace Wall. This line climbs a corner and may stay wet until late in the season.

routes.name: Turbine
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This climb is located just east (climber's left) of Death by Spanking. The climb starts on a ledge with a tough boulder start.

routes.name: Zulie's Trad Line
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Zulie's Trad Line

routes.name: 45th Parallel
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the steep overhanging dihedral feature left of Man Overboard.  This good new route will get even better after it sees a few ascents.

routes.name: Cracker
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rappel just east (hiker's right) of Pickpocket to a hanging belay at a tree and one bolt. Climb the face and crack (fingers to hand sized gear optional) to the top.
routes.Protection: Hanging belay at tree and one bolt, fingers to hand sized gear optional for crack.

routes.name: Elmo in Space
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climber's left about 150 feet from Man Overboard, this line starts in a corner and moves left to a bolted face. Use gear in the corner to gain the bolted face.

routes.name: Free Peltier
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the ramp and clip the bolts furthest up the ramp, climbing up to a roof. Traverse over to Niagro and finish up there.

routes.name: The Honey Badger
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Just left of Skunks do it in Tevas, climb the face to the top.

routes.name: Lady Slipper
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The namesake of this crag and a classic line, start on the right side of the ledge and climb the face to an easier finish.

routes.name: Magica Moment
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Just left of Ladyslipper, a tough start leads to easier climbing.

routes.name: Man Overboard
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: At the left end of the ledge, climb the techy arete to a face.

routes.name: Master! Teach Me the Secrets of the Universe
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: About 250 feet left of Kung-Fu, this line heads up the cliff with technical movement. Be careful on the fragile rock at the beginning of the climb.

routes.name: Mixed Martial Arts
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Ascend the large corner system just right of "So You Think You Know Good Kung-Fu." The climb starts with stemming and face climbing past several well spaced bolts, which can be backed up with some gear.  After reaching a stance, the corner kicks back to a gentle overhang and the crack climbing begins in earnest.  Jam, layback, and smear your way up the next 10 meters to arrive at the chains.
routes.Location: Just right of "So You Think You Know Good Kung-Fu."  About 25 meters left of the Ladyslipper Ledge.

routes.name: N.N.
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Between Magica Moment and Slugmeisters

routes.name: Niagro
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Just left of Free Peltier, climb fragile rock at the start (careful!) to a rest, finally heading up and left to the anchors.

routes.name: Pickpocket
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rappel to a hanging belay about 300 feet east (hiker's right) of a lookout. Climb up the slabby short face.

routes.name: Skunks Do it in Tevas
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the arete to a technical ending near the top.

routes.name: The Slugmeister's High-Techs
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start from the left side of the ledge and climb through long moves to an easier finish.

routes.name: So! Maybe You Need a Lesson in Humility!
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Just left of Kung-Fu, this route has a lot of fragile and very loose rock at the beginning.

routes.name: So! You Think You Know Good Kung-Fu?
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: About 75 feet climber's left from Elmo in Space; climb the right side of the wall up this continuous route. *60m rope required.

routes.name: Burn Judy Burn
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The left line on the boulder and the hardest of the three contains some nice climbing.

routes.name: Ring of Fire
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This is the middle line and is also the longest of the three.

routes.name: Unnamed
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The rightmost line on the boulder; not quite as good or as hard as the other two lines but still worth doing.

routes.name: Anarchy in High Heels
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start as for Fleet Footed Rock Moose but take the left bolt line up.

routes.name: Bart's Yellow Scooter
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This route starts from the far right side of the ledge and moves through tough technical climbing to the top.

routes.name: Busted at Berford
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start as for Lefty but move up and right up the slightly slabby line.

routes.name: Curveball
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The left of the two cracks, this great trad line moves up and left to the top.
routes.Protection: Fingers to hand sized trad gear.

routes.name: Doggies Dirt Pipe
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The furthest left bolt line on the ledge is this very nice varied route.

routes.name: Fastball
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The right of the two cracks, this often-wet route is nice when dry.

routes.name: Fleet Footed Rock Moose
routes.FA:: Reg Smart, Marc Bracken, Fraser Allen
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A nearly vertical line that starts left around the corner from Plastic Victory.  Stick to the right bolt line to the top.

routes.name: Green Velvet
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Another off-vertical route that's a bit tougher; climb flakes to a roof and then take it to the top, ending at the same anchor as Anarchy in High Heels.

routes.name: Lefty
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The first bolt line to the right of the rappel dihedral, this route starts by climbing the corner and then moving up the face.

routes.name: Plastic Victory
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Similar to Surf Zone, this route climbs just left of it and also goes up slabby rock to the top.

routes.name: Project
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start the same as Bart's Yellow Scooter but go straight up through hard moves (instead of moving right as you do on Bart's).

routes.name: Sargasso Sea
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Just left of the two cracks, this route climbs the difficult looking slab.

routes.name: Shorty Long Back
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Just left of the ledge proper, climb up to a roof and finish on a flake. As with many routes, be careful on the starting chossy rock.

routes.name: Surf Zone
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The first bolted line to the left of the rappel corner, this is one of several decent slab lines that moves through some bulges on lower angle rock.

routes.name: Viva From the Ground
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A variation/link up that climbs Viva La Difference from the ground, start on Shorty Long Back and merge right into Viva La Difference.

routes.name: Viva La Difference
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: About 80 feet past (east) of the rappel for the Latvian Ledge area proper, rappel to a hanging belay and climb the seam and face to the top.

routes.name: Zone of Extreme Beauty
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the line of bolts just right of the two cracks, Fastball and Curveball, and search your way to the top.

routes.name: The Cave of Despair
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This route is located in a hallway/cave on the way down to the beach from the Titan Wall. This desperate looking line is usually wet.

routes.name: Cold Hard Beach
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The leftmost route on the Life's a Beach Boulder, take the bolt line up and left to the roof and follow it around to the right and the anchors.

routes.name: LoreLai
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: An amazing climb in a spectacular position right above the water. Climb up easy terrain and clip the first bolt. A long draw here will keep the rope off the lip. Traverse out on jugs, until you can gain the large, granite-esque flake in the roof. Get a quick rest at the top of the flake, then bust a big move out right and launch into the crux boulder problem. Keep it together on the top, and reward yourself with a nice dip in the lake. - Tony Brengosz

routes.name: Madagascar
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This is the rightmost line on the boulder.

routes.name: Open Top Rope Project
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This steep toprope project heads up the underside of the wall on the side of the boulder facing the water.

routes.name: Barking Baritones
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Left of Stinger and not quite as good.

routes.name: Feeling Dolomighty
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rappel at the east (hiker's right) side of the area at the top of Scalphunter to the hanging belay. It's also possible to climb the first part of Limestone Cowboy to get to the hanging belay. Climb up while moving right to a cool finish.

routes.name: Feeling Groovey
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rappel at the east (hiker's right) side of the area at the top of Scalphunter to the hanging belay. It's also possible to climb the first part of Limestone Cowboy to get to the hanging belay. Climb the channel to a wide crack.

routes.name: Gateway to the Vertical World
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the line on the left end of the ledge and move through continuous climbing to a crux through pockets.

routes.name: Limestone Cowboy
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This route starts where the Stinger gulley descent ends at the bottom of the cliff. Start in a dihedral, eventually moving up and right to wander to the top.

routes.name: The Mandate
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The furthest right route on the dark colored rock, this is a quality route except for the dihedral near the beginning.

routes.name: Paradigm Shift
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The leftmost climb on the dark rock contains some nice face climbing and starts in a dihedral.

routes.name: Quantum Utensils
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Middle route on the black rock, take the blank face to the top.

routes.name: Scalphunter / Stinger Descent Area | 7302
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Move up the rightmost bolted line on the ledge to the top of the cliff.

routes.name: Spirit in the Sky
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The middle line on the ledge, this interesting line moves up the face to the top.

routes.name: Stinger
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This route starts most of the way down the descent gulley just before the final hand rope. Short but nice.

routes.name: Big Kahuna
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The first route to the right of the rappel dihedral, an extremely varied, extremely hard, and extremely thin route.

routes.name: Coeur de Lion
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: An excellent line featuring thin pockets, this is the second bolt line climber's right of the rappel dihedral.

routes.name: Dandelion Whine
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Slab start to steep finish, this line is the first one left of the corner.

routes.name: I Wonder Where the Hanging Belays Are
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb I Wonder Where the Lions Are from the ground to add one letter of difficulty.

routes.name: I Wonder Where the Lions Are
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A classic line with a hanging belay, rappel down 10 feet west of the rappel dihedral and climb the technical line to the top.

routes.name: The Lion King
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The furthest right line on the ledge, another very thin line moves up through multiple boulder problems.

routes.name: Lost at Sea
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb from the Lost at Sea ledge and climb the vert face to gain larger holds to the finish.

routes.name: Margin of Terror
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Go west (hiker's left, climber's right) from the Lost at Sea rappel corner to another lookout. Rappel down to the hanging belay and climb up and right before moving back left near the top.

routes.name: New Way Out
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Slab start the trends to the left, follow the left most set of bolts from here. Multiple pockets and small slab sections finishing on overhanging large jug style pockets.
routes.Location: Left most line on the Lost at Sea Ledge sharing a common slab start with Lost at Sea and Dandelion Whine.
routes.Protection: Bolted with fixed quick draw anchors.

routes.name: Thor
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rappel the same as for Margin of Terror but move climber's left to a different hanging belay. Climb the excellent vertical line to the top.

routes.name: Thor from the Floor
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb Thor from the ground to bump the difficulty to 13a by adding four bolts of difficult climbing.

routes.name: Making Waves
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rappel at the point described in the wall overview and climb this very nice technical route.

routes.name: Maneline
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rappel at the point described in the wall overview at a painted "M" at the top of the cliff. An ultra classic 5.10, this route climbs right from the hanging belay to the dihedral and then straight to the top of the wall. It is also possible to climb this route from the ground.

routes.name: Manelining
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start up the same as Maneline (clipping the first two bolts) and head up and left plugging gear along the way.
routes.Protection: Mixed - clip the first two bolts and then use trad gear to the top. A wire is required to cinch around a fixture bolt hanger.

routes.name: Pipeline
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A linkup of sorts, this route climbs Maneline from the ground, moving left just before the hanging belay, going through a couple of bolts on a bulge to reach more climbing to the top. This route ends just left of the fixture hanger on Manelining.

routes.name: Queue de Lion
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: **Note: It has been suggested that this route be removed. First 4 bolts are missing and the rock is actively crumbling up until halfway up the wall. Not safe for climbing and the cliff should be preserved. No longer the 5.8 it used to be.**  Rappel near the west (hiker's left) end of the wall to reach this hanging belay. Climb the slab start to a more vertical middle and finish on this excellent moderate.

routes.name: Intergalactic Cantaloupes
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A bit left of Melon is this line which consists of mostly technical climbing on edges.

routes.name: Open Project
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This partially bolted route is an open project on the left side of this wall.

routes.name: Shacked
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rappel near a white rock at the cliff top to get to this hanging belay. Start up a lower angle arete to a steeper finish.

routes.name: The United Colors of Melon
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This line goes up the black rock at the right end of the ledge. Balance and tech up the slab to reach the more overhanging wall above.

routes.name: In a Rut
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the crack just left of the ledge you rappel to.

routes.name: Merchants and Thieves
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Namesake of the wall, this mixed line starts down off the ledge of the previous routes.
routes.Protection: Mixed - bolts with medium sized gear useful.

routes.name: Renegade
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This is the leftmost sport line on the ledge you rappel to and climbs up a sustained face.

routes.name: Rum, Sodomy, and the Lash
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The best route on the wall, this route starts on the right side of the ledge and consists of very good face climbing. There is a large dyno at the crux that can be avoided by climbing a bit to the right (although it is probably easier than 12b this way).

routes.name: Summers End
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Just right of Renegade and rather similar to it.

routes.name: Attila the Hung
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A typical chossy start changes to a weird crack that leads to a roof.

routes.name: Breaking Dinner Plates
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Find the easiest way you can up the cliff, plugging gear along the way.

routes.name: Green Eyed Monster
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Best route on the wall. Climb the face on the right side of the ledge.

routes.name: Middle Aged Ninja Tortoise
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This gear line is about 30 feet right of the Monster Ledge and starts up a ramp before moving up and right.

routes.name: Mr. Moderate
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the short dihedral on the left side of the ledge.

routes.name: Nominal Nominal
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb up clipping one bolt along the way and then continue up the crack formed by stone blocks.

routes.name: Schools Out
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the face just left of the Mr. Moderate dihedral.

routes.name: The Troll
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This route starts from the ground just to the right of Green Eyed Monster and takes a small finger crack through a roof.
routes.Protection: Mixed - bolts and small trad gear.

routes.name: 30 years in the making
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Easy climbing to the crux move. Then reach the roof an go right to reach the belay anchor of reach for the sky.
routes.Location: ~6 meters right of windy city from the ground.

routes.name: Fancy Dancer
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rappel east (hiker's right) of Windy City to a hanging belay to climb this vertical route.

routes.name: Forgotten Dreams
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rappel the same as and start at the same hanging belay as Windy City but head out left through a short crack to link up with the bottom of Fancy Dancer.

routes.name: Mr. Bojangles
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rappel from the top of the cliff at the letters "Mr. B" to the hanging belay. Climb the bolted line straight up from the belay for a very nice ride.

routes.name: North Country Club Crack
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rappel the same as Poochy but move left to belay at the left side of the ledge and climb the overhanging crack.

routes.name: Northern Delights
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rappel at the point described in the area overview to a hanging belay and climb the corner past a couple of fixed pins before moving out right onto the black slab.

routes.name: On the Edge
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rappel at the dihedral with the letters "Mr. B" painted at the top of the cliff to the hanging belay. Move right off the belay to the bolted arete.

routes.name: Open Project
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Open project with a very blank middle.

routes.name: Otis
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rappel the same as Poochy but continue straight up after the 4th bolt to more face climbing.

routes.name: Poochy
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rappel from the west (hiker's left) side of the lookout near a dead tree to the hanging belay. Start on the first 4 bolts of Otis before trending right and moving through the roof to the headwall before the anchors.

routes.name: Sculpted Cow
routes.FA:: John Kaandorp, P. Zabrok, S. Worthington
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rappel from the cliff top at the painted letters "Mr. B" but go past the hanging belay to the ground. Start in a right-facing dihedral and move up the cliff. The FA did this in 3 pitches that went at 10a/7/10b with the pitch 1 and pitch 2 belays being off of ledges.

routes.name: Windy City
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rappel a bit east (hiker's right) of the rappel for Poochy/Otis/NCCC to the hanging belay and climb up and right to the top of the cliff.

routes.name: Windy City from Ground
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rappel past the Windy City hanging belay to the ground to add some more climbing without a change in grade.

routes.name: Above the Clouds
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The extension to Stratus Fear that bumps the difficulty a letter grade and takes the line all the way to the top.

routes.name: Aud Ball
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Move hiker's right from the rappel for Swashbuckler/Victor Mature/IWIWDIOJM and rappel to the hanging belay. Climb the short face.

routes.name: Buccaneer
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rappel the same as Nimbus but rap further down to a lower hanging belay. Climb a technical face to a steeper finish. It's also possible to finish on Nimbus for the same grade of 10d.

routes.name: Fully Erect
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The free version of the aid line Homo Erectus.

routes.name: Homo Erectus
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rappel through the hole at the top of the cliff and climb P1 (A1) to the belay stance where you climb the P2 (10c) crack and top out through the hole you rappelled in through.

routes.name: I Wish I Was Deep Instead of Just Macho
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rappel the same as for Victor Mature/Swashbuckler and swing left to another hanging belay. Move up tricky pockets to the top of the cliff.

routes.name: Mulleteer
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rappel the same as Buccaneer and use the same hanging belay but move left off the belay and clip painted hangers.

routes.name: Nimbus
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: An excellent line with great exposure climbing up the lookout. Rappel just east (hiker's right) of the lookout to a hanging belay. If the seconding climber falls at the crux they can be stranded hopelessly in space.

routes.name: Renaissance Man
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rappel through the hole to a hanging belay and climb the obvious crack.Located just down the trail right of lions head Lookout.Top anchor from trees is required.Area is heavy for traffic from hikers.

routes.name: The Roaming Gypsies
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rappel to the ground as for Maneline (or approach at the base). Pitch 1 starts in a dihedral on the west (climber's right) side of the Lion's Head lookout (10m left of Maneline). The pitch ends on a ledge to the left, Do Not Climb The Bolts To The Right In The Chimney! Pitch 2 starts with making a long traverse left, at least 12m. To top out on the lookout keep climbing left and up following the far placed bolts to the top. Be prepared for lots of hikers congratulating/asking questions as you pull over the edge.The massive traverse on the second pitch makes this route difficult, exciting, and a very mentally challenging climb for both leader and follower; a fall off this pitch by either will put you in space. Make sure you have ascending gear and know how to use it.

routes.name: The Sick and the Afflicted
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rappel 30 feet east (hiker's right) of Nimbus/Buccaneer/Mulleteer to another hanging belay and climb the vertical face with a steeper finish.

routes.name: Stratus Fear
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The obvious crack up the middle of the Lion's Head overhang. This route ends at the midpoint anchor while the extension Above the Clouds goes to the top.

routes.name: Swashbuckler
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rappel down 15 feet east (hiker's right) of the hole and move to the the west (rappeller's right) of the arete to the hanging belay at a stance. Climb through a boulder problem to get to the arete.

routes.name: Victor Mature Lives
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rappel as for Swashbuckler but stay east (rappeller's left) of the arete to a hanging belay. Climb up classic face climbing on thin holds.

routes.name: Anaesthesia
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Overgrown. Start up about 150 feet right of Time, the Avenger to climb the crack.

routes.name: Boiling a Dust Speck
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Overgrown. Near the far right end of the cliff, climb the crack to the top.

routes.name: Katrina and the Waves
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This route starts from the ground a couple hundred feet climber's right of the B-52 overhang and heads up the arete with technical movement.

routes.name: Medalta
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Starts up just right of Katrina off of a ledge to move up the face.

routes.name: North Shore
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This route starts from the ground but can be identified by the letters NS on top of the cliff. Face climbing without bolts.

routes.name: Time the Avenger
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Overgrown. Start about 200 feet right of Transition or Transgression to go up a thin crack through an overhang.

routes.name: Transition or Transgression
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start from the ground and layback your way to some hard movement.

routes.name: Twenty Feet in Tennessee
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Overgrown. Climb the roof at the far right side of the cliff. This is the rightmost route at Lion's Head.

routes.name: You Sweaty Hippy
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Another face climb that starts from the ground, might be a bit reachy.

routes.name: Howg's is M'Favorit Critters
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The first route right of the rappel corner, climb the face with a weird move down low.

routes.name: Looks Can Be Deceiving
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Just left of the rappel corner, top rope this face.

routes.name: Pigme
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb thin holds up the vert face.

routes.name: Pigroast
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Right of Howg's is this interesting route.

routes.name: Project
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Partially bolted line up the arete just right of the ledge.

routes.name: Gin and Juice
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rappel down just to the left of the ledge to the hanging belay and follow the leftward trending line of bolts to end straight up the face.

routes.name: Laid Back
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rappel the same as for Gin and Juice to the hanging belay just left of the ledge and climb straight up after the first bolt, plugging gear along the way.

routes.name: The Lighthouse Pitch
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Use the same start as Thunderball but trend further right through some huecos before moving up a right facing dihedral.

routes.name: Storm Warning
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A very nice trad line, this one starts in the dihedral at the right side of the ledge to gain the ledge itself. The second pitch takes the leftward trending crack to the top, ending a bit left of Tricks are for Kids. The first pitch is the money pitch and climbing one of the other routes to the top from the ledge is probably best.

routes.name: Thunderball
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This fantastic gear line is a great option for a second pitch to Storm Warning. Step off the right side of the ledge and pull an exciting roof, continuing up slinging threads and placing gear to the top.

routes.name: Tom's Lucky Bun-Day
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This is the leftmost line on the ledge and climbs through long movement to the top.

routes.name: Tricks are for Kids
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This excellent sport route starts in the center of the ledge and climbs nice pockets to the top.  It starts beneath a thread.

routes.name: Summer Residence
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Starts in a corner where you can stem and lay back on side crimps for 4 bolts (2-3 & 3-4 are slightly run out), then turns into an overhand with generally juggy holds. Bolt #5 is run out but it is just before pulling the crux of the climb. You then come around under the roof onto an awkward block. Come out into the upper corner, clip above the roof, then easy slabby climbing to the top (7th bolt halfway up). The bolting is spread out on this route. Fun climbing, but eases up severely after coming out from under and around the roof. Expect big falls on the 3rd, 4th, and 5th clips... Missing the 3rd may result in a ground fall. You shouldn't be concerned about the 6th or 7th clip as you've already pulled the crux at this point. The slabby climbing on the upper section is very gentle.

routes.name: Albatross
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rappel in just to the west (hiker's left) of a corner filled with some loose rock.

routes.name: Czar
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Probably the best route on the cliff, rappel just east (hiker's right) of the cedar trees to a 3-bolt hanging belay. Move left from the belay and then straight up to the top.

routes.name: Electric Avenue
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start at the same hanging belay as fuse box but climb the left bolt line on more pockets.

routes.name: Fuse Box
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rappel just west (hiker's right) of the trees at the edge of the cliff to the hanging belay and take the right bolt line up through a line of pockets.

routes.name: Laputa
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rappel just east (hiker's right) of Czar to a lower hanging belay than that of Czar to climb this very nice long route.

routes.name: Project
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Project

routes.name: Short Circuit
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rappel to a hanging belay from a lower area of the top of the cliff. Might have some loose rock.

routes.name: Swept Away
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rappel near some dead trees to the hanging belay and move left before moving back right to a seam to the top.

routes.name: Wired
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rappel at the furthest west (hiker's left) point before the cliff drops down a bit. Climb up and right then straight up the wall.

routes.name: Bat Man
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Spectacular line. Start as if for Dr. Zeaus but move left at the fourth bolt into excellent face climbing before heading up the huge overhang. Jump off at the top after pulling the roof.

routes.name: Dr Zeaus
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start up about 20 feet left of Drunken Master; head right after the second bolt.

routes.name: Drunken Master
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the steep left side of the wall and climb up to a hard boulder problem to climb another full 35m line.

routes.name: Far From the Maddening Crowd
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: An excellent line, rappel at the letters FFMC painted at the top of the cliff to a hanging belay. Climb the face at the start to large holds at the top.

routes.name: Hotel Bennell
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This link-up combines the bottom of Titan with the top of The Man in Me to create this difficult line. Traverse over after pulling the lip on Titan. This is tougher than Titan but is also given 14a.

routes.name: Lion's Head Express
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: One of the hardest lines at Lion's Head, start up about 30 feet left of Bat Man to go up this fantastic climb. Some of the holds might be very sharp. Jump off after climbing a bit past the last bolt.

routes.name: Living on the Edge
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on Drunken Master but head left after its crux to climb through exposed terrain over the massive roof of Bat Man.

routes.name: The Man in Me
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: An excellent technical line, this starts 10 feet to the left of Titan and climbs 35m to the top.

routes.name: Maxi Pista
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A great line that starts 15 feet left of Lion's Head Express, this line moves through multiple cruxes including one using a very small edge.

routes.name: Mini Me
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Another link-up that's backwards of Hotel Bennell, start on Man in Me and traverse just after the roof to the upper part of Titan.

routes.name: Road to No Depression
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start the same as for Dr. Zeaus but continue up the dihedral after the second bolt.

routes.name: Three Sheets to the Wind
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Varied climbing with two distinct cruxes. Great exposure.
routes.Location: Dihedral feature.

routes.name: Titan
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: One of the harder climbs at Lion's Head, thin face moves lead to more varied climbing and a difficult crux. Climbing to the midpoint anchor is 5.13d and the climbing from there is 5.13b from the hanging belay.

routes.name: Waveguiding
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Just left of the dihedral next to Man in Me, start up this long adventurous route.

routes.name: Baby Flatanger
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Baby Flatanger: The coolest granite pockets I've ever seen!Start up an easy corner to a high bolt left of The Seamstress. Up a slab with a pocket, left in a crack to the upper wall. Up underclings and sidepulls to the pockets and slab. Fun move to reach the anchor. NOTE: Still needs a final cleaning. Done by early October 2023.
routes.Location: Starts a foot left of The Seamstress.

routes.name: Bow and Arrow
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start by climbing an easy crack system with varied gear to a bulge.  Surmount the bulge directly or bypass the difficulties to the right on easier terrain.  Either way puts you at a stance below an imposing overhang.  Clip a bolt (the only one on the route), and make a long reach to gain the flake above.  Continue enjoyably up the steep flake to a stance, then climb up and right to the chains.
routes.Location: This route is located 25 meters to the right of Matt's Route.  It is easily identified by the large overhang with a beatiful flake.
routes.Protection: Standard rack up to #3 camalot.  Save a #1 for the flake, as I couldn't get anything else to fit.

routes.name: Caesar Salad
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Overhanging climbing to a dihedral, then more overhang to the anchors.
routes.Location: Furthest right bolted line.

routes.name: Desperado
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This route makes more sense as a single pitch, but there is a difficult move at 2/3 height directly above a ledge.  For that reason (as far as I can tell), there are anchors on the ledge so you can safely commit to the move off the ledge.Scramble easily up the slab, clipping a couple of bolts before the handholds get smaller and the feet disappear.  Delicately make your way up the next 3 meters before the climbing eases off and you can scramble to the ledge.  Either stop here to belay, or continue up and left towards the upper dihedral.  Fun climbing on steep rock past a few more bolts will deposit you at the anchors.
routes.Location: The bolted slab 75 meters right of the Seamstress.
routes.Protection: Quickdraws, plus a long sling for the intermediate anchors if doing as one pitch.

routes.name: Johnson
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: From the start, climb up a series of horizontal and vertical cracks ranging from finger to fist sized. Chains are located on the face right of a vertical crack.
routes.Location: Start is hidden behind some trees climbers right of Squirmish and left of Caesar Salad. The chains are easily identifiable from the top and can be top roped (use your own gear).

routes.name: McCrae Lake | 0833
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sloppy Pussy

routes.name: Muskoka Gold
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Short but awesome! Rappel access.Rappel in: Continue past the top of The Seamstress to the next rocky point that looks like a wave. Find two bolts beneath a bush 10 feet back from the edge. Build an anchor and rappel straight down to a 2 bolt anchor. Build an anchor and pull the rope. Pre-clip an extended draw from the bolt 1 foot back from the top of the route.Muskoka Gold: Climb the easy corner to short headwall. Good hold on the right and big move to a left-hand crimp. Slopers on the upper right rail. Compression moves to the top jug and pop over the top.
routes.Location: Easy to spot from the top of The Seamstress

routes.name: Pandemic Isolation
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Crux is low at the start with a second crux after the 4th bolt.
routes.Location: Climbers right of "warm up 11a" start in dihedral to the left of the arete at the end of this portion of the wall.
routes.Protection: 7 bolts plus anchors.Update: bolts have been chopped as of April 2021. Only anchors.

routes.name: Pandemic Restriction
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Route starts over water. Anchors installed for comfortable hanging belay. first bolt requires stick clip for safety. We don't want the climber falling in the water. 7 bolts plus anchors. Equipped  05/30/2020Large log moved into position under the start to avoid having to Rappel into the route or wade in the water. 06/04/2020
routes.Location: from the southern edge of the crag, follow the shoreline north to the end  of the first accessible wall just past "seamstress".At this point walk on a log to reach the belay station equipped with anchors for a comfortable stance.

routes.name: Pandemic Solution
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Should be a classic once it’s cleaned and seen some action. Recommend the use of an alpine draw on the hanger below the perma-draw to reduce rope drag.
routes.Location: Starts on the very end of the log anchored below the wall.
routes.Protection: 8 hangers, 1 perma-draw, anchors at the top.

routes.name: Pockets are Good
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The first half of this route is called Crackers (5.7 PG). It is protected with traditional gear which is good through the crux. The second half is correct (Pockets are Good, sport climb) but had an original grade of 5.9. If you don't have a trad rack then you can reach Pockets are Good by climbing the first pitch of Desperado.
routes.Location: Starts a few meters right of Desperado below a very shallow left facing corner.

routes.name: Seamstress
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: One of Ontario's best 5.12's.  crack, to overhang to slab climbing.   Packs a punch.
routes.Location: the biggest line, obvious.
routes.Protection: 10 bolts & anchors.as of 2016 the anchors on this route are rendered unusable. (Needs retrobolting)New anchor rings installed as of June 4, 2020

routes.name: Squirmish
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the right crack and follow to a ledge, climb a slightly overhung ledge to gain the first bolt, follow a rail up to the second bolt and place gear as you climb through the off-width to the anchors.
routes.Location: Shares the start with Bow and Arrow but moves right at the overhang.
routes.Protection: Mixed gear, standard Ontario trad rack to 3", couple of sport draws for the bolts, alpine draw might reduce rope drag.

routes.name: This is what you want, this is what you get
routes.Type:: Trad, TR, Mixed, Ice, 25 ft (8 m)
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start just right of the drip on good rock, move upwards on good rock cracks until it's possible to traverse left onto the hanging ice drip. The crux of the route will be moving from the rock onto the ice face. The mixed climbing might be bypassed by a talented (i.e. - skinny pillars) ice climber, or if conditions allow ice to form to the base.
routes.Location: Shares a start with the trad route "Johnson (5.9)"; which is on the adjacent wall, climbers right of "Squirmish (5.11-)".
routes.Protection: Standard trad rack to 3", standard ice rack.

routes.name: Unknown
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Short route up a corner and juggy flake.
routes.Location: Left of Seamstress, just before the water.

routes.name: The Warm Up
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: While this isn't a warmup for most, it's definitely a classic.  This route has a lot to offer: tricky face moves, juggy overhangs, and some spicy sections to keep you on your toes.Getting to the high first bolt requires difficult climbing above a bad landing.  Consider a stick clip.Start atop a boulder and make your way left with an interesting sequence below a roof.  Pull the small roof at it's left side to easier climbing up a quartzy dihedral.  A mantle move and a few bolts later, you'll find yourself below a larger roof.  Continue up through the roof, and establish yourself on the face above (crux).  The hard climbing is over, so gun it for the chains before the pump clock runs out!!  If you blow it here, you'll be going for a ride!
routes.Location: 20 meters right of the Seamstress below a section of golden rock with orange lichen.

routes.name: The Asshole That Killed Liberace
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!

routes.name: Deep Cheeze
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!

routes.name: Lucky Number 13
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.beta: Great climbing to a high foot crux on two crimps, obvious from the closely spaced bolts and clear crux bolt at your waist.  Pull hard to a jug. 13-14 Draws.

routes.name: Polish Warmup
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: One of the best 10's at Metcalfe, solid for the grade and a little run out on good holds.
routes.Location: Start left of Rigor Mortis and follow bolts through crack systems.

routes.name: Chantilly Lace
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Pumpy despite climbing on jugs for almost the whole way. Toughest around the middle, where you'll start climbing pretty far right of the bolts to take the easiest line.  7 bolts + anchors

routes.Location: In the corridor to climber's right if they are facing Good Fethas in the Metcalfe North area.Route is at the far end of the corridor on the left side if approaching from the anvil.

routes.Location: Slab start to the right of the overhang that forms the 'anvil". Left of the gully trial

routes.name: Strike Three

routes.Location: The only route on the main cliff wall, towards the back where there is a bend in the climber's corridor and it splits off to where pigeons hang out.
routes.Protection: 6 bolts to chains. The top bolt hanger is missing, creating a small run-out to anchors over very easy terrain.

routes.name: When I Put My Helmet On I Become Like Superman
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Beta: Crack line of epic proportions,  an easy start and a few pieces of gear will get you to the first bolt.  Solid laybacks to the small bulge traverse right on some crimps and smears to a decent rest and hold it together on a few pinches and pockets to the anchor.

routes.name: Zen Garden
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Cool: Classic 5.10, arete slapping, mega crimping climb.  Anchors on this climb were a botch job. Left bolt is loose and the quick link attached to it doesn't provide enough clearance to lower without very severe rope dragging.  Safer to rappel from this one, due to its steep nature and much easier to clean this way too.

routes.Location: The route is around the gully area of Golf Town, at the end of Metcalfe. The area is called in a guidebook 'The Land Before Time'

routes.name: Called Out
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: First bolted line on the ledge to the left of the ground-level belay station. Start to the left of the bolted line and moves up and right. Can be started from the crack with the tree but makes the start much harder. Shares the 5th bolt and anchors with 'Just a 5.6'*(starting from the crack with the tree makes the start easier and safer as the landing is quite treacherous)
routes.Location: Walk the gully trail to the left from the bridge. Look for the climbers trail that heads up the cliff to a small ledge just to the left of the ground-level belay station.

routes.name: If Your Day Ain't Going Right, Go Left
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Pitch #2 (final pitch) of an easy multi-pitch (Pitch 1 is Burrglass) - good to practice. Big traverse directly left.

routes.name: Iron Souls
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Another fun addition to the cliff in the moderate grade range. Enjoy good holds and consistent climbing as you work your way to the anchors.
routes.Location: Starts low on the left corner of the wall by the tree.

routes.name: Just a 5.6
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Located on the same ledge as, but to the climbers left of, 'Called Out'. Shares the 5th bolt and anchors with 'Called Out'.
routes.Location: Walk the gully trail to the left from the bridge. Look for the climbers trail that heads up the cliff to a small ledge just to the left of the ground-level belay station.

routes.name: Lack of Patience
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Description: Pull through a series of small bulges to an anchor that's shared with the route to the right, Man of Patience

routes.name: Man of Patience
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Pretty consistently pumpy route heading left as you go up. Will be hard to clean, better to have someone backlead.

routes.name: Monday's Child
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on a small ledge near a tree. Powerful slightly overhung start leads to awesome and varied slab climbing. Monos, thumbderclings, gastons, a little something for everyone.

routes.name: Wide Lover Not a Fighter
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!

routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Beta: Traversing start on good holds leads to fun climbing with some hidden pockets.

routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)

routes.Description: This relatively short route climbs an arete with some hard to find holds.

routes.name: El Camino Real
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Classic: Ultra classic trad line. Bring wide gear.

routes.name: Good Featha's
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Pumpy start on overhang leads to a good rest and then progressively easier climbing above. The bouldery start is more difficult since some holds broke off.

routes.name: Peak Season In Harlem
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This route has some tough moves down low but then eases up a bit around the third or fourth bolt. Mostly jugs up to the very top and then one hard move again at the anchors.

routes.name: Phoenix
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Starts roughly in the same crack/corner as With Boots before heading onto the left face under a bulge. Has a similar end to Peak Season in Harlem, though of lesser quality overall.
routes.Location: Climbers right of Peak Season in Harlem, just left of the crack system.

routes.name: Sir Od
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!

routes.name: Unknown gully climb
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb up easier moves on low quality rock to a definite crux and a difficult clip with a ledge awaiting below. Continue up and around a brushy cedar to an anchor out and right.
routes.Location: Route directly opposite Phoenix. Starts just left of the cedar up on right side of gully.

routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)

routes.name: Angelica Houston (formerly Meat Puppets)
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The first bolted line on the arete directly climbers right of Vox Angelica.This section of the wall was formerly TR only and was known as “Meat Puppets”. Gus’s guidebook added, “If properly bolted, this would be a worthy sport climb.” Now fully bolted, the route is described as having a reachy crux.

routes.name: Architecture & Morality
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This route offers an outstanding climbing experience, characterized by power endurance, excellent movement, and solid rock quality. Those who have completed it consistently agree that it's a top-tier climb. Alongside A&M and Vox, it stands out as one of the best routes at Metcalfe, worthy of inclusion on any 'Best of the Escarpment' list.Begin on a crimp beneath a rounded roof, making your way up an angling crack that trends to the left. Reach for a jug beneath a crack in a bulge, then tackle the first crux to access some easier climbing. Continue up and to the right to reach the base of a seam. Solve the crux sequence, then maintain your composure as you take on the upper wall.Like most routes on this wall, it often remains wet well into the summer due to seepage and humidity. But if it's dry enough, don't miss the chance to get on it!Additional Route:At the second bolt of A&M (A&M goes up and right), there is a new sport route that traverses left and then follows the weakness that would be Seepy Broken. The new route is Mergers & Acquisitions, 5.12d.

routes.name: De je View
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This route is hardest getting through the first three bolts on overhanging rock. This section will probably be easiest if you stay right. Enjoy easier climbing above until you reach a shelf of sloped grips. Easy from there again through the anchors.

routes.name: Decade
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Should almost be given a V-grade! Funky start moves gain a shake, then slap your way through the bouldery crux and cruise long moves on good holds to anchors.
routes.Location: Immediately left of 'Harry's Climb' 5.12a and right of 'Mother of Jam' 5.6

routes.name: Dynamic Duo
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!

routes.name: The Eagle has Landed
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!

routes.name: Industrial Accident
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Burly moves on overhanging rock for three bolts. Then take a good rest because while the top is more vertical, it is still pretty sustained.

routes.name: Kodachrome
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A trad climb that climbs very much like a sport climb!Climb up the inside corner to a good rest and build yourself a little nest of gear before pulling through the bulge. Climbing slightly left to the parallel cracks before getting to a good horizontal crack with some rest holds. Keep it together and climb through the waves of black rock to get to the chains. Woooo! Fun.This route can easily be set up on TR from the top of the cliff.

routes.name: Mergers & Acquisitions
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start as for A&M, then follow the line of bolts up and left.

routes.name: Shaven Yak
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Enjoyable and intriguing climbing leads to the first crux—a cryptic and fingery boulder problem that is rewarded with a heel hook rest at the lip. The second crux is both technical and fun, arriving just after the lip, before transitioning into easier climbing on the white upper wall.As with many routes on this wall, seepage can be an issue for much of the season, but when the holds are dry, seize the opportunity to climb!

routes.name: Sleeping Husky
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!

routes.name: Sponge
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start by climbing the first four bolts of Vox, then traverse left and continue up. You'll encounter a reachy clip at the fifth bolt, followed by an incredible V8 boulder problem that provides a challenging gateway to easier climbing before reaching the chains. The boulder problem alone is a high-quality, enjoyable challenge, and when combined with the start of Vox, it makes this route a must-try.

routes.name: Vox Angelica
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!

routes.name: White Hand
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Beta: This overhanging dihedral/ crack line is often overlooked for its neighbouring Vox but is not to be missed.  A few easier moves off the ground gain you a thin crack.  Start locking those fingers (taping will help), and good footwork will help you stave off the pump longer for the top section.  Climbs like a trad route more than a sport.  Crack takes good gear along the way if you're looking for a 12 range trad route.

routes.name: WHMIS
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Trend left for the first twenty or so feet to get over the bulge and then follow the crack up to the top.

routes.name: Air Togo
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!

routes.name: Boulder In a Briefcase
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Bolted alternate finish that moves up and right from the start of shower in a briefcase.
routes.Location: Start as for Shower in a Briefcase and traverse right at the overhang into a hanging draw then bolts. Finishes on its own anchor.

routes.name: Half Wits
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!

routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!

routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!
