routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!

routes.name: Shower In a Briefcase
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!

routes.name: Nauti Nana
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A meandering and fun little jaunt up the right side of the prow where the 60' jump is located.Start on the lowest of the series of ledges closest to the water. Start up to the right until you reach a short vertical crack and a mantle off to the right. This is the first "crux" (if anything on this cruiser could be considered a crux). Then walk left along the large ledge until you round a small corner and see another crack. Continue upwards to the right, passing the old piton and finishing by lay-backing up the crack underneath the anchor tree at the top. This piton and others that I have encountered at this crag makes me think that I am far from the first person to climb here. If anyone has any info on previous ascents please let me know!There is a 5.9+ variant where you stay left after the piton instead of bearing right.There are 2 ways up to the top and three ways down. You can access the anchor tree either by scrambling up the low cliffs a couple hundred feet to the left, or by doing some 5th class scrambling up to the right of Nauti Nana where some cliff jumpers have included some sketchy fixed ropes.
routes.Location: Midway along the chain of cliffs is a large corner protruding into the lake. The top of this is the hallowed jumping cliff. To the right, along the edge of the cliff there is a large, old, gnarled tree. This is what we used for a top-rope anchor.
routes.Protection: Top-rope off the tree at the top.  I have yet to lead this trad, but it seems very doable.   I have soloed this in the past, which was a pretty fun climb. I'm not sure whether this could be considered a deep water solo, as there are points where you would hit a ledge if you fell, but the water is very deep and safe on the whole.

routes.name: Jenny's crack
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This is the obvious crack just north on of Six Mile Marina on the north side of the peninsula.   We heard the name of the crack was called Jenny's crack from previous Top rope attempts.   Comes straight out of the water, but there is a nice ledge at the bottom where you can belay.
routes.Location: This is the obvious crack just north on of Six Mile Marina on the north side of the peninsula.
routes.Protection: Takes larger cams (#1 and #2) for the bottom roof section.  Smaller stuff up top.  2 bolts at the top belay.

routes.name: Banter
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Taking The Water Cooler and turning it into a power boulder that goes straight for the top. It has a very similar start to The Water Cooler but you need to ninja kick out to the left.

routes.name: The Water Cooler
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the right and work your way left across the bottom as a slab then go up on an angle to your right. This route is all about side pulls and balance.

routes.name: Air Jamaica
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A classic Oates route.  Steep and bulging, densely packed with hard moves like a Chinese cemetery.

routes.name: High Times
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Crank up some powerful moves at the roof near the start, quickly mellows out to a fun finish afterwards.

routes.name: Impact Zone
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Work through a roof using a crack in the wall to gain a roof, then another.  keep pulling hard on some steep rock and chains.

routes.name: Ira Mahn
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Classic climbing up steep bulges, thugy and technical.  Stick clip or die falling into a hole.

routes.name: Jello on a Spring
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Make a series of funky moves up the face to the bulging roof.  A broken hold will make you feel like you've been hit with a sandbag.  The roof move favours the taller, while shorter climbers will feel as if they are grasping at straws. Pull your way through and gain some easier finishing moves.

routes.name: Mahseeve Cool
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Classic Baldy sandbag.  Crank through some steep overhanging moves off the ground (crux moves) to gain a ledge.  Take a rest and enjoy the view, keep pulling some overhangs on typical Baldy-esque pockets to gain another large ledge, clip the bolt and rest again before tackling the last 15 feet of rock to the chain.

routes.name: Napoleon Complex
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Named after Mike Smythe.  Short but full of attitude. You'll need to fight for this one, right to the very end.

routes.name: Renaissance Man
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Like a renaissance man/woman, you'll need to pull out your many talents to send this long route. Great movement with 4 district areas of climbing separated by rests. The best route on the Air Jamaica wall.

routes.name: Solid Goldie
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Stick clip the first bolt. New route put up March 31, 2012. Follow the obvious crack to a shelf and big throw. Nice movement to a large flake. No hands rest before the crux. Pull the lip to top out! Burly!In memory of Bill Goldie (1952 - 2012) and his work putting up traditional routes at Baldy years ago.
routes.Location: Air Jamaica Wall

routes.name: Traverse Route
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Follow the layback flake to the bulge. Go left around it and a cedar tree, then continue up the face to anchors.

routes.name: Gridlock
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Good warmup. The face towards the bottom eases up more to jugs at the top. Jugs may be filled with water if it has rained lately.

routes.name: Indigo Girl
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Crux is down low and eases up a bit as you get higher.

routes.name: Jagged Edge
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Big moves on good holds.  Some small moves with sequencing.  Another...sandbagged Baldy route.

routes.name: Look Moe, a Poifect Coicle
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Crux is down low. There is risk of swinging on falls.
routes.Location: South end of the Alley, under overhanging rock.

routes.name: Neo Nautilus
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Pockets galore, but don't get your hopes up, they aren't that great.  Pull through them on some spaced bolts, and one particularly far gone bolt.

routes.name: Optical Delusion
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A medium route in the middle of The Alley.  Some of the holds seemed slippery and dirty.
routes.Location: Third route from the Left or Right on the cliff side of The Alley.

routes.name: Pins & Needles
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Long moves of decent holds, boulder around your local gym before getting on this one.

routes.name: Return of the Jingoid
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Short route doesn't look like much, but actually pretty enjoyable.
routes.Location: The first route on the pinnacle side of the alley on the right.

routes.name: Salieri's Revenge
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A few crux's and sustained from start to finish.
routes.Location: Last route on the pinnacle inside The Alley.

routes.name: Sneakers and Roids
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Follow the left line of bolts from a boulder stack.
routes.Location: Right side of the alley, very close to the Change Guy.

routes.name: Split Leggings
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Great route some tricky moves but easy to read.  Tricky traverse right to start.  Crux at top with 2 crimps.
routes.Location: Left of the entrance to The Alley.  Is the left most sport route for a little bit.

routes.name: Troglodyte Delight
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the obvious right leaning crack system until you reach a small cave. Watch out that you don’t kick some of the choss in the cave onto you belayer and continue up the left side of the cave to the bolts

routes.name: Undomesticated
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Great climb if your a solid 11 climber, some spaced bolting will make it heady otherwise.  Crux moves off the ground and a few great moves on the overhanging headwall.
routes.Location: Easy toprope access from Split Leggings.  Don't climb off the cold shuts, use your own gear.

routes.name: Who's This Change Guy
routes.Shared By::
Randy Von Zee
            on Jun 15, 2018

                             · Updates
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Follow the right set of bolts. Traverse right and head up on jugs.
routes.Location: Towards the right side of the Alley.

routes.name: The Anniversary
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Follow crack line right of French Revolution.  Place some gear at the end of it and keep it together for the rest of the non protected face.  Blow it and risk groundfall.

routes.name: Black Magic Woman
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Thin crack system left of French Revolution. One low bolt and one high bolt plus gear will get you to the top.
routes.Protection: Trad route with two bolts and anchors.

routes.name: Cobra
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Similar is style to it's counterpart Frenzy, only smaller and more technical holds.

routes.name: Conan
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A great climbing following a left facing crack system. Solid layback climbing. Unique climb different from any other climb at Baldy. Classic!

routes.name: Curly
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the obvious crack left of the dihedral. Tends to be wet in spring. No anchor.
routes.Protection: Takes great gear. Stoppers and single rack of c4’s up to 3 or 4.

routes.name: French Revolution
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb overhanging face past bolts and two pitons to anchors. Tricky Crux!

routes.name: Frenzy
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: technical face climbing with a redpoint crux before the anchors.  Small holds but good feet aplenty,

routes.name: Larry
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the chimney to a hand crack up to a cedar to an easier finish.
routes.Location: Obvious corner in Black Forest Wall

routes.name: The Stung
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A must do for a 5.10 trad climber.  Nice jamming through cruxes and a few fun steep moves in the upper bit.  It now has anchors.
routes.Location: Black Forest area.

routes.name: Unplucked Gems
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: "It's hard to say, it's sad but true. I'm kinda dumb and so are you."  Easier moves lead to a fun problem up high
routes.Location: Climbs direct along the bolt line right of Frenzy.

routes.name: Welcome to my Nightmare
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb 4 bolts to a large runout to the anchor or bring gear .5 and smaller to stitch up the upper dihedral.
routes.Location: Right of Conan, Left of Black Magic Woman.

routes.name: A Shot Across the Bow
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Old baldy CLASSIC. Incredibly hard moves off the boulder rock field.  Crank like all hell up the first couple of moves and start working up the arete while it gets steeper. Stick clip the first bolt if you like living, otherwise take your chances falling into the abyss.

routes.name: Amadeus Rocks
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Good fun climbing to a crux before the anchors. Solid 12, or cockroach to the arete on the left and make it an 11a.  Classic position either way you do it, puts on a great show for the tourists who will think your the coolest person around.  (Which you are)

routes.name: Bear on a Wire
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Short and Stout, great for boulderers and gym climbers. high first bolt and an inobvious move up high might have you falling back to reality.

routes.name: Elixir
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This is what it's all about, steep bulging rock and a stack of boulder problems.  A chew you up and spit you off crux awaits you.  All that training finally pays off when you reach the chains on this drink.

routes.name: Pig Labour
routes.FA:: FA John Kaandorp, Pete Zabrok FFA John Kaandorp, Steve De Maio (1983)
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Formerly a R rated trad route, now retrobolted with some shiny new hardware.Guidebook description: The overhanging shattered groove found in the south end of the Central Pinnacle. A lot of loose rock.If you’re all out of 5.10’s to climb this pile is ready and waiting for you. Stick clip recommended.
routes.Location: Climb is located on the corner of the pinnacle, between “Elixir” and “Amadeus Rocks”

routes.name: Pigeon Camera
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Shares the start with “No Refunds, No Crybabies”. A low first bolt protects a tricky start with a questionable hold, leading to a blocky flake that may also give pause to the leader. From here the bolted line diverges from No Refunds and trends right. High quality rock above grants fun movement on good holds with a bit of route reading.
routes.Location: Bolted line immediately climbers right of Elixir. “No Refunds, No Crybabies” can be TR from Pigeon Camera’s anchors.

routes.name: Beaver Fever
routes.FA:: Chris Oates, Bob Bennell, Tom Valis
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A low crux guards some of the best 10B climbing at baldy.  Stick clip the first bolt or bring a .75 cam to protect the start.
routes.Protection: bolts and anchors (0.75 cam if you please)

routes.name: Dyno Route
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: It is what it is.Throw amongst solution holds to link together the route.  Look for a glue in and that's your crux.

routes.name: Entropy
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start up in the black streak on some easy holds, a definitive crux at a small bulge hits like a brick.  Exposed and cool headwall is only mildly less difficult.

routes.name: Where's Waldo
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Do This Route.Beware the detached flake that has now teetered a few feet from the wall (it used to be a few inches).  A problem roof into a small dihedral and a whole lot of great moves and exposure.  There is a move up top that tends to chew up and spit out those seeking onsights. Be wary.

routes.name: Bagger's Jig
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Yet another sandbag on the crystal-like slippery holds of Baldy.  Keep it together for the sucker move.

routes.name: Crystalline
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Great climb if you love getting sandbagged, like most at baldy. Bring your headgame and prepare the crank harder than you've ever pulled on an 11C.

routes.name: Labour Pains
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Prolonged difficult climbing on some of the best rock around, crux at the bulge will have you fully contracted for a big move to a jug.

routes.name: Laplacian Ballroom
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Goes between slab to pockets with slight overhangs.  There are 2 very tricky sequences near the middle-top.  Where you need to figure out the right sequence to get through intermediates.  There are interesting fossils in the rock.
routes.Location: This is the left most route in the area.  To the direct left of a ledge with roots.

routes.name: Cemetery Polka
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on Exit Stage Right and follow the left bolt line to a few hard moves and anchors.

routes.name: Death by Lougin
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the technical arete to a shelf and a good shake out. Clip the fourth bolt as you enter the second crux and power through the fun moves to the anchors.
routes.Location: Located just right of Scimitar and climbs the arete

routes.name: Dirty Harry
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Almost totally hidden in the trees on the main wall left of the Scimitar (North) Pinnacle. Unless you know it’s there you would likely never find it. First bolt is quite high even for a stick clip.

routes.name: Exit Stage Right
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Kind of a sandbag for a 10B.  Since initially put up some holds have crumbled over the years.  Sustained for the grade.

routes.name: Penner for Your Thoughts
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Surmount a slabby face and traverse around a roof and crank through the next one.

routes.name: Scimitar
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: An awesome arete up high is protected by some cryptic climbing... and an incredibly high first bolt requiring some gear or a huge stick clip.

routes.name: Sweet Jane
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Easy climbing on pretty chossy rock in terms of protection.
routes.Location: Obvious crack to climbers right (south of) of the entrance of the North Pinnacle.

routes.name: Wild Dogs
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: In the gully of the Scimitar Pinnacle. Enjoyable climbing out of a chimney that leads onto an arete with a few killer moves before the end.

routes.name: Wild Thing
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: An old traditional route, done by linking Penner for Your Thoughts into Death by Lougin.Retrobolted into a sport route.

routes.name: Bionic Spaceboy
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Height dependant, one move wonder. Don't get your hopes up on this one.

routes.name: Blood n Valour
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A good route pretty standard 5.8 climbing.  Start was the crux.
routes.Location: The first route you see after descending the trail

routes.name: Christina's World
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: David Smart classic, no longer needs gear to get an ascent. Just godly strength.After the roof, follow the bolts to the right for the original variation.New, pumpier variation goes straight up after the roof through a series of pockets and approaches the same anchors from the left.

routes.name: Christina’s World (direct finish)
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb Christina‘s world through the roof, then follow the line of bolts going directly to the anchors.

routes.name: Climb Hard or Take In a Show
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Crux is low and a miss at the first two bolts may result in ground fall.

routes.name: Domestic Pressure
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Slabby at start but gets more steep and difficult. Traverse left after the third bolt instead of heading straight up.

routes.name: Domestic Pressure Direct
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Slabby at start but gets more steep and difficult. Continue straight up after the third bolt instead of taking the traverse.
routes.Location: In the gully on the left.

routes.name: Fallout
routes.Shared By::
Randy Von Zee
            on Jun 15, 2018

                             · Updates
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The crux is cranking the roof. Get over that and continue over some milder overhang to anchors.
routes.Location: Starts quite left of Domestic Pressure, but shares its anchors and the last 2 bolts.

routes.name: Libyan Democracy
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Originally headpointed by Chris Knight. Per the guidebook description, “A headpoint that, according to the first ascensionist, requires a few bolts to make it viable for future ground-up attempts…Perhaps someone will bolt this route so it can see some traffic.” Now fully bolted, the line begins in a corner moving up a section of very questionable rock til a roof crack encounter on more solid, but still dubious rock. Pull the roof crux and enjoy higher quality rock above to a second crux with some fantastic exposure. Fight the pump as you climb easier moves to the anchors.
routes.Location: Immediately climbers left of Christina’s World.

routes.name: Lougins Go to the Ballet
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Just over from Resplendence.  Shared first bolt with Bionic Spaceboy then move right to a cave.  To the mid route anchors is 11B, the full extension is 12A.

routes.name: Rabid Grannies
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Very hard between first and second bolt then progressively gets easier.
routes.Location: The second route a bit to the left of Blood n Valour.

routes.name: Resplendence
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Some loose blocks at the very bottom and solid rock for the rest. Great route with excellent stances to place pro. Bomber placements.
routes.Protection: Takes small gear + C4's up to 3 with creative placements. NO bolted anchor. Natural walk off puts you on the trail at the top of the cliff.

routes.name: Tiny Bubbles
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Pulling the final roof is the hardest part.  But it is not over when you get to the vertical climbing.
routes.Location: The obvious roof on the south pinnacle.  This is the sport route that goes to the left.  There is a harder route that goes right.

routes.name: Sink the Pink
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Kind of sketchy to fall on but really easy climbing. You can jump off the top into the water. A large tree near the top can be used to top rope this route.
routes.Location: To the right of the pink rock, traverse from the right and go up the slabby face.
routes.Protection: Water but there's some rocks sticking out.

routes.name: Mr Goose Direct
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Direct vertical line to the right of the bush. Start either on the lowest prominent jug or pull out of the water for slightly more challenge. It's not cleaned and is fairly dirty.
routes.Location: See photo. Directly right of the bush.

routes.name: Mr Gooses Wild Ride
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Pull out of the water near the right of the formation and traverse left then climb up right of the bush.

routes.name: Center Left
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A little harder than Left Light.
routes.Location: The second light, or left-center.

routes.name: Center Right
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Finishes at the jug below the 3rd light.
routes.Location: 3rd light from the left, or, Center Right light.

routes.name: Finger Lock
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The only problem here with a finger jam.
routes.Location: spot the small crack, where the mortar fell out, between the the two right lights.

routes.name: Jack's Arete
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climbs to the left of the left-most light up to the ledge using the cornerstones.Named for Jack Stewart who worked at the jail for many years when it still held inmates.
routes.Location: Just before the first light.

routes.name: Left Light
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Finishes under the left most light.
routes.Location: Left light.

routes.name: Bio Terror
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Route Info: Thin crimping leads to a rest before some pocket pulling and a finishing crux.

routes.name: Guantanamo Bay
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Route Info: Tech corner leads to a compression crux and anchors.

routes.name: Osama Bin Laden
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Route Info: Minimal sized pockets and fancy footwork lead to the upper section of this route.

routes.name: Poison Gas
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Route Info: Powerful roof pulling and solid 11b all the way through. Longer than it looks, pack extra draws. (Roof is missing 3 holds that were pulled off, the grade has been bumped substantially and lowered its quality.)

routes.name: Saddam Hussein
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Route Info: Cryptic route opener into straightforward sending and a solid crux.

routes.name: Sleeper Cell
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Route Info: Two crux's at bottom and top protect anchors, solve the sequence and save some for the end.

routes.name: Taliban
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Route Info: Solid enigmatic climbing, originally 10c.  Very sequence dependent.

routes.name: Weapons of Mass Destruction
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Route Info: A boulder problem off the start feeds into easier pulling with a runout to finish off.

routes.name: Daisy Cutter
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A harder start on an arete with some reachy holds with good rests along the way and some great movement.  While off the beaten path, it makes for a great day out along with a few other area gems.

routes.name: Definitely not a 5.6
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Great route, shitty rock.

routes.name: A Division of Sabre
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!

routes.name: Half Grade Diamond
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!

routes.name: Unleash the Power of the Pyramid
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!

routes.name: Tankini Time
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Overhanging DWS problem. Wide crack that goes from off-width to fists to fingers throughout. A good crimp 3/4 of the way is the only reason this goes. Good luck with wet shoes!
routes.Location: South side of Hole in the Wall. Obvious crack up steep clean face.

routes.name: Castles in the Sand
routes.FA:: Jeff McKeough A. Frith, Corey McCue July 2020
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Stick clip recommended as the crux is down low. After sticking the crux, make a couple delicate moves past the first bolt, the climbing gets easier from here. Slab styles moves with pockets bring you to a small ledge, using a finger crack on your right and some small holds on the left you can head straight up to the anchors or venture in to the corner on the right for a slightly easier although dirtier finish.
routes.Location: First bolted line on the wall.

routes.name: Crack of Time
routes.FA:: Corey McCue Jeff McKeough Dylan Carson July 2020
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start the same as Lichen and Vitamin D, then trend right to a small birch tree halfway up the wall. Move left around the tree to enter a left trending finger crack that eventually turns back to the right. Once back in line with the tree climb straight up on face holds to top out.
routes.Location: Same start as Lichen and Vitamin D
routes.Protection: Trad, BD .3 to #2 with option for some nuts. Shared anchor

routes.name: Just What the Doctor Ordered
routes.FA:: Jeff McKeough, A. Frith, A. Savoie June 2020
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Get your slab game on, crux is between 2nd and 3rd bolts. Some cool little features on this route
routes.Location: Currently the furthest right bolted line at The Sandbox

routes.name: Lichen and Vitamin D
routes.FA:: Jeff McKeough and A. Frith May 2020
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on some small flakes and blocky holds and move up to a significant horizontal seam. Pull past the seam and on to the upper slab with a varying fingers+/- crack that thins out near the top. Move slightly left to top out. (Shares bolted anchor with Crack of Time.)
routes.Location: First established route on The Sandbox wall
routes.Protection: Trad, mostly small gear (finger and smaller) with option of #1 or #2 at horizontal seam. Shared bolted anchor.

routes.name: Bashi-Bazouk
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sit start with feet to the left, hands matched in waist-high crack. Switch feet to right, left jug straight up. Side pull right on good edge, foot up on ledge, finish on jug about 10 ft up.Landing is sketchy (sloping ground and rocks, very small), topping out not recommended. Some holds broke during climbing but the ones on this boulder should be good now.
routes.Location: Short face with small landing on the southern end of the cliff.

routes.name: Banana Pancakes
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Lower down until the last ledge, and then do about 20 feet of face/slab climbing to another small ledge and then finish the climb with a finger crack (the crux). There are plenty of footholds around the finger crack, but it is more fun to just send the crack to top out.
routes.Location: Set a top rope on the furthest set of bolts to the left on the main cliff (these bolts are actually separated from the main cliff, but are still part of the main section).

routes.name: The Ryan Rooks Experience
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Pull a tricky roof and climb the sustained face to the top. The roof/bulge pull is the crux, but the vertical face climbing is pretty consistent.
routes.Location: When facing out towards the water from the top of the cliff, locate the furthest left set of bolt anchors. This climb starts below the relatively obvious roof bulge below.

routes.name: Dandy Blend Coffee Substitute
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start with undercling at chest height and the pocket in the crack at face height. Follow the crack up and over the lip.

routes.name: Golden Latte
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb up the dihedral.

routes.name: Kefir and Cereal
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Walk up the slab without using either arête. Top out between the two notches.

routes.name: Milk and Cereal
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Obvious easiest way up and down.

routes.name: Over Easy
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Up the steep side of the arête.

routes.name: Sunnyside Up
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Up the steep side of the arête.

routes.name: Vanilla Smoothie
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Same start as Milk and Cereal, continue straight up the juggy slab.

routes.name: Amazon Zombie Jail
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Good crack with better pro. Usually damp. Fall is best for gully routes.

routes.name: Creature from the Black Lagoon
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Slab to steep crux with not great pro.

routes.name: Forbidden Planet
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Techinical start leads into easier climbing.   Its location is much cooler than the climb itself.

routes.name: Mars Needs Women
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Seepage on a layback.

routes.name: Boom Shot
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the mossy slab then climb the face above.

routes.name: Boys Trip!
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The old project just left of Pacing the cage now established at 5.13c, Joe completed every guidebook climb at the Swamp with his send of Boys Trip!Per FA: Very hard to get off the ground and hopefully people don’t add cheater stones Maybe 13d? But we will go with c for now. Named after going on a bachelor party in Collingwood with my childhood friends.

routes.name: Chained Heat
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start as per The Fat Cobra or Liquid Plumber.  Once at the ledge, clip the fixed draw and plow through the boulder problem on the overhang.

routes.name: Cool Runnings
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Boulder your way up the beginning on black rock.  There is a slab section and crux before the anchors.

routes.name: The Fat Cobra
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Get on this thing. a beast of a route with every kind of move and a textbook photo finish.

routes.name: Ferratagate
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Shares start with Snakes and Ladders and trends Left.

routes.name: Gigajoule
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Highly rated climb that requires a full repertoire  of skill, Fist Jams, roof pulling and balance finish.
routes.Location: Right most climb on the Big Boy Wall

routes.name: Johnny Drama
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Hard crimping to start on a high first bolt.  Dynamic movements get you to a bulge.  Go right around the bulge to another crux.  Easy finish with a view.

routes.name: KPI
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: 12d if you ride it to the small cave and 13c/d if you take on the ensuing headwall above.  A V8 boulder problem guards some easier terrain until you reach the headwall and realize your too short to reach the spaced out holds.

routes.name: Left Hand of God
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Cries out to be climbed. Kind of like Liquid Plumber.  Just more splitter and go left instead of right.

routes.name: Liquid Plumber
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A thin crack that goes right. Can be linked into Chained Heat with the use of thin gear and some finger strength.

routes.name: Mac Attack
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Full value, Hard third clip and pumpy to the top
routes.Location: left of Gigajoule

routes.name: Nanotech
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the left corner which travels right.  Varied movements on difficult to see holds will make you feel hopeless.  Find the sequence and pull past the crux to a better section of jugs.  One last set of hard moves awaits you before clipping the chains.

routes.name: OB-1
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Difficult start with a sequence, miss it and get sandbagged. Easier climbing up top is guarded by a low crux.

routes.name: Pacing the Cage
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Bouldery start on hard moves gets easier the higher you get, pace yourself and save a little for a redpoint crux before the chains.

routes.name: Polynesian Town
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the corner then move onto the slab and then face higher up.

routes.name: Project
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Needs new bolt at crux.  Low percentage throw to a pocket will have you second guessing a fall on this manky bolt.

routes.name: Project
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: No starting holds and a critical loose hold up high.

routes.name: Project
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Anchors are in a loose block. Avoid until they are moved.

routes.name: Rookie Flair
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Alternative start to Cool Runnings, good for when the start of CR perpetually seeps.

routes.name: Shirts Off Posse (S.O.P)
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Steep awkward pockets to crank all the way through, a stiff 10C.  There is a hold on the right side that will alleviate the potential to be sadbagged.

routes.name: Snakes and Ladders
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Shares start with Ferratagate but splits right. Easy climbing gets more difficult near the top.

routes.name: Sumatra
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Epic line, stand in awe at the monumental task ahead on some great rock.  Start on the crack of LHOG and trend right.  Reach a bulge and do some hard moves, technical finish will keep you in the zone.

routes.name: Three Day Road
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Right veering crack systems. Good protection.

routes.name: U-Pak U-Suk
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Cockroach line for OB-1, may seem like a sandbag if you miss the hold/move. Perplexing for a 10b.

routes.name: Wish-A-Grip (Project)
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Big blank section of nothing.

routes.name: La Tache
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: "Located in a small clearing with 2 routes, this is the bolted route on the left side. Step onto a small ledge and up a short arete on the right. Sustained climbing on good holds. Belayers, be attentive of climbers moving to the second bolt." (from Ontario Rock Climbing: The Best of Southern Ontario guidebook)

routes.name: Mr. Pleasure
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Hard opening sequence leads to exposed steep finish
routes.Location: Right of Thunder Bunny

routes.name: Sunday Sausage
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Fun route that is unfortunately short.   Loose/Hollow rock above anchors.
routes.Location: First climb on the wall on climbers right.

routes.name: Thunder Bunny
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: "Start on a ledge/shallow cave and work your way up and left. Mantle onto a small ledge halfway up and work your way up the face. Sustained climbing on varied hold." (from Ontario Rock Climbing: The Best of Southern Ontario guidebook)
routes.Protection: 7 bolts to rap rings. Shares first 2 with Mr. Pleasure (12d)

routes.name: Where's The Money Lebowski
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb up the small mossy face, then head right up a nice ramp towards the far right side of the Dude Fin. A small traverse left then pull onto the ledge with the anchors twenty feet right of Sunday Sausage.
routes.Location: Located opposite to "Balls Deeper" on a small knife edge plateau.

routes.name: One Step Beyond
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Dirty as per all Gully routes. Hard start to easier top section.
routes.Location: left of Strictly Ballroom, hidden around a corner.

routes.name: Private Dancer
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Perpetually wet climb on solid stone.  Sprouter of many bail biners throughout the season.
routes.Location: Behind Dude Wall, descend into Yellow Card area then go right behind dude fin. First climb on left side.

routes.name: Safety Dance
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Awesome line on decent holds, pull the roof if you can get past the wet start.
routes.Location: opposite Private Dancer, rightmost climb of the wall.

routes.name: So You Think You Can Dance
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The big line right up the middle, stretched out moves will bump the grade for shorter climbers.  As with all gully routes, damp start.
routes.Location: left of Safety Dance

routes.name: Strictly Ballroom
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Fun route, poor rock.  Dirty and damp make this more of an adventure climb.
routes.Location: Left of So You Think You Can Dance.

routes.name: Waltzing Matilda
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Short roof to a surprisingly nice crack.

routes.name: Welcome to Shang Gorilla
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Wet, slimy and insecure. Off angle corner.

routes.name: White Russian (Project)
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sort of not really a route, put up but hard to piece together.

routes.name: Booty Duty
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: easy climbing to a crux at the anchor.
routes.Location: On the wall opposite Wobbler.

routes.name: First Ascent Folies
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Crack climbing into a roof.
routes.Location: Bolt line left of Hamsterdam.

routes.name: Hamsterdam
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Great movement on solid stone, Venturing above anchors will yield loose rock.

routes.name: Ridden Hard and Put Away Wet
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Good 5.8 lead, last clip can be difficult for some.
routes.Location: Start on Booty Duty and keep left.

routes.name: Scarlet Fever
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Solid 10d on a great piece of rock, perpetually wet however and seldom traveled.
routes.Location: Left of Alex in Wonderland. on opposing wall.

routes.name: Wobbler
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sketchy start on small regularly damp holds into easier more straightforwardness up top.

routes.name: Alpa Chino
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Couple of hard moves off the ground gets easier the higher you go, great holds on big moves and overhanging rock.
routes.Location: around the corner to the left of Hump the Honeys, inside the gully. Can't miss it.

routes.name: The Big Boss
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Line of epic proportion, easy climbing that gets progressively harder.  Pull the roof and gain anchors.
routes.Location: left of Curbed only climb on the wall.

routes.name: Curbed
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Steep boulder problem leading into Loan Shark
routes.Location: Left line of Loan Shark

routes.name: Easy 11a
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Thin opening moves and hard pulling with a tiptoe finish.  Short and stout.
routes.Location: wall left of The Big Boss.

routes.name: Face Taco
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Banger crux off the ground on some cool holds.  Gets easier the higher you go.
routes.Location: Opposite wall from Alpa Chino, Looks manky, but worth the time.

routes.name: Hatorade
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Pull easier moves on solid holds up to a small roof, gain some small crimps and move into a layback section.  Clip a long draw while on the ledge to reduce drag and pull some jugs to the anchor.
routes.Location: First route on the left side from the crevice.

routes.name: Hump the Honeys
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start of the arete, move out onto the face of the wall and follow the bolts.  Be solid on the first two clips, there is groundfall potential.

routes.name: Loan Shark
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Easy start with a 10c crux before the anchors.  Sweet moves, sweet rock. top notch.
routes.Location: right of the log staircase heading towards the islands main wall.

routes.name: The Oz Man Cometh
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Easy pulling gains you a few strenuous moves at a small roof, hit the seam and throw for a jug. Easier climbing up top.

routes.name: Pump and Dump
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Genuine one move wonder.  large dynamic move protecting much easier flake jug hauling. put a sling on the last draw to avoid rope drag then run it out to the anchor.

routes.name: Pup Star
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the face.

routes.name: Pushover
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Technical and somewhat powerful beginning, top section is much more dynamic and overhung.  Be wary of holds breaking from top section. Wear a helmet.
routes.Location: First climb on the right of the second wall.

routes.name: Ride the Lightning
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A bouldery start will get you to a feature shaped like a lightning bolt. Continue up the face and cruise to the chains.

routes.name: The Sausage Factory
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: One of the best 10a's around.  Steep and committing for the grade and not great for toproping.  Varied movements.
routes.Location: Right most climb on the first main islands wall.

routes.name: Blood in the Water
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!

routes.name: Business Time
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!

routes.name: Does Your Boyfriend Drive Too
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This route goes up the right side of a gully though you'll probably want to start on the rocks to the left because going directly up would be hard.

routes.name: Gerry Gerbil
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Goes diagonally left up a flake until shared anchors with hammy hamster

routes.name: Hammy Hamster
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Trend right for the first two bolts then a bit left until #4. Then head up to shared anchors with Gerry gerbil

routes.name: The Last Stand Wall | 6396
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Route Info: Wide corner on the left upwards to good rock and a crux.

routes.name: Man of Stihl
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!

routes.name: DDWM
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!

routes.name: Guns N' Roses
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Pitches: Pitch 1 - 5.9 to anchors.Pitch 2 - 10b to 3rd set of anchors.There is a chain anchor after topping out on the pinnacle. Use this as a belay for 2nd pitch.

routes.name: Pure Plaisir/Pixelated
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!

routes.name: She's A Tease
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.---: A few bolts to a thin crack.

routes.name: Unknown
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on back of pinnacle and cross over to a crack that goes up headwall.First pitch is an easy 5.9 and second pitch is 5.10a/b

routes.name: 12 Pump Chump
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A short and pumpy line on good holds that are consistently awkward to grasp. Good first for the grade if your a strong boulderer.
routes.Location: Rightmost climb in Sector 9, start atop a rock shelf higher than the rest of the wall.

routes.name: ASSMAN
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Hard, reachy boulder problem spits you out before the great 5.12 climbing above.
routes.Location: left of 12 Pump Chump

routes.name: Balls Deeper
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Short Off width. Go DEEP.

routes.name: Godzilla
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Follow the corner to a thin groove, follow up towards a scoop. Go right and up to reach anchors.

routes.name: Meat Wagon Speedway
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start directly below bolt #1, thin edges and thinner pockets.  Big moves in the middle get you onto an amazing headwall.
routes.Location: first bolted route coming from west end.

routes.name: Mothra (Project)
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Still needs some route finding, hard project route.

routes.name: Rodan (project)
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Seeping roof and minuscule holds have held off a first ascent of this route.

routes.name: Coma Girl
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Hard crimpin off the deck and some hard moves leads into a small dihedral. Finish  on some steep jugs.

routes.name: Liquid Brain
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start left of the first bolt, a difficult opening boulder sequence leads to a couple of more hard sequences and some chains.

routes.name: The Meat Grinder
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Difficult start with a few power moves gets easier towards the end.  Starting far left makes it easier, but will not be true to the grade.

routes.name: The Poppy Salesman
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Some cool layback moves gets you into position for a leftward traverse under a small roof.  Pull to the crack above and get you feet high.  Awesome finish.

routes.name: Sundial Wall | 9505
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Crux start gets easier the higher you go. Great climb.

routes.name: Timex
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Easy technical start and a mellow headwall.  Crux is before the chains.

routes.name: Turf Builder
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start in an easy crack and make your way up and out right to a ledge. The fun begins after as you follow the short undercling/lieback up the face.
routes.Protection: Large fingers to small hands. There's a fixed nut at the beginning of the crack. Anchors.

routes.name: Uno
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A difficult pocket at the roof on a reach move might have you questioning the grade if you're a shorter climber.  You might not even be able to reach the pocket if your. Easier climbing after that crux.

routes.name: White Rajahs
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Steep and Powerful roof to a nice crack system above.

routes.name: Alex in Wonderland
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!

routes.name: El Diablo
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)

routes.name: Shake and Bake
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Steep arete climbing eases up to more vertical above.

routes.name: Boom Boom Boom and it's Total Steep Sickness to the Chains
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: 5.6 to the first set of anchors.  5.11a to the second set.

routes.name: Bubba Ho Tep
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!

routes.name: Dreadnought
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!

routes.name: Full Brazilian
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!

routes.name: Hall of Shadows
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!

routes.name: Juggernaut
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Thin crimps lead to a decent shake, where a powerful and sequential boulder guards an easy romp to the chains

routes.name: King of the Beasts
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!

routes.name: Kitty Kalamity
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!

routes.name: Perplexidon
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!

routes.name: Watch Your Back
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!

routes.name: Chewie is my Co-Pilot
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Short and powerful, small seam cranking.
routes.Location: Rightmost climb of White Castle

routes.name: The Lucifer Effect
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on Swamp Thing and move right up steep technical rock.
routes.Location: Right of Swamp Thing.

routes.name: Swamp Thing
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Classic.  Pocket pulling to a rest, steep section to tough roof moves.  Pull to the headwall and throw to a jug at the anchors.  Big air will ensue if you miss, good belaying is essential.
routes.Location: Leftmost/westernmost route on the White Castle.

routes.name: The Weak and The Wounded
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Bouldering start guards easier steep rock.  Throw to a pocket and cash in your air miles if you miss.  Hold it down to the anchors.
routes.Location: Start on The Wookie and move left to anchors.

routes.name: The Wookie
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Steep climbing on good holds, leave some for the end or pump out. A good first for the grade.

routes.name: Balls Deep
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!

routes.name: The Down Low
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!

routes.name: Fortune Teller
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start left of Whoopee Cushion. Surmount the initial ledge and climb a nice slab boulder problem to fun jugs, and then find your way through an interesting final crux. Can get dirty due to runoff, but brush it up and it will reward you with super nice climbing and rock.
routes.Location: First bolted route left of Whoopee Cushion, and right of Carbon Stihl.

routes.name: McLovin
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!

routes.name: Whoopee Cushion
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!

routes.name: Invisible Sun
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Layback up a flake.

routes.name: King of Pain
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Awesome pocket climbing up the face.

routes.name: Message in a Bottle
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Up the face 2nd from left.

routes.name: Secret Journey
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Leftmost route, climb the face. Duh.

routes.name: Synchronicity
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the arete.

routes.name: Walking on the Moon
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start in the hole, go up and right.

routes.name: Abdul's Crack
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Offwidth with somewhat lack of protection

routes.name: Amazing Grace
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Fun and nice climbing on a slightly overhanging wall. Crux up top after the pump has set in.

routes.name: Edgy
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Face and arete climb, short but a good warm up.
routes.Location: Up a dirty ramp on the wall left of Corner Stone.

routes.name: The Fin
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the bulgy arete.5.11a if using the crack on the left (Good Clean Fun) as well.

routes.name: Good Clean Fun
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Enjoyable and fun varying crack
routes.Protection: Use top anchors of The Fin

routes.name: Heart of Stone
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Excellent corner crack, exciting layback finish! Classic!

routes.name: Ivory Tower
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on a ramp and clip the first bolt, work around the aerate back to the face and move up on a mono and a few good holds.  Make the next few clips and run it out on some easy terrain to the 2nd last bolt, the sooner you clip the last bolt the better to save yourself some strenuous clip later.  Big huecos to finish on the exposed overhang.

routes.name: The Jester
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Reachy moves on smaller holds than it's neighbour Ladder Face.

routes.name: The Ladder Face
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A few curved cracks and a triangular hold gets you to a rest with a ton of horizontals to the finish.

routes.name: The Loner
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Face climbing to an overhanging finish.

routes.name: Music at Work
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Thin holds on the first half guard a squeeze job up top.

routes.name: Open Project (Minifly)
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Large overhanging bulge split by a seam and small corner.  Still awaits an ascent. 2 bolts and a single ring anchor.

routes.name: Paleface
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Steep and powerful, bring that dead point strength.

routes.name: Poets
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on Spaced Out and lean far right, small pockets and hard moves to anchors.

routes.name: Sentinel
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Blank looking face/arete of a white pillar.  Difficult sequences on what looks like nothing.
routes.Location: Large white pillar with 3 climbs on it.

routes.name: Slap Happy
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: An arete with not much to grasp, desperately slapping your way to the top.

routes.name: Space Odyssey
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on a ledge and follow up the arete, move onto the face and use the pockets to gain ground.  A few slopers and pockets on the headwall to anchors.

routes.name: Spaced Out
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: 2-pitch sport climb, due to a large ledge and an incredible amount of rope drag it is advised to split this pitch in 2 so it doesn't feel like your dragging a truck up the overhang finish.

routes.name: Wild Orchid
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Thought provoking the entire way, lots of variety and a beautiful sequence. Classic!

routes.name: Whiskey's Traverse
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Cruxes are 1) before the corner when the feet drop out (depending on water level) and 2) after the corner when the rock gets higher and the face has less features.
routes.Location: Start on the West end of the rock (right when looking at the face). This is the shorter side and lower jumps. Climb over the side and traverse to the left as far as you can.

routes.name: Blood Beach
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This route climbs the left hand side of the right cave. It sort of follows a seam. It is one of the steeper routes on the B-MW. Makes a great warm-up if you have it wired or can handle a good pump.
routes.Location: Left side of the right cave. Shares a start with Pocket Rocket.

routes.name: Blood Sucking Pharaohs
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Pocket climbing to hard to see holds and then jugs at the top. It heads right after clipping the forth bolt. Chain anchor
routes.Location: This is the 7th route from the left. The forth from the cave. It ends at the right set of chains.

routes.name: Brain Damage
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This route diagonals left along the right side of the left cave. It ends at the same anchor as They Live.
routes.Location: It is on the outside of the right side of the left cave. This is the forth bolted line from the left.

routes.name: Bride of the Reanimator
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Boulder out the roof and over the lip to the ledge then work right around the corner and up to the anchor of Dollman.
routes.Location: This route is left of Dollman and starts on the right side of the right cave and works right to the anchor of Dollman.

routes.name: The Corpse Vanishes
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This route climbs up to a small arete then onto chain anchors. Pretty good pockets, but some are hard to find at first.
routes.Location: This route is the 6th from the far left. 3rd from the left cave. It ends at the left set of chain anchors.

routes.name: Creepers
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: One of the longer routes it ends on a right facing dihedral. It climbs past pretty good holds, you just need to find them. The section just before the anchors is a pile of hollow sounding blocks. Climb gently.
routes.Location: This route is on the wall between the two caves. It is the second on that part of the wall, just right of "Brain Damage" it is the 5th route overall from the left of the cliff.

routes.name: Deadzone
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This route is a linkup of sorts. This route starts on Blood Beach and does a rising traverse to the 4th bolt of Hitler's Brain. The bolts you are supposed to clip are supposed to be painted black but there might not be paint still on all of them. No stars because I haven't been on it yet.
routes.Location: It starts on Blood Beach and finishes on Hitler's Brain.

routes.name: Dollman
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The route is pretty much all huge jugs. The start traverses left then you climb up to the Supershut anchor. There is a direct start to this route that is a bit harder and climbs pretty much straight up to the anchor. 5.8 doesn't get much steeper than this. Much more solid than it looks! Sharp holds.
routes.Location: This route is just left of White Zombie and just right of the right cave. There is a direct start just below it.
routes.Protection: Three bolts and anchors. This route is a bit runout but the bolts protect the hardest moves pretty well. The first bolt doesn't do much but make it harder to belay. So skip it, either with a stick clip or not. There are two more bolts for the direct start.

routes.name: Dollman Direct
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This is the direct start to Dollman and bumps the difficulty from 5.8 to 5.10a. If you traverse in from the right, that is the 5.8 variation. A difficult start and pocket pull lead to jugs.
routes.Location: Direct start to Dollman

routes.name: Evil Dead
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Just left of Hitler's Brain and right of The Punisher. Might be a little tougher if you are short. No stars because I haven't been on it yet, it is supposed to be a pretty decent route.
routes.Location: 10th route from the far left. 3rd route left of Blood Beach.

routes.name: Hell on Earth
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This route is just right of Hellraiser and shares and anchor with it. Power climbing on pretty good holds for the most part. Don't stray to far left and onto Hellraiser around the third bolt when the holds get small for a couple moves.
routes.Location: On the left side of the left cave.

routes.name: Hellbound
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This route is an extension to Hellraiser. There is a bouldery move to get the lip of the roof. It is a bit harder since a hold broke. The grade listed is approximate.
routes.Location: Climb Hellraiser and keep going up through the roof.

routes.name: Hellraiser
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: One of the routes often used as a warm-up. There is a big left facing flake thing about 1/3 to half way up.
routes.Location: This is the furthest route to the Left. It starts just left of the left cave and climbs along the edge of the cave.

routes.name: Mars Need's Women
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A hard start then bigger pockets lead up to a big block/shelf. Climb along the left edge of the block/shelf and through the small awkward roof to bigger holds above.
routes.Location: This is the second bolt line left of Blood Beach. There is an oddball bolt under the ledge/shelf that often confuses people. That bolt is the third bolt of Deadzone. This route is the 12th from the left end of the cliff.

routes.name: Pocket Rocket
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Big holds separated by big moves on steep rock. Shares a start with Blood Beach but heads right at third bolt and the shelf.
routes.Location: Starts on the left side of the right cave. Shares a start and first three bolts with Blood Beach and then climbs just to the right of BB.

routes.name: The Punisher
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Pretty decent holds with bad feet. Powerful. Mostly pockets. This route is probably the first or second route you will see when you get to the cliff. Big, somewhat dirty holds quickly get you into the real climbing. It ends at a wiggly pig tail and glue-in bolt anchor Many people lower off the anchor for Mars Needs Women when cleaning. Others just lower off the one wiggly pigtail.
routes.Location: This it the second bolt line left of Blood Beach. Or the 11th from the far left of the cliff. It is just left of "Mars Need's Women".
routes.Protection: 5 bolts and something resembling an anchor.

routes.name: Reanimator
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Steep climbing up a big flake leads to a dirty shelf and more steep climbing out of the cave on pockets. Due to frail holds stickclipping the first bolt isn't a bad idea.
routes.Location: Just left of Bride of the Reanimator. This is the center route in the right cave.

routes.name: Repoman
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Bouldery in spots.
routes.Location: This route is near the center of the wall. 8th from the left. Just right of BSP and left of Hitler's Brain.

routes.name: Subspecies
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Link up of Bride of the Reanimator into the Reanimator. It would be a lot harder if it didn't have a good rest between the two hard parts.
routes.Location: Right hand cave.

routes.name: They Live
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Steep climbing to pockets on the right side of the left cave. Stick clip the second bolt since the first bolt is obviously bad. I haven't been on this route yet so I didn't give it any stars. It is supposed to be quite good. A few holds have broken. Could be tough for the grade.
routes.Location: Right side of the left cave.

routes.name: They Live Above
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: An extension to they live. After reaching the anchor for "They Live" head up and right to the big roof. There is a bolt part way out the roof. Pull the lip and climb to the anchor. Supposedly it can be conditions dependent. I haven't been on this route yet so I didn't give it any stars.
routes.Location: The roof above the anchor of They Live.

routes.name: They Saved Hitler's Brain
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Bouldery with pretty small holds for this wall and a tough 4th clip. The route kinda zigzags at the bottom and then pretty well follows the bolts from the third up. There is a big pocket just below the third bolt. Cool moves.
routes.Location: Near the center of the wall. Just right of Repoman. 9th route from the left overall. 6th from the left cave. 4th from the left of Blood Beach.

routes.name: Unknown
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This is the crack that goes out the center of the left cave. I haven't done it and don't know the name. The rating is just a guess since they make you list one. It looks tough. There is a pin at the start and a couple of fixed wires along the way.
routes.Location: The center of the left cave.
routes.Protection: Gear plus one fixed pin and a couple of fixed nuts. Lower off one of the sport route anchors to the side.

routes.name: White Zombie
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This route starts below a right facing corner and climbs up to a ledgy area and then trends left and up over the roof.
routes.Location: This route is the furthest route to the right on the B-MW

routes.name: Keep Summer Safe
routes.FA:: FA: Peter Hoang, Matthew Sapiecha (Also Equipped) FFA: Andrew Thomas
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Techy first pitch (5.11+?) then only a huge ledge with anchors. Either bring up your 2nd or clip your rope through the first permadraw for the traversing prow (back clean the anchors if you are doing the route in one pitch). 6 or 7 permadraws to the anchors. Super fun movement on some good pockets/huecos and some bad sloping edges. Classic!
routes.Protection: Bolted hangers for first pitch, permadraws on second pitch.

routes.name: The Monument
routes.FA:: Kaandorp, Zabrok, De Maio  F.F.A. Croft
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The route EVERYONE wants to do, but it rarely gets done. This nearly horizontal 30 foot roof is massive and split by a fist/hand crack that pinches to fingers just before the lip.   Climb the chossy looking face until it is possible to start using the crack. The rock is more solid than it looks, but use caution.   Climb the easy, slightly overhanging crack up to the roof then take a deep breath before leaning back and seeing how far away the lip looks. After choking back down the vomit, fight the pump and work your way out to the lip. The crux is gaining the lip. Once established at the lip it is easy climbing to the anchor.   The route seeps most of the year and the black rock can be extremely slippery. Once it drys out, the crack usually needs a light brushing the first time out it.   This route is best cleaned by aiding it on toprope. Some people just leave the roof fixed and pinkpoint. Knock at least a letter grade off if you do this.
routes.Location: At the far end of the cliff. 5-10 min from the B-MW. The second you see it you will know you are at it.
routes.Protection: Cams up to #4 Camalot with doubles of the 3&4 sizes. A double of the #2 size can be useful for cleaning purposes. There are a few fixed wires on the section before the roof that are asked to be left in place. Lower from the anchor.

routes.name: She's So Hexy
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Tough bouldery start (unless you have really thin fingers). Some small placements in the crack, then place a #6 halfway up in the obvious off-width that you can laybacks through. Remember to extend your pieces in the upper corner before traversing out towards the anchors. A medium size hex could protect the traverse... You can stay high or low for the traverse. Definite classic!
routes.Protection: 0.3 to #4. #6 highly suggested down low. Optional medium hex for the traverse finish.
